14569.title: Diabetic Retinopathy Detection
14569.description: Multilabel classification of retinal images into 5 severity levels.
14569.description_zh: 视网膜图像的5个严重程度等级的多标签分类。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
Diabetic retinopathy detection challenge

Classify retinal fundus images into 5 severity levels of diabetic retinopathy using machine learning.

Objective

Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a diabetes complication that damages the blood vessels of the retina. It is a leading cause of blindness worldwide, affecting over 100 million people. Early detection through automated screening of retinal images can drastically improve patient outcomes.

Your goal is to build a model that takes a retinal fundus photograph as input and predicts which severity level of DR it corresponds to.

Severity levels

Label Severity Description
0 No DR Healthy retina, no signs of diabetic retinopathy
1 Mild Microaneurysms only
2 Moderate More than just microaneurysms, but less than severe
3 Severe Extensive intraretinal hemorrhages, venous beading
4 Proliferative DR Neovascularization and/or vitreous hemorrhage

Dataset

The dataset consists of 510 retinal fundus images from the IDRiD (Indian Diabetic Retinopathy Image Dataset), available under the CC BY 4.0 license.

  • Training set: 407 images with labels
  • Public test set (Development Phase): ~50 images
  • Private test set (Final Phase): ~50 images

Labels are provided as one-hot encoded vectors of length 5 (columns 0 to 4). Each image belongs to exactly one severity class.

Evaluation metrics

Submissions are evaluated on the following metrics:

Metric Description Direction
F1 Macro Average F1 score across all classes (treats each class equally) ↑ Higher is better
F1 Micro Global F1 computed from total TP, FP, FN (favors majority classes) ↑ Higher is better
Hamming Loss Fraction of incorrectly predicted labels ↓ Lower is better
Runtime (s) Total execution time (training + inference) ↓ Lower is better

Predictions are thresholded at 0.5 to produce binary labels before computing F1 and Hamming Loss.

How to participate

  1. Download the starting kit notebook (starting_kit.ipynb) from the GitHub repository : here.
  2. Explore the data and understand the evaluation metrics.
  3. Implement your model in a submission.py file following the required interface:
    • A get_model() function that returns a model object
    • The model must have fit(X_train, y_train, train_img_dir) and predict(X_test, test_img_dir) methods
    • predict should return a numpy array of shape (n_samples, 5) with values in [0, 1]
  4. Zip your submission.py and upload it to CodaBench.

Rules

Up to 100 submissions total (10 per day).

References

  • IDRiD Dataset (Roboflow)
  • Scikit-learn F1 Score Documentation
pages.content_zh:
# 糖尿病视网膜病变检测挑战

## 目标

使用机器学习将视网膜眼底图像分类为糖尿病视网膜病变的 **5 个严重程度等级**。

---

## 目标

**糖尿病视网膜病变 (DR)** 是一种糖尿病并发症，会损伤视网膜血管。它是全球导致失明的主要原因，影响超过 **1 亿人**。通过对视网膜图像进行自动筛查进行早期检测可以大幅改善患者预后。

您的目标是建立一个模型，该模型以 **视网膜眼底照片** 作为输入，预测其对应的 **DR 严重程度等级**。

## 严重程度等级

| 标签 | 严重程度 | 描述 |
|:----:|---------|------|
| 0 | 无 DR | 健康视网膜，无糖尿病视网膜病变迹象 |
| 1 | 轻度 | 仅有微动脉瘤 |
| 2 | 中度 | 超过仅微动脉瘤，但不及严重程度 |
| 3 | 严重 | 广泛的视网膜内出血、静脉串珠样改变 |
| 4 | 增殖性 DR | 新生血管形成和/或玻璃体出血 |

## 数据集

数据集包含来自 [IDRiD（印度糖尿病视网膜病变图像数据集）](https://universe.roboflow.com/officeworkspace/diabetic-retinopathy-dataset) 的 **510 张视网膜眼底图像**，遵循 **CC BY 4.0** 许可证。

- **训练集**：407 张标注图像
- **公开测试集**（开发阶段）：约 50 张图像
- **私有测试集**（最终阶段）：约 50 张图像

标签以 **独热编码向量** 形式提供，长度为 5（列 `0` 至 `4`）。每张图像恰好属于一个严重程度类别。

## 评估指标

提交评估的指标如下：

| 指标 | 描述 | 方向 |
|------|------|:----:|
| **F1 宏平均** | 平均 F1 分数 | ↑ |

16306.title: ACM MM 2026 PCBA Standard-to-Real Grand Challenge
16306.description: ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenge on Cross-domain visual question answering for real-world PCBA manufacturing inspection.
16306.description_zh: ACM MM 2026 跨域视觉问答大挑战赛 —— 真实PCBA制造检测应用

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
## Overview

The **PCBA Standard-to-Real Grand Challenge** focuses on **cross-domain Visual Question Answering (VQA) for real-world manufacturing inspection**.

Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA) inspection requires more than visual recognition. Practical factory decision-making depends on both **industrial standards** and **real production-line imagery**. A reliable inspection model must understand manufacturing rules, identify context-dependent defects, reason about possible causes, and provide actionable answers.

This challenge evaluates multimodal models on their ability to generalize from **standards-derived knowledge** to **dense, heterogeneous real-world PCBA images**. Participants are invited to develop VQA systems that can interpret standard documents, recognize components and defects, perform counting tasks, and reason under realistic visual variation.

Participants can register for the challenge on [HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge) by applying for a Dataset and submit their prediction files through the official competition platform.

## Task

Participants are given PCBA-related visual questions and must submit predicted answers for each test sample. The benchmark covers four major VQA families:

1. **Standard-based Knowledge QA**
   Questions derived from industrial standards, covering standard interpretation, defect-cause analysis, and handling decisions.

2. **Factuality**
   Perception-centric questions, including component type recognition, mount-side identification, defect existence detection, and defect type recognition.

3. **Quantitative Reasoning**
   Numeric short-answer questions, including component counting and pin/lead counting.

4. **Attribute Reasoning**
   Questions about visual attributes such as the shape of main components.

## Dataset

The released dataset https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge
is built from two complementary sources:

* **Standards-derived VQA**
  Constructed from industrial packaging standards and converted into VQA items. This subset focuses on standards knowledge, defect-cause analysis, and decision-making.

* **Real-world VQA**
  Built from factory production-line data. This subset focuses on real manufacturing imagery and inspection-oriented reasoning under practical visual variation.

The public split contains:

| Split | Number of Questions |
| ----- | ------------------: |
| Train |               8,200 |
| Test  |               8,200 |
| Total |              16,400 |
pages.content_zh:
## 概述

**PCBA标准到真实场景大挑战**聚焦于**真实制造检验的跨域视觉问答（VQA）**。

印刷电路板组装（PCBA）检验不仅需要视觉识别。实际工厂决策取决于**工业标准**和**真实生产线图像**。可靠的检验模型必须理解制造规则、识别与上下文相关的缺陷、推理可能的原因，并提供可执行的答案。

本挑战评估多模态模型从**标准衍生知识**泛化到**密集、异质真实PCBA图像**的能力。邀请参赛者开发VQA系统，能够解读标准文档、识别组件和缺陷、执行计数任务，并在现实视觉变化下进行推理。

参赛者可在[HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge)上通过申请数据集进行注册，并通过官方竞赛平台提交预测文件。

## 任务

参赛者需回答PCBA相关视觉问题，并为每个测试样本提交预测答案。基准包含四大VQA类别：

1. **基于标准的知识问答**
   源自工业标准的问题，涵盖标准解读、缺陷原因分析和处理决策。

2. **事实性**
   感知为主的问题，包括组件类型识别、安装面识别、缺陷存在检测和缺陷类型识别。

3. **定量推理**
   数字短答题，包括组件计数和管脚/引线计数。

4. **属性推理**
   关于视觉属性的问题，如主要组件的形状。

## 数据集

已发布的数据集 https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge 由两个互补来源构建：

* **标准衍生VQA**
  由工业包装标准构建并转换为VQA项目。该子集聚焦于标准知识、缺陷原因分析和决策制定。

* **真实场景VQA**
  源自工厂生产线数据。该子集聚焦于真实制造图像和实际视觉变化下的检验推理。

公开分割包含：

| 分割 | 问题数量 |
| ---- | -------: |
| 训练 |    8,200 |
| 测试 |    8,200 |
| 合计 |   16,400 |

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
## Evaluation

All submissions are evaluated automatically on the official Codabench server. Participants must submit one prediction file for the released public test questions. The public leaderboard reports a single **Overall Score**.

## Submission Format

For Codabench submission, participants must upload a single `.zip` file. The `.zip` archive must contain one CSV file named `submission.csv` at the root level:

```text
your_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

The `submission.csv` file must contain exactly two columns:

```csv
qid,answer
1,B
2,12
3,E
```

Submission rules:

* `qid` must exactly match the question identifier in `vqa_test_public.json`
* `answer` must contain only the final prediction
* For multiple-choice questions, submit the option key exactly as shown in the question, e.g. `A`, `B`, `C`, `D`, `E`, or `F`
* For counting questions, submit an integer as a plain string, e.g. `12`
* The file must contain one row for every test question
* Missing rows, duplicated `qid` values, or unknown `qid` values will result in an invalid submission
* Do not place `submission.csv` inside an extra subfolder in the submission archive

The public test JSON does not expose task-type annotations. Task grouping is handled internally by the official evaluation server.

## Official Metrics

Each test question belongs internally to one of the following task groups:

* `standard_knowledge`
* `component_type`
* `mount_side`
* `defect_existence`
* `defect_type`
* `count_component`
* `count_pin_lead`
* `attribute_reasoning`

Task-level metrics:

* **Accuracy** for `standard_knowledge`, `component_type`, `mount_side`, `defect_type`, and `attribute_reasoning`
* **F1-score** for `defect_existence`
* **Normalized MAE** for `count_component` and `count_pin_lead`

For `defect_existence`, the official score uses binary F1 on the anomaly-positive class.

For counting tasks, the official normalized score is:

```text
count_score_t = max(0, 1 - MAE_t / C_t)
```

where the fixed normalization constants are:

* `C_count_component = 138`
* `C_count_pin_lead = 260`

## Overall Score

Each task-level metric is first converted to the same `0-1` range. The final public leaderboard score is then computed as the weighted average across task groups, using the task proportions in the evaluation set:

```text
Overall Score = 100 * sum_t (w_t * s_t)
```

where:

* `s_t` is the task-level score for task `t`
* `w_t = n_t / N`
* `n_t` is the number of evaluation questions in task `t`
* `N` is the total number of evaluation questions

The leaderboard displays the final **Overall Score** on a `0-100` scale.

## Evaluation Notes

* The official Codabench server is the only valid source of scores
* Organizer-side task annotations are hidden from participants
* Invalid option labels for multiple-choice questions are counted as incorrect answers
* Non-integer answers for counting questions are treated as invalid counting predictions
* Organizers may additionally run the same scoring protocol on private evaluation data for verification or post-competition analysis
pages.content_zh:
## 评估

所有提交都在官方Codabench服务器上自动评估。参与者必须为发布的公开测试问题提交一个预测文件。公开排行榜报告单一的**总体得分**。

## 提交格式

对于Codabench提交，参与者必须上传单个`.zip`文件。`.zip`压缩包必须在根级别包含一个名为`submission.csv`的CSV文件：

```text
your_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

`submission.csv`文件必须包含恰好两列：

```csv
qid,answer
1,B
2,12
3,E
```

提交规则：

* `qid`必须完全匹配`vqa_test_public.json`中的问题标识符
* `answer`必须仅包含最终预测
* 对于多选题，按照问题中显示的选项键精确提交，例如`A`、`B`、`C`、`D`、`E`或`F`
* 对于计数问题，以纯字符串形式提交整数，例如`12`
* 文件必须为每个测试问题包含一行
* 缺少行、重复的`qid`值或未知的`qid`值将导致提交无效
* 不要在提交压缩包中将`submission.csv`放置在额外的子文件夹内

公开测试JSON不公开任务类型注释。任务分组由官方评估服务器内部处理。

## 官方指标

每个测试问题在内部属于以下任务组之一：

* `standard_knowledge`
* `component_type`
* `mount_side`
* `defect_existence`
* `defect_type`
* `count_component`
* `count_pin_lead`
* `attribute_reasoning`

任务级指标：

* **准确率**用于`standard_knowledge`、`component_type`、`mount_side`、`defect_type`和`attribute_reasoning`
* **F1得分**用于`defect_existence`
* **标准化MAE**用于`count_component`和`count_pin_lead`

对于`defect_existence`，官方得分使用异常阳性类的二元F1。

对于计数任务，官方标准化得分为：

```text
count_score_t = max(0, 1 - MAE_t / C_t)
```

其中固定的标准化常数为：

* `C_count_component = 138`
* `C_count_pin_lead = 260`

## 总体得分

每个任务级指标首先转换为相同的`0-1`范围。最终公开排行榜得分随后计算为跨任务组的加权平均，使用评估集中的任务比例：

```text
Overall Score = 100 * sum_t (w_t * s_t)
```

其中：

* `s_t`是任务`t`的任务级得分
* `w_t = n_t / N`
* `n_t`是任务`t`的评估问题数
* `N`是评估问题总数

排行榜在`0-100`范围内显示最终**总体得分**。

## 评估说明

* 官方Codabench服务器是得分的唯一有效来源
* 组织者方面的任务注释对参与者隐藏
* 多选题的无效选项标签被计为错误答案
* 计数问题的非整数答案被视为无效计数预测
* 组织者可能会在私有评估数据上另外运行相同的评分协议以进行验证或赛后分析

16299.title: ACM MM AffectiveArt 2026 - Track 1 Generation
16299.description_zh: 情感感知艺术图像生成

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Track 1: Emotion-Aware Artistic Image Generation

## Overview

Given a multimodal prompt describing semantic content, artistic movement, and desired emotional state, the goal of Track 1 is to generate an image that fulfills all three conditions.

This track evaluates whether generative models can preserve the requested visual content while aligning with both the specified artistic movement and the target emotional state.

## Problem Statement

Participants are provided with a set of captions.

For each caption, participants must generate one artistic image that simultaneously satisfies the described semantic content, artistic style, and emotional condition.

## Scientific Challenge

Participants must develop models that can disentangle style from emotion. Successful models should learn to subtly manipulate visual attributes to achieve a hybrid state, such as a calm Expressionist painting.

This requires models to reason about both artistic style and emotional expression through visual attributes including composition, brushwork, color, line, and lighting.

## Evaluation Metrics

Track 1 submissions will be evaluated using:

- Frechet Inception Distance (FID), which evaluates image quality and distribution-level similarity to reference artwork images.
- Attribute Alignment Score (AAS), which evaluates prompt-image alignment in terms of content, style, and artistic attributes.

The final Track 1 score combines FID and AAS with equal weights.
pages.content_zh:
# 赛道1：情感感知艺术图像生成

## 概述

给定描述语义内容、艺术风格和期望情感状态的多模态提示词，赛道1的目标是生成满足全部三个条件的图像。

该赛道评估生成模型是否能够保留请求的视觉内容，同时与指定的艺术风格和目标情感状态保持一致。

## 问题陈述

参赛者获得一组图像描述文字。

对于每个描述，参赛者必须生成一个艺术图像，该图像同时满足所描述的语义内容、艺术风格和情感条件。

## 科学挑战

参赛者需要开发能够将风格与情感分离开来的模型。成功的模型应学会巧妙地操纵视觉属性以实现混合状态，例如冷静的表现主义绘画。

这要求模型通过包括构图、笔触、色彩、线条和光线等视觉属性来推理艺术风格和情感表现。

## 评估指标

赛道1提交内容将使用以下指标进行评估：

- 弗雷歇初始距离（FID），用于评估图像质量和与参考艺术作品图像的分布级相似度。
- 属性对齐得分（AAS），用于评估提示词与图像在内容、风格和艺术属性方面的对齐程度。

最终赛道1得分将FID和AAS以相等权重相结合。

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
# Data

## Test Set

The official test set has been distributed to registered participants by email.

Participants should use the emailed test set to prepare their final submissions. Please make sure that every submitted result corresponds to the official `sample_id` values provided in the test set.



## Data Use

The test set is intended only for participation in this challenge. Participants should not redistribute the test set or use it for purposes outside the competition without permission from the organizers.
pages.content_zh:
# 数据

## 测试集

官方测试集已通过电子邮件分发给注册参赛者。

参赛者应使用电子邮件收到的测试集来准备最终提交。请确保每个提交的结果都对应测试集中提供的官方 `sample_id` 值。



## 数据使用

测试集仅用于参加本竞赛。参赛者不应在未获得组织者许可的情况下重新分发测试集或将其用于竞赛外的目的。

pages.title: Submission
pages.content:
# Submission Instructions

Participants must upload a `submission.zip` file following the required Track 1 format.

Do not include an extra parent directory inside the zip file. The required files should appear directly at the top level of `submission.zip`.

Participants must submit a `submission.zip` file containing:

```text
submission.zip
+-- submission.json
`-- images/
    +-- track1_0001.jpg
    +-- track1_0002.jpg
    `-- ...
```

The `submission.json` file must follow the format below:

```json
[
  {
    "sample_id": "track1_0001",
    "path": "images/track1_0001.jpg"
  },
  {
    "sample_id": "track1_0002",
    "path": "images/track1_0002.jpg"
  }
]
```

Each generated image filename must exactly match its corresponding `sample_id`.

## Upload Rules

- Upload only the required `submission.zip` file.
- Do not upload the full project folder or source code unless explicitly requested.
- Ensure that all `sample_id` values match the official test set.
- Ensure that all paths in `submission.json` match the files inside the zip archive.
- Invalid JSON, missing samples, missing files, or mismatched filenames may cause the submission to fail validation.

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# Evaluation

## Track 1: Emotion-Aware Artistic Image Generation

For Track 1, submissions will be evaluated using Frechet Inception Distance (FID) and Attribute Alignment Score (AAS).

## FID

FID measures the distribution-level similarity between the generated image set and the reference artistic image set.

Lower FID indicates better performance.

## AAS

AAS evaluates the prompt-level alignment between each generated image and its corresponding caption. It consists of three aspects:

- Content Alignment: whether the generated image correctly reflects the main visual content described in the prompt.
- Style Alignment: whether the generated image reflects the required artistic style.
- Attribute Alignment: whether the generated image aligns with the artistic attributes specified in the caption, such as composition, brushwork, color, line, or lighting when applicable.

AAS will be assessed using a standardized LLM-assisted multimodal evaluation protocol. Each generated image will be evaluated together with its corresponding prompt under fixed evaluation prompts, predefined scoring rubrics, and consistent inference settings. The final AAS will be computed by aggregating the scores across the three alignment aspects and all test samples.

The final Track 1 score will be computed by combining FID and AAS with equal weights: 50% FID and 50% AAS. Since lower FID indicates better performance, FID will be normalized or converted into a score before being combined with AAS.

## Invalid Content

Any attempt to manipulate the evaluator, such as including instructions directed at the evaluator rather than valid task responses, will be treated as invalid content and may lead to score deduction or disqualification.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估

## 赛道1：情感感知艺术图像生成

赛道1的提交作品将使用Frechet初始距离(FID)和属性对齐分数(AAS)进行评估。

## FID

FID测量生成图像集与参考艺术图像集之间的分布级相似性。

FID越低，性能越好。

## AAS

AAS评估每个生成图像与其对应标题之间的提示级对齐程度。它包括三个方面：

- 内容对齐：生成的图像是否正确反映提示中描述的主要视觉内容。
- 风格对齐：生成的图像是否反映所需的艺术风格。
- 属性对齐：生成的图像是否与标题中指定的艺术属性一致，如构图、笔法、色彩、线条或光线等适用方面。

AAS将使用标准化的大语言模型辅助多模态评估协议进行评估。每个生成的图像将与其对应的提示一起在固定的评估提示、预定义的评分标准和一致的推理设置下进行评估。最终的AAS将通过聚合三个对齐方面和所有测试样本的分数来计算。

赛道1的最终分数将通过以相等权重结合FID和AAS来计算：50% FID和50% AAS。由于较低的FID表示更好的性能，FID在与AAS结合之前将被归一化或转换为分数。

## 无效内容

任何试图操纵评估器的行为，如在评估器中包含针对评估器的指令而非有效任务响应，将被视为无效内容，可能导致分数扣除或取消资格。

pages.title: Rules
pages.content:
# Rules

Participants may use their own models and methods to complete the challenge tasks, provided that all submissions follow the official submission format and are based on the official test set distributed by the organizers.

## General Rules

- Each submitted result must correspond to a valid official `sample_id`.
- Submissions must follow the required JSON schema for the selected track.
- Participants are responsible for ensuring that submitted files are complete, readable, and correctly packaged.
- The test set distributed by the organizers must not be redistributed without permission.
- Participants should not submit content that is unrelated to the task or intended to interfere with the evaluation process.

## Invalid Submissions

A submission may be rejected, penalized, or disqualified if it:

- contains missing, duplicated, or invalid `sample_id` values,
- contains unreadable files or invalid JSON,
- does not follow the required zip structure,
- includes instructions directed at the evaluator instead of valid task responses,
- attempts to manipulate the scoring system or evaluation protocol.
pages.content_zh:
# 规则

参赛者可以使用自己的模型和方法完成挑战任务，前提是所有提交内容遵循官方提交格式，并基于组织者分配的官方测试集。

## 通用规则

- 每个提交的结果必须对应一个有效的官方 `sample_id`。
- 提交内容必须遵循所选赛道要求的 JSON 模式。
- 参赛者负责确保提交的文件完整、可读且打包正确。
- 组织者分配的测试集不得未经许可进行重新分发。
- 参赛者不应提交与任务无关的内容或试图干扰评估过程的内容。

## 无效提交

在以下情况下，提交可能被拒绝、受罚或被取消参赛资格：

- 包含缺失、重复或无效的 `sample_id` 值，
- 包含不可读的文件或无效的 JSON，
- 不遵循所需的 zip 文件结构，
- 包含针对评估员的指令而非有效的任务回复，
- 试图操纵评分系统或评估协议。

15769.title: The Challenge on General AIGC Audio–Video Detection
15769.description: AIGC Audio-Video Deepfake Classification Challenge using the MVAD dataset.
15769.description_zh: AIGC音视频深度伪造分类挑战赛（基于MVAD数据集）

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# General AIGC Audio-Video Detection

This competition focuses on **General AIGC Audio-Video Detection**. To counter the growing threat of advanced AI-generated video forgery (e.g., Sora2, Veo3.1), we propose a large-scale multimodal detection challenge based on the **MVAD dataset** (released as DDL‑GAV), which contains more than **200,000 real‑forgery audio‑video samples**. It covers both realistic and anime visual styles, four content categories (humans, animals, objects, and scenes), three typical cross‑modal forgery patterns, and more than **23 AI generation techniques**, including text‑to‑video, image‑to‑video, video‑to‑video, and audio‑to‑video synthesis. The competition aims to promote the research and application of multimodal deepfake detection and support the sustainable development of **AI for Good**.

Competition link: https://www.codabench.org/competitions/15769/

If any participants have any questions, please send an email to: 17318508682@163.com

#### Note: The Test Set has been officially released on the GitHub repository as of May 15, 2026.

---

## Evaluation Metrics
This challenge includes **two hierarchical detection tasks**: binary forgery detection and four‑class modality classification.
### Task Definitions
- Real‑Real (RR): Both audio and video are authentic.
- Fake‑Real (FR): Video is forged, audio is real.
- Real‑Fake (RF): Video is real, audio is forged.
- Fake‑Fake (FF): Both audio and video are forged.

### Evaluation Metrics
Detection performance is evaluated using **AUC, ACC, F1, and AP**.

### Final Score Calculation
- Binary Classification Score = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- Four‑Class Classification Score = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- **Overall Final Score = 0.4 × Binary Classification Score + 0.6 × Four‑Class Classification Score**

## Submission Format

Participants must submit **two prediction files** packaged together in **a single `.zip` archive**.

### Packaging Rules

- The zip archive can have **any name** (`submission.zip`, `result.zip`, etc.).
- It must contain exactly two files, named **precisely** `binary.txt` and `four_class.txt` (case-sensitive — do not rename).
- The two files may sit at the zip root or inside a subfolder — both are accepted.
- Submit prediction results only — **do not** submit model code.

### 1. `binary.txt` — Binary Classification (Real / Fake)

Each line: `video_path  0_pred  1_pred`

- `0_pred` = probability of **Real**
- `1_pred` = probability of **Fake**
- An optional header line `video_path,0_pred,1_pred` is allowed and will be skipped.

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.5066 0.4934
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.5100 0.4900
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.6434 0.3566
```

### 2. `four_class.txt` — Four-Class Classification

Each line: `video_path  0_pred  1_pred  2_pred  3_pred`

- Class indices: `0 = Real-Real (RR)`, `1 = Fake-Fake (FF)`, `2 = Fake-Real (FR)`, `3 = Real-Fake (RF)`
- An optional header line is allowed and will be skipped.

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred,2_pred,3_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.1292 0.0075 0.3836 0.4796
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.2880 0.0590 0.2999 0.3531
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.3144 0.0230 0.3682 0.2944
```

### Important Notes

- **`video_path` must exactly match the test-set filenames** (the hashed `.mp4` names in the released test data). Mismatched names are treated as missing and scored with a default uniform probability.
- Within a line, use **either spaces or commas** as the separator consistently — do **not** mix `comma + space`.
- Probabilities need not sum exactly to 1 — the scorer normalizes each row automatically.
- **Every test-set sample should appear in both files.** Missing rows are filled with default probabilities and will lower your score.

## Awards and Recognition
- Outstanding solutions will be invited for presentation at IJCAI.
- **Top 1–5 teams** will receive official Ranking Certificates.
- **Top 6–10 teams** will receive Excellence Awards.
- Submitted papers for **Track 2** will have the opportunity to be selected for the Best Paper Award.

## Dataset
This competition is built on the **MVAD dataset** (released as DDL‑GAV), the first large‑scale benchmark for multimodal AI‑generated audio‑video detection. It contains more than **205,000 video‑audio samples**, covering two visual domains (realistic and anime), four content categories (humans, animals, objects, scenes), three cross‑modal forgery patterns, and over **23 AI generation methods**. The dataset provides comprehensive and diverse support for advancing research in generalizable AIGC audio‑video detection.

GitHub repository: https://github.com/HuMengXue0104/MVAD

## Competition Rules
1. Model Submission Requirements
Each track permits only one model submission to address the designated tasks.
All pre-trained backbone models used must be open-source. Teams that develop proprietary backbone models during the competition are required to publicly release their model specifications and training protocols under an open-source license (e.g., MIT, Apache 2.0) during the competition period.
Winning solutions must open-source their complete implementation, including:
    - Training pipelines and hyperparameter configurations
    - Evaluation code and reproducibility documentation
    - Final model weights in standard formats
2. Dataset Usage Requirements
Only organizer-recommended, publicly released datasets are permitted for training and evaluation.
Participants may use extended samples generated from these recommended datasets via data augmentation or deepfake tools for training, but all generation tools used must be submitted to the organizers to ensure reproducibility.
The use of any additional external datasets is strictly prohibited.

3. Violations and Sanctions
Violations of the above rules will result in disqualification. The organizing committee reserves the final authority over all competition-related matters.
pages.content_zh:
# 通用AIGC音视频检测

本竞赛聚焦于**通用AIGC音视频检测**。为应对高级AI生成视频伪造（如Sora2、Veo3.1）日益增长的威胁，我们基于**MVAD数据集**（发布为DDL-GAV）提出了一项大规模多模态检测挑战赛，包含超过**200,000个真实-伪造音视频样本**。数据集覆盖逼真和动画两种视觉风格、四个内容类别（人类、动物、物体和场景）、三种典型跨模态伪造模式，以及超过**23种AI生成技术**，包括文本生成视频、图像生成视频、视频生成视频和音频生成视频合成。本竞赛旨在推进多模态深度伪造检测的研究与应用，支持**AI向善**的可持续发展。

竞赛链接：https://www.codabench.org/competitions/15769/

如有任何疑问，请发送邮件至：17318508682@163.com

#### 注：测试集已于2026年5月15日在GitHub库中正式发布。

---

## 评估指标
本挑战赛包含**两个分层检测任务**：二元伪造检测和四类模态分类。
### 任务定义
- 真-真（RR）：音频和视频均真实。
- 伪-真（FR）：视频伪造，音频真实。
- 真-伪（RF）：视频真实，音频伪造。
- 伪-伪（FF）：音频和视频均伪造。

### 评估指标
检测性能使用**AUC、ACC、F1和AP**进行评估。

### 最终评分计算
- 二元分类得分 = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- 四类分类得分 = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- **最终总得分 = 0.4×二元分类得分 + 0.6×四类分类得分**

## 提交格式

参赛者必须提交**两个预测文件**，打包在**单个`.zip`压缩包**中。

### 打包规则

- zip压缩包可以使用**任意名称**（`submission.zip`、`result.zip`等）。
- 必须恰好包含两个文件，名称**必须**为`binary.txt`和`four_class.txt`（区分大小写——不要重命名）。
- 两个文件可以位于zip根目录或子文件夹中——两种方式都可接受。
- 仅提交预测结果——**不要**提交模型代码。

### 1. `binary.txt` — 二元分类（真实/伪造）

每行格式：`video_path  0_pred  1_pred`

- `0_pred` = **真实**的概率
- `1_pred` = **伪造**的概率
- 可选的标题行`video_path,0_pred,1_pred`被允许且会被跳过。

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.5066 0.4934
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.5100 0.4900
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.6434 0.3566
```

### 2. `four_class.txt` — 四类分类

每行格式：`video_path  0_pred  1_pred  2_pred  3_pred`

- 类别索引：`0 = 真-真(RR)`、`1 = 伪-伪(FF)`、`2 = 伪-真(FR)`、`3 = 真-伪(RF)`
- 可选的标题行被允许且会被跳过。

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred,2_pred,3_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.1292 0.0075 0.3836 0.4796
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.2880 0.0590 0.2999 0.3531
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.3144 0.0230 0.3682 0.2944
```

### 重要说明

- **`video_path`必须与测试集文件名完全匹配**（发布测试数据中的哈希值`.mp4`文件名）。不匹配的文件名被视为缺失，评分时使用默认均匀概率。
- 每行内，使用**空格或逗号**作为分隔符保持一致——**不要**混合使用`逗号+空格`。
- 概率无需恰好求和为1——评分器会自动对每行进行归一化。
- **测试集中的每个样本应同时出现在两个文件中。**缺失的行将用默认概率填充，会降低您的分数。

## 奖励与认可
- 优秀方案将被邀请在IJCAI进行展示。
- **前1-5名团队**将获得官方排名证书。

16461.title: Explainable Deepfake Detection Challenge @ ACM Multimedia 2026
16461.description: Hidden-test evaluation for the ACM MM 2026 Explainable Deepfake Detection Challenge on image-level deepfake detection and grounded explanation generation.
16461.description_zh: ACM MM 2026 可解释深伪检测挑战赛隐测集评估：图像级深伪检测和基础解释生成

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Overview

Welcome to the **Explainable Deepfake Detection Challenge 2026**, an **ACM Multimedia 2026 Grand Challenge** on image-level deepfake detection and grounded explanation generation.

Deepfake detection is increasingly moving beyond simple face swaps toward richer semantic manipulations. In practical settings, a prediction alone is not enough; systems should also indicate the visual evidence that supports a decision.

This challenge introduces **XPlainVerse**, a large-scale benchmark for evaluating both detection and explanation. Participants are asked not only to determine whether an image is real or fake, but also to provide two grounded natural-language explanations: a detailed complex explanation and a non-technical simple explanation.

## Tasks

The challenge has two main tasks:

1. **Deepfake Detection**: determine whether an input image is real or fake.
2. **Deepfake Explanation**: generate explanations that support the authenticity decision using visible evidence from the image.

For the explanation task, each submission must include:

- a **complex explanation**: detailed, evidence-rich, and specific, intended for technical or forensic review;
- a **simple explanation**: shorter, clearer, and more accessible, while preserving the key reason behind the decision.

Both explanations should remain consistent with the submitted authenticity label.

## CodaBench phase

This CodaBench platform is used for the official result-submission phase.

- **Phase opens:** 28 May 2026
- **Result submission deadline:** 15 June 2026
- **Submissions per day:** 1
- **Total submissions:** 10

General challenge information, announcements, registration links, and organizer updates are maintained on the official challenge website:

https://explainable-deepfake-detection.github.io/
pages.content_zh:
# 概述

欢迎参加**可解释深度伪造检测挑战2026**，这是**ACM Multimedia 2026大赛**中关于图像级深度伪造检测和接地式解释生成的竞赛。

深度伪造检测正在超越简单的人脸交换，转向更丰富的语义操纵。在实际应用中，仅有预测结果是不够的；系统还应该指出支持该决策的视觉证据。

本挑战引入**XPlainVerse**，这是一个大规模基准数据集，用于评估检测和解释的质量。参赛者不仅需要判断图像是否为真实或伪造，还需要提供两个接地式自然语言解释：一个详细的复杂解释和一个非技术性的简单解释。

## 竞赛任务

本挑战包含两个主要任务：

1. **深度伪造检测**：判断输入图像是真实还是伪造。
2. **深度伪造解释**：使用图像中的可见证据生成支持真伪判断的解释。

对于解释任务，每份提交必须包括：

- **复杂解释**：详细、证据充分且具体，旨在供技术或法医审查；
- **简单解释**：更短、更清晰、更易理解，同时保持决策的关键原因。

两个解释都应与提交的真伪标签保持一致。

## CodaBench阶段

本CodaBench平台用于官方结果提交阶段。

- **阶段开启**：2026年5月28日
- **结果提交截止**：2026年6月15日
- **每天提交次数**：1次
- **总提交次数**：10次

一般性挑战信息、公告、注册链接和组织方更新发布在官方挑战网站上：

https://explainable-deepfake-detection.github.io/

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
# Data

The challenge uses **XPlainVerse**, the official benchmark for both detection and explanation tasks.

The public release provides training and validation data with paired explanation annotations and manifest metadata, plus test images for final evaluation. The XPlainVerse release for this challenge contains **760,000 images**, with **450,000 training images**, **110,000 validation images** and **200,000 test images**.
The test set is released for final evaluation. Test labels and reference explanations are not included in the participant release and are used only by the CodaBench evaluation system.

## Dataset structure

The released package is organized as:

```text
XPlainVerse/
├── train/
│   ├── manifest.jsonl
│   ├── images/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   ├── complex_explanations/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   └── simple_explanations/
│       └── fake/
├── val/
│   ├── manifest.jsonl
│   ├── images/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   ├── complex_explanations/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   └── simple_explanations/
│       └── fake/
└── test/
    └── images/
```

The training and validation splits include an `images` directory, paired `complex_explanations`, paired `simple_explanations`, and a `manifest.jsonl` file. Complex explanations are provided for fake and real images, while simple explanations are provided for fake images. The test split includes released images only; labels and reference explanations remain hidden for evaluation.

## Manifest fields

Each line in `manifest.jsonl` of the training and validation splits stores the label and relative paths needed to load an image together with its paired explanation file.

For the training and validation splits, important fields include:

- `label`: binary image label, fake or real;
- `image_path`: relative path to the image file;
- `complex_explanation_path`: relative path to the paired complex explanation JSON;
- `simple_explanation_path`: relative path to the paired simple explanation JSON.

## Hidden test set

The CodaBench final phase uses the released test images with hidden ground-truth labels and reference explanations. These references are not included in the participant release and are used only by the CodaBench worker.

For each test image, the `id` field in the submission file must exactly match the image filename in the test set, including the file extension, for example `example.png`.
pages.content_zh:
# 数据

该挑战使用**XPlainVerse**，这是检测和解释任务的官方基准。

公开发布提供了带有配对解释注释和清单元数据的训练和验证数据，以及用于最终评估的测试图像。此挑战的XPlainVerse发布包含**760,000张图像**，其中**450,000张训练图像**、**110,000张验证图像**和**200,000张测试图像**。
测试集为最终评估而发布。测试标签和参考解释不包含在参赛者发布版本中，仅由CodaBench评估系统使用。

## 数据集结构

发布的数据包组织如下：

```text
XPlainVerse/
├── train/
│   ├── manifest.jsonl
│   ├── images/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   ├── complex_explanations/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   └── simple_explanations/
│       └── fake/
├── val/
│   ├── manifest.jsonl
│   ├── images/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   ├── complex_explanations/
│   │   ├── fake/
│   │   └── real/
│   └── simple_explanations/
│       └── fake/
└── test/
    └── images/
```

训练和验证分割包括一个`images`目录、配对的`complex_explanations`、配对的`simple_explanations`和一个`manifest.jsonl`文件。为真实和虚假图像提供复杂解释，仅为虚假图像提供简单解释。测试分割仅包括发布的图像；标签和参考解释对评估保持隐藏。

## 清单字段

训练和验证分割的`manifest.jsonl`中的每一行存储了加载图像及其配对解释文件所需的标签和相对路径。

对于训练和验证分割，重要字段包括：

- `label`：二进制图像标签，虚假或真实；
- `image_path`：图像文件的相对路径；
- `complex_explanation_path`：配对复杂解释JSON的相对路径；
- `simple_explanation_path`：配对简单解释JSON的相对路径。

## 隐藏测试集

CodaBench最终阶段使用发布的测试图像与隐藏的真实标签和参考解释。这些参考不包含在参赛者发布版本中，仅由CodaBench工作人员使用。

对于每个测试图像，提交文件中的`id`字段必须完全匹配测试集中的图像文件名（包括文件扩展名），例如`example.png`。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# Evaluation

The leaderboard reports detection performance and explanation quality. Higher scores are better for all displayed metrics.

## Detection

Detection is evaluated on the hidden final test set using binary image labels:

- `0`: real/authentic image;
- `1`: fake/generated or manipulated image.

The leaderboard reports:

- **Detection Macro F1**: primary detection score;
- **Detection Accuracy**;

Detection is evaluated on the full official test set.

## Complex explanations

The complex explanation track evaluates whether the submitted explanation captures the same important visual evidence as the reference explanation.

In this CodaBench scoring bundle, complex explanations are evaluated using **BERTScore F1** between the submitted complex explanation and the hidden reference complex explanation.

The BERTScore model is:

```text
microsoft/deberta-xlarge-mnli
```

Baseline rescaling is disabled.

For leaderboard scoring, complex explanation BERTScore is computed on a 10,000-sample subset of the test set. After the test phase closes, we will compute the remaining complex explanation metrics: **Complex Entity F1** and **Complex Evidence F1** for the **top 5 teams** for final reporting and analysis. The complete complex explanation score is:

```text
complex_overall_score =
  0.3 * complex_bert_f1 +
  0.4 * complex_entity_f1 +
  0.3 * complex_evidence_f1
```

## Simple explanations

The simple explanation track is designed to reward two things at the same time: faithfulness and simplicity. A good simple explanation should preserve the core reason from the reference while expressing it in a shorter, easier form.

In this CodaBench scoring bundle, simple explanations are evaluated using:

- **Simple BERTScore F1** against the hidden simple reference;
- **SLE score** using the offline SLE checkpoint on the worker;
- **Simple SLE Norm**, obtained by clipping raw SLE to `[-1, 4]` and mapping it to `[0, 1]`;
- **Simple Overall Score**:

```text
0.7 * simple_bert_f1 + 0.3 * simple_sle_norm
```

For leaderboard scoring, simple explanation metrics are computed on the same 10,000-sample subset of the test set.

## Overall explanation score

The displayed explanation score combines the complex and simple tracks on the same 10,000-sample explanation-evaluation subset:

```text
explanation_score =
  (complex_bert_f1 + simple_overall_score) / 2
```

## Failed or incomplete submissions

Missing files receive zero for the corresponding metric group. If a valid submission file is present but omits some required rows, those missing samples are scored as zero for the relevant metric. Malformed JSON, duplicate IDs, invalid labels, empty explanations, or unknown IDs may cause scoring to fail.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估

排行榜报告检测性能和解释质量。所有显示的指标中，分数越高越好。

## 检测

检测在隐藏的最终测试集上使用二元图像标签进行评估：

- `0`：真实/原真图像；
- `1`：假图像/生成或被篡改的图像。

排行榜报告：

- **检测宏观F1分数**：主要检测分数；
- **检测准确度**；

检测在完整的官方测试集上进行评估。

## 复杂解释

复杂解释追踪评估提交的解释是否捕捉了与参考解释相同的重要视觉证据。

在此CodaBench评分包中，复杂解释使用提交的复杂解释和隐藏参考复杂解释之间的**BERTScore F1**进行评估。

BERTScore模型为：

```text
microsoft/deberta-xlarge-mnli
```

基线重缩放已禁用。

对于排行榜评分，复杂解释BERTScore在测试集的10,000样本子集上计算。测试阶段关闭后，我们将为**前5个队伍**计算剩余的复杂解释指标：**复杂实体F1**和**复杂证据F1**，用于最终报告和分析。完整的复杂解释分数为：

```text
complex_overall_score =
  0.3 * complex_bert_f1 +
  0.4 * complex_entity_f1 +
  0.3 * complex_evidence_f1
```

## 简单解释

简单解释追踪旨在同时奖励两个方面：忠实性和简洁性。好的简单解释应该保留参考中的核心原因，同时以更短、更易理解的形式表达。

在此CodaBench评分包中，简单解释使用以下方法进行评估：

- **简单BERTScore F1**与隐藏的简单参考对比；
- **SLE分数**使用工作人员的离线SLE检查点；
- **简单SLE标准化**，通过将原始SLE裁剪为`[-1, 4]`并将其映射到`[0, 1]`得到；
- **简单整体分数**：

```text
0.7 * simple_bert_f1 + 0.3 * simple_sle_norm
```

对于排行榜评分，简单解释指标在相同的10,000样本解释评估子集上计算。

## 总体解释分数

显示的解释分数将复杂和简单两个追踪在相同的10,000样本解释评估子集上相结合：

```text
explanation_score =
  (complex_bert_f1 + simple_overall_score) / 2
```

## 失败或不完整的提交

缺失的文件在相应的指标组中得零分。如果提交文件有效但遗漏某些必需行，这些缺失样本在相关指标中被评分为零。格式错误的JSON、重复的ID、无效的标签、空解释或未知ID可能导致评分失败。

pages.title: Submission
pages.content:
# Submission

Submit a single `.zip` file containing model outputs for the official test samples.

```text
submission.zip
├── detection.jsonl
├── complex.jsonl
└── simple.jsonl
```

Each line must be one valid JSON object. **The `id` value must exactly match the released test image filename including extension**.

## Detection file

`detection.jsonl` should contain one row for every final-test image:

```json
{"id": "sample1.png", "pred_label": 1}
```

`pred_label` must be:

- `0` for real;
- `1` for fake.

## Complex explanation file

`complex.jsonl` should contain complex explanations for the explanation-evaluation subset:

```json
{"id": "sample1.png", "complex_explanation": "A detailed explanation grounded in visible image evidence."}
```

A complex explanation should be a detailed explanation grounded in visible image evidence, such as artifacts, geometry issues, texture inconsistencies, text errors, lighting mismatches, or object interactions.

## Simple explanation file

`simple.jsonl` should contain simple explanations for the same explanation-evaluation subset:

```json
{"id": "sample1.png", "simple_explanation": "A simple explanation for the image."}
```

A simple explanation should be a shorter and more accessible explanation that preserves the key reason behind the decision without carrying every low-level detail from the complex explanation.

## Submission limits

Each team may make at most:

- **1 submissions per day**;
- **10 submissions in total** during the final phase.

## Leaderboard entry

Each team can have only one public leaderboard entry. Teams may choose which of their submitted runs to display on the leaderboard using the submission controls in CodaBench.

## Validate before uploading

The starting kit includes a format checker. Run it locally before submitting:

```bash
python validate_submission.py submission.zip
```

The validator checks public format rules only.
pages.content_zh:
# 提交要求

提交单个 `.zip` 文件，包含官方测试样本的模型输出。

```text
submission.zip
├── detection.jsonl
├── complex.jsonl
└── simple.jsonl
```

每一行必须是一个有效的 JSON 对象。**`id` 值必须完全匹配发布的测试图像文件名，包括扩展名**。

## 检测文件

`detection.jsonl` 应包含每个最终测试图像的一行：

```json
{"id": "sample1.png", "pred_label": 1}
```

`pred_label` 必须为：

- `0` 表示真实；
- `1` 表示伪造。

## 复杂解释文件

`complex.jsonl` 应包含解释评估子集的复杂解释：

```json
{"id": "sample1.png", "complex_explanation": "基于可见图像证据的详细解释。"}
```

复杂解释应该是基于可见图像证据的详细解释，例如伪影、几何问题、纹理不一致、文本错误、光线不匹配或物体交互。

## 简单解释文件

`simple.jsonl` 应包含同一解释评估子集的简单解释：

```json
{"id": "sample1.png", "simple_explanation": "针对该图像的简单解释。"}
```

简单解释应该是更短且更易理解的解释，保留决策的关键原因，但不包含复杂解释中的每个低级细节。

## 提交限制

每个团队最多可以进行：

- **每天 1 次提交**；
- **最终阶段总共 10 次提交**。

## 排行榜条目

每个团队在排行榜上只能有一个公开条目。团队可以使用 CodaBench 中的提交控件选择要在排行榜上显示的提交运行。

## 上传前验证

起始工具包包含格式检查器。在提交前在本地运行：

```bash
python validate_submission.py submission.zip
```

验证器仅检查公开格式规则。

pages.title: Announcement
pages.content:
---
#### Notice, May 27, 2026

Dear participants,

As our benchmark has been publicly released for an extended period of time, We recognize that the current challenge description may not have been sufficiently clear on evaluation protocol. In particular, different levels of the same problem may contain overlapping information, which may potentially affect the intended comparison across evaluation settings.

To ensure fair and comparable evaluation across different levels, we require all participants to evaluate each level independently using **only the information provided within the current level.** Participants should **avoid** using information, predictions, captions, or annotations from other levels of the same problem during inference.

In addition, to maintain the integrity and transparency of the challenge, all top-3 winning teams will be required to submit:

- Source code
- Inference/evaluation protocol
- A technical report describing their method and evaluation setup (should **explicitly disclose whether cross-level information was used**).

The organizing committee reserves the right to audit submitted materials. These requirements are intended to ensure that the benchmark more accurately reflects multimodal reasoning ability under different information settings, rather than improvements that may primarily arise from transferring textual information across levels. We appreciate the community’s understanding and cooperation in helping us maintain a fair and meaningful evaluation setting.

Good luck!
Challenge Chairs

---
#### Notice, May 11, 2026

Dear participants,

Going forward, the following rules apply:

1. **Reproducibility is now required** for all submissions. You need to provide code or a clear description of your method. For the top 3 finalists, reproducibility is a condition for receiving your ranking and prize money.

2. Manual annotation of test samples is not a valid approach and will lead to disqualification.

3. We also **encourage everyone to write a short technical report** on your methodology and [submit it to the AI4Math Workshop](https://ai4math2026.github.io/#cfp) by May 24, 2026 AoE. Please start your paper title with "Technical Report for AI4Math-2026 Track n" followed by your own subtitle. It gives you more visibility and a chance to present your work in person at ICML.

Good luck!
Challenge Chairs

---
pages.content_zh:
---
#### 通知，2026年5月27日

亲爱的参赛者们，

鉴于我们的基准已经公开发布了相当长的一段时间，我们意识到当前的挑战描述在评估协议方面可能不够清晰。特别是，同一问题的不同级别可能包含重叠信息，这可能会影响不同评估设置之间的预期比较。

为确保不同级别之间的评估公平且可比，我们要求所有参与者**仅使用当前级别内提供的信息**独立评估每个级别。参与者应**避免**在推理过程中使用来自同一问题其他级别的信息、预测、标题或注释。

此外，为了维护挑战赛的完整性和透明度，所有前3名获奖团队将被要求提交：

- 源代码
- 推理/评估协议
- 技术报告，描述其方法和评估设置（应**明确说明是否使用了跨级别信息**）

组织委员会保留审计提交材料的权利。这些要求旨在确保基准更准确地反映不同信息设置下的多模态推理能力，而不是主要源自跨级别文本信息转移的改进。我们感谢社区的理解与配合，帮助我们维持公平有意义的评估环境。

祝你们好运！
挑战赛主席

---
#### 通知，2026年5月11日

亲爱的参赛者们，

从即日起，以下规则适用：

1. **所有提交都现在需要可重现性**。你需要提供代码或清晰的方法描述。对于前3名入选者，可重现性是获得排名和奖金的条件。

2. 手动标注测试样本不是有效方法，将导致取消资格。

3. 我们还**鼓励大家撰写关于你们方法论的简短技术报告**，并[在2026年5月24日AoE前提交至AI4Math研讨会](https://ai4math2026.github.io/#cfp)。请以"Technical Report for AI4Math-2026 Track n"开头论文标题，后跟你自己的副标题。这将为你们的工作增加可见度，并获得在ICML大会上亲身展示工作的机会。

祝你们好运！
挑战赛主席

---

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Track 3: Seephys Pro - Visual Physics Reasoning

A Multimodal Physics Reasoning Challenge with Diagrams, Expressions, and Explanations

Seephys Pro evaluates whether AI systems can solve physics problems that require visual perception, mathematical reasoning, and faithful explanation. The benchmark combines structured physics problems, diagram-grounded questions, and a curated image-only extension. To rigorously evaluate the systems' generalization capabilities across unseen environments, the benchmark incorporates a specialized Out-of-Distribution (OOD) test set captured via photography, which is represented by the Level 5 dataset. The Level 5 dataset is provided by Transsion, a leading smart device and mobile internet company focused on emerging markets. It consists of anonymized service data from Transsion’s AI photo-based problem-solving service. This level evaluates models in realistic education scenarios, including diverse question formats and natural image quality variations. Participants submit both final answers and reasoning; submissions are scored by an OpenAI-based judge against held reference answers.

Physics reasoning is a core scientific capability. It requires a system to identify quantities, interpret diagrams, connect visual evidence with equations, and produce a defensible conclusion. Recent multimodal models have made rapid progress, but physics questions with diagrams and formal expressions still expose a difficult gap between seeing, reading, and reasoning. The central question of Seephys Pro is: > **How can we build AI systems that reliably integrate visual information, physical laws, and mathematical derivations to solve real physics problems?** This challenge is designed as a compact but demanding public benchmark for that question. ## Challenge at a Glance | Item | Details | |---|---:| | Challenge name | Seephys Pro | | Public leaderboard questions | 830 | | Private leaderboard questions | 3320 | | Required submission fields | `question_id`, `prediction`, `reasoning` | | Scoring | OpenAI API semantic judge | | Overall score | Question-count weighted accuracy | ## Dataset The datasets are hosted on Hugging Face: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro ## What to Submit Participants must submit a `.zip` file containing both: - `prediction.csv` - `prediction_private.csv` Each file must include `question_id`, `prediction`, and `reasoning`. `prediction.csv` is used for the Codabench public leaderboard. `prediction_private.csv` is used for the offline private leaderboard after the deadline. Submissions without `prediction_private.csv` will not be eligible for prizes. ## Notes The public leaderboard is intended for fast iteration. Private evaluation is not automatically run by this Codabench bundle. ## Citation 
@article{xiang2026seephyspro,
  title={SeePhys Pro: Diagnosing Modality Transfer and Blind-Training Effects in Multimodal RLVR for Physics Reasoning},
  author={Xiang, Kun and Zhang, Terry Jingchen and Liu, Zirong and Zhou, Bokai and Tang, Yueling and Yu, Junjie and Lu, Jiacong and Huang, Shangrui and Li, Heng and Zhang, Likui and Liu, Kunkun and Zhang, Changzheng and Fang, Yangle and Guo, Boqiang and Zhen, Hui-Ling and Tu, Dandan and Huang, Yinya and Liang, Xiaodan},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.09266},
  year={2026}
}
pages.content_zh:
# 赛道 3：Seephys Pro - 视觉物理推理

涵盖图表、表达式和解释的多模态物理推理挑战

Seephys Pro 评估 AI 系统是否能够解决需要视觉感知、数学推理和忠实解释的物理问题。该基准结合了结构化物理问题、图表接地的问题和精选的仅图像扩展。为了严格评估系统在未见环境中的泛化能力，该基准纳入了通过摄影捕获的专门分布外（OOD）测试集，由第 5 级数据集表示。第 5 级数据集由传音（Transsion）提供，这是一家专注于新兴市场的领先智能设备和移动互联网公司。它由传音基于 AI 照片的问题解决服务中的匿名服务数据组成。该级别在现实教育场景中评估模型，包括多种问题格式和自然图像质量变化。参与者提交最终答案和推理过程；提交内容由基于 OpenAI 的判断器与参考答案进行对比评分。

物理推理是一项核心科学能力。它要求系统识别物理量、解读图表、将视觉证据与方程相关联，并得出可防守的结论。最近的多模态模型取得了快速进展，但含有图表和形式表达式的物理问题仍然暴露了"看、读、推理"之间的困难鸿沟。 Seephys Pro 的核心问题是： > **我们如何构建能够可靠整合视觉信息、物理定律和数学推导以解决真实物理问题的 AI 系统？** 该挑战被设计为针对该问题的简洁但高要求的公开基准。 ## 挑战概览 | 项目 | 详情 | |---|---:| | 挑战名称 | Seephys Pro | | 公开排行榜题目数 | 830 | | 私有排行榜题目数 | 3320 | | 必需提交字段 | `question_id`、`prediction`、`reasoning` | | 评分方式 | OpenAI API 语义判断器 | | 总体评分 | 题目数加权准确率 | ## 数据集 数据集托管在 Hugging Face： https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro ## 提交内容 参与者必须提交包含以下两项的 `.zip` 文件： - `prediction.csv` - `prediction_private.csv` 每个文件必须包含 `question_id`、`prediction` 和 `reasoning`。 `prediction.csv` 用于 Codabench 公开排行榜。`prediction_private.csv` 用于截止日期后的离线私有排行榜。没有 `prediction_private.csv` 的提交不符合获奖资格。 ## 说明 公开排行榜旨在实现快速迭代。私有评估不由此 Codabench 包自动运行。 ## 引用 
@article{xiang2026seephyspro,
  title={SeePhys Pro: Diagnosing Modality Transfer and Blind-Training Effects in Multimodal RLVR for Physics Reasoning},
  author={Xiang, Kun and Zhang, Terry Jingchen and Liu, Zirong and Zhou, Bokai and Tang, Yueling and Yu, Junjie and Lu, Jiacong and Huang, Shangrui and Li, Heng and Zhang, Likui and Liu, Kunkun and Zhang, Changzheng and Fang, Yangle and Guo, Boqiang and Zhen, Hui-Ling and Tu, Dandan and Huang, Yinya and Liang, Xiaodan},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.09266},
  year={2026}
}

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
# Seephys Pro Data
Benchmark Data:
https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro

Project Page:
https://seephyspro.github.io/

Paper:
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.09266
## Public Leaderboard Splits Use the following splits for public leaderboard submissions: | Split | Questions | Public leaderboard | |---|---|---| | `level1_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level2_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level3_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level4_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level5_testmini` | 30 | Yes | Total public leaderboard size: **830 questions**. ## Private Leaderboard Splits The Hugging Face repository also contains private data: | Split | Questions | Private leaderboard | |---|---|---| | `level1` | 800 | Yes | | `level2` | 800 | Yes | | `level3` | 800 | Yes | | `level4` | 800 | Yes | | `level5` | 120 | Yes | Total private leaderboard size: **3320 questions**. The final rankings are determined by the Overall Accuracy on the **private leaderboard.** ## Schema Each row includes a stable `question_id` used for submission alignment. Common columns: | Column | Description | |---|---| | `question_id` | Stable ID used in submissions | | `row_id` | Row index within the split | | `idx` | Original source index | | `problem` | Problem text, when available | | `images` | Image list in Hugging Face image format | ## No-Answer Release This prevents answer leakage. Reference answers are kept only in the Codabench reference bundle used by the scoring program. ## Loading Example Load the Testmini dataset: ```python from datasets import load_dataset testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini") testmini_level1 = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini", split="level1_testmini") ``` Load the Test dataset: ```python from datasets import load_dataset level1_test = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="test") level1_testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="testmini") ``` If the repository is private, authenticate with a Hugging Face token before loading the dataset.
pages.content_zh:
# Seephys Pro 数据
基准数据：
https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro

项目主页：
https://seephyspro.github.io/

论文：
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.09266
## 公开排行榜数据集 在公开排行榜提交中使用以下数据集： | 数据集 | 问题数 | 公开排行榜 | |---|---|---| | `level1_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level2_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level3_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level4_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level5_testmini` | 30 | 是 | 公开排行榜总规模：**830 个问题**。 ## 私有排行榜数据集 Hugging Face 存储库还包含私有数据： | 数据集 | 问题数 | 私有排行榜 | |---|---|---| | `level1` | 800 | 是 | | `level2` | 800 | 是 | | `level3` | 800 | 是 | | `level4` | 800 | 是 | | `level5` | 120 | 是 | 私有排行榜总规模：**3320 个问题**。 最终排名由**私有排行榜**上的总体准确率决定。 ## 数据结构 每行包含用于提交对齐的稳定 `question_id`。 通用列： | 列 | 描述 | |---|---| | `question_id` | 提交中使用的稳定 ID | | `row_id` | 数据集中的行索引 | | `idx` | 原始来源索引 | | `problem` | 问题文本（如果可用） | | `images` | Hugging Face 图像格式的图像列表 | ## 无答案发布 这防止了答案泄露。参考答案仅保存在评分程序使用的 Codabench 参考包中。 ## 加载示例 加载 Testmini 数据集： ```python from datasets import load_dataset testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini") testmini_level1 = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini", split="level1_testmini") ``` 加载 Test 数据集： ```python from datasets import load_dataset level1_test = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="test") level1_testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="testmini") ``` 如果存储库是私有的，在加载数据集前使用 Hugging Face token 进行身份验证。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# SeePhys Pro Evaluation
Important: each submission must provide both a final answer and a supporting explanation. Responses that contain only a final answer without reasoning will not be considered complete.
## Submission Format Please submit a `.zip` file containing both public and private prediction files at the root of the archive. Your submission should contain: ```text submission.zip ├── prediction.csv └── prediction_private.csv ``` ### Required Files #### 1. `prediction.csv` This file is used for the Codabench public leaderboard. #### 2. `prediction_private.csv` This file is reserved for the private leaderboard. The private leaderboard will **not** be evaluated automatically on Codabench. After the competition deadline, the organizers will download the final submissions and evaluate `prediction_private.csv` offline using the private reference answers. ### Required Fields Both `prediction.csv` and `prediction_private.csv` must contain the following fields: ```text question_id prediction reasoning ``` ### Important Notes The public leaderboard score shown on Codabench is computed only from `prediction.csv`. The private leaderboard score is computed offline only from `prediction_private.csv`. If `prediction_private.csv` is missing, the submission will not receive a valid private leaderboard score. ## CSV Example ```csv question_id,prediction,reasoning level1_testmini_000000,BD,"The two circuit branches give the stated acceleration and charge relations, so B and D are correct." level1_testmini_000001,1.44,"Using Snell's law and the measured geometry, the refractive index is approximately 1.44." ``` ## JSONL Example ```jsonl {"question_id":"level1_testmini_000000","prediction":"BD","reasoning":"The induced-current relations make B and D correct."} {"question_id":"level5_testmini_000000","prediction":"A","reasoning":"The diagram matches option A after comparing the labeled quantities."} ``` ## Evaluation Rules Submissions are evaluated against hidden reference answers. For each question, the evaluation system checks whether: 1. A final answer is provided. 2. A reasoning field is provided. 3. The reasoning is relevant to the problem. 4. The submitted final answer is equivalent to the reference answer. 5. The reasoning supports the submitted final answer. 6. Required units are respected when applicable. A response may receive no credit if it contains any of the following issues: - missing final answer; - missing reasoning; - malformed or unreadable submission format; - unrelated or empty reasoning; - reasoning that contradicts the submitted answer; - incorrect final answer; - missing or incorrect units when units are required. ## Leaderboard Metrics The public leaderboard reports performance on the released public evaluation split. | Metric | Questions | Weight in Overall | |---|---:|---:| | Level 1 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Level 2 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Level 3 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Level 4 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Solve150 Testmini | 30 | 30 / 830 | The overall score is computed as: ```text overall = total_correct_questions / 830 ``` The leaderboard also reports: - `Judged`: number of rows successfully evaluated; - `Missing`: number of rows missing either a prediction or reasoning; - `Invalid`: number of malformed rows or rows missing required fields. ## Dataset Release The Hugging Face dataset is released as a public no-answer version for participant use. It intentionally excludes the following ground-truth fields: - `answer` - `unit` - `error_range` These fields are reserved for private evaluation and are not distributed to participants. ## Notes Participants are responsible for ensuring that all `question_id` values match the released dataset exactly. Duplicate predictions for the same `question_id` are not allowed and may be treated as invalid.
pages.content_zh:
# SeePhys Pro 评估
重要提示：每次提交必须同时提供最终答案和支持性解释。仅包含最终答案而没有推理过程的回答将不被视为完整提交。
## 提交格式 请提交一个包含公开和私密预测文件的 `.zip` 文件，文件位于压缩包根目录。 您的提交应包含： ```text submission.zip ├── prediction.csv └── prediction_private.csv ``` ### 必需文件 #### 1. `prediction.csv` 此文件用于 Codabench 公开排行榜。 #### 2. `prediction_private.csv` 此文件保留给私密排行榜使用。 私密排行榜**不会**在 Codabench 上自动评估。竞赛截止期限后，组织者将下载最终提交，使用私密参考答案离线评估 `prediction_private.csv`。 ### 必需字段 `prediction.csv` 和 `prediction_private.csv` 都必须包含以下字段： ```text question_id prediction reasoning ``` ### 重要说明 Codabench 上显示的公开排行榜分数仅根据 `prediction.csv` 计算。 私密排行榜分数仅根据 `prediction_private.csv` 离线计算。 如果缺少 `prediction_private.csv`，提交将无法获得有效的私密排行榜分数。 ## CSV 示例 ```csv question_id,prediction,reasoning level1_testmini_000000,BD,"两个电路分支给出了所述的加速度和电荷关系，所以 B 和 D 正确。" level1_testmini_000001,1.44,"使用斯涅尔定律和测量的几何关系，折射率约为 1.44。" ``` ## JSONL 示例 ```jsonl {"question_id":"level1_testmini_000000","prediction":"BD","reasoning":"感应电流关系使 B 和 D 正确。"} {"question_id":"level5_testmini_000000","prediction":"A","reasoning":"比较标记的物理量后，图表与选项 A 相匹配。"} ``` ## 评估规则 提交将根据隐藏的参考答案进行评估。对于每个问题，评估系统检查以下内容： 1. 是否提供了最终答案。 2. 是否提供了推理字段。 3. 推理是否与问题相关。 4. 提交的最终答案是否等同于参考答案。 5. 推理是否支持提交的最终答案。 6. 如适用，是否遵守了必需的单位。 如果回答包含以下任何问题，可能无法获得分数： - 缺少最终答案； - 缺少推理； - 提交格式格式不正确或无法读取； - 无关或空白的推理； - 与提交答案相矛盾的推理； - 最终答案不正确； - 当需要单位时缺少或单位不正确。 ## 排行榜指标 公开排行榜报告在已发布的公开评估集上的性能。 | 指标 | 问题数 | 总体权重 | |---|---:|---:| | 第 1 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | 第 2 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | 第 3 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | 第 4 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Solve150 Testmini | 30 | 30 / 830 | 总体分数计算方式为： ```text overall = total_correct_questions / 830 ``` 排行榜还报告： - `Judged`：成功评估的行数； - `Missing`：缺少预测或推理的行数； - `Invalid`：格式不正确或缺少必需字段的行数。 ## 数据集发布 Hugging Face 数据集以公开无答案版本发布供参与者使用。它意图排除以下真实标签字段： - `answer` - `unit` - `error_range` 这些字段保留用于私密评估，不向参与者分发。 ## 说明 参与者负责确保所有 `question_id` 值与已发布数据集完全匹配。同一 `question_id` 的重复预测不被允许，可能被视为无效。

pages.title: Terms
pages.content:
# Seephys Pro Terms and Participation Rules

By participating in this challenge, participants agree to follow the rules below.

## Allowed

- Use the Hugging Face dataset for model development, prompting, analysis, and submission generation.
- Use external models and tools, unless they violate Codabench, Hugging Face, or API provider policies.
- Submit final answers with reasoning in the required format.

## Not Allowed

- Attempt to recover, infer, scrape, or leak hidden reference answers.
- Attack, overload, or bypass the Codabench evaluation system.
- Submit prompt-injection text intended to manipulate the judge rather than answer the physics problem.
- Submit empty, unrelated, or intentionally deceptive reasoning.
- Upload content that violates the policies of Codabench, Hugging Face, OpenAI, or applicable law.

## Evaluation Integrity

The public judge uses an OpenAI API based semantic evaluator. The judge is intended to evaluate answer equivalence and reasoning consistency, not to reward formatting tricks. Submissions may be reviewed or removed if they exploit the evaluator or violate the spirit of the benchmark.

## Data Use

The Hugging Face dataset distributed for this challenge excludes answer-bearing fields. Reference answers are used only for Codabench scoring.
pages.content_zh:
# Seephys Pro 条款和参赛规则

参赛者参与此竞赛即表示同意遵守以下规则。

## 允许的行为

- 使用Hugging Face数据集进行模型开发、提示、分析和提交生成。
- 使用外部模型和工具，但不得违反Codabench、Hugging Face或API提供商的政策。
- 按照要求的格式提交包含推理过程的最终答案。

## 不允许的行为

- 尝试恢复、推断、抓取或泄露隐藏的参考答案。
- 攻击、过载或绕过Codabench评估系统。
- 提交旨在操纵评判者而非回答物理问题的提示注入文本。
- 提交空白、无关或故意欺骗性的推理。
- 上传违反Codabench、Hugging Face、OpenAI或适用法律政策的内容。

## 评估完整性

公开评判者采用基于OpenAI API的语义评估器。该评判者旨在评估答案等价性和推理一致性，而非奖励格式技巧。如果提交内容利用评估器漏洞或违反基准的本意，可能被审查或删除。

## 数据使用

为此竞赛分发的Hugging Face数据集不包含包含答案的字段。参考答案仅用于Codabench评分。

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Real-World All-in-One Image Restoration

Important dates

  • 2026.05.20 Release of train data (input and output)
  • 2026.05.20 Release of validation data (inputs only)
  • 2026.05.20 Validation server online
  • 2026.07.01 Final test data release (inputs only)
  • 2026.07.08 Test output results submission deadline
  • 2026.07.08 Fact sheets and code/executable submission deadline
  • 2026.07.15 Preliminary test results release to the participants
  • 2026.07.15 Paper submission deadline for entries (Internal Review)
  • 2026.09.x LoViF workshop and challenges, results and award ceremony (ECCV 2026)

 

Challenge overview

The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers with Generative AI, Preference Optimization and Agentic Systems (LoViF) will be held in September 2026 in conjunction with ECCV 2026.

Low-level vision is undergoing a paradigm shift. Traditional restoration methods are being augmented and redefined by Generative AI, Preference Optimization, and Agentic Systems. The LoViF workshop aims to explore these frontiers, focusing on how generative foundation models can provide superior priors, how human feedback can refine visual quality, and how intelligent agents can autonomously handle complex restoration tasks.

Recent advancements have shown that low-level tasks are no longer just about pixel-wise accuracy (like PSNR), but about perceptual richness and alignment with human intent. This evolution opens new doors in fields ranging from professional content creation to autonomous systems and wearable devices, where visual signal integrity and aesthetic quality are paramount.

This challenge, as a core part of the LoViF workshop, provides a platform for researchers and practitioners to benchmark their latest algorithms and push the boundaries of what is possible in next-generation image and video processing.

 

Motivation for this competition

While classic image restoration has seen significant progress, real-world scenarios often involve complex, non-linear degradations and subjective quality requirements that traditional metrics fail to capture. The emergence of Generative AI offers powerful tools to "re-imagine" lost details, yet maintaining high fidelity and aligning these outputs with human preferences remains a critical challenge.

To bridge this gap, the LoViF Challenge introduces a specialized benchmark focused on real-world all-in-one image restoration. We aim to encourage solutions that not only achieve high quantitative scores but also demonstrate superior visual quality and adaptability, potentially leveraging agent-based reasoning or preference-based refinement.

The goal of this competition is to establish a new and applicable benchmark for the community. The final ranking will be determined by a combination of standard metrics (e.g., PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS) and advanced perceptual or alignment-based evaluations.

Please refer to [1] [2] for more details about real-world all-in-one restoration data.

[1] Hao Li, Xiang Chen, Jiangxin Dong, Jinhui Tang, Jinshan Pan. "FoundIR: Unleashing Million-scale Training Data to Advance Foundation Models for Image Restoration", In: ICCV, 2025.

[2] Qiyuan Guan, Qianfeng Yang, Xiang Chen, Tianyu Song, Guiyue Jin, Jiyu Jin. "WeatherBench: A Real-World Benchmark Dataset for All-in-One Adverse Weather Image Restoration", In: ACM MM, 2025.

Top-ranked participants will be awarded and invited to submit their workshop papers to the ECCV 2026 Workshop proceedings, describing their winning solutions.

More details are found in the competition data section.

Competition

The training data will be made available to the registered participants according to the timeline.

Award

The following awards are provided:
  • One first-class award (i.e., the champion) with case prize US$1000;
  • One second-class award with case prize US$600;
  • One thrid-class award with case prize US$400;

Provided Resources

  • Scripts: The organizers will provide baseline models and evaluation scripts to ensure reproducibility and fair comparison. Check the "Data" page for more information.
  • Contact: Please use the forum on the challenge page for technical discussions, or contact the LoViF organizing committee via the official website: https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/, or directly contact the challenge organizers by email ( chenxiang [at] njust.edu.cn and haoli [at] njust.edu.cn) if you have doubts or any question.
pages.content_zh:
第2届低阶视觉前沿工作坊 (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

真实世界全能图像恢复挑战赛

重要日期

  • 2026.05.20 训练数据发布（输入和输出）
  • 2026.05.20 验证数据发布（仅输入）
  • 2026.05.20 验证服务器上线
  • 2026.07.01 最终测试数据发布（仅输入）
  • 2026.07.08 测试输出结果提交截止
  • 2026.07.08 事实表和代码/可执行文件提交截止
  • 2026.07.15 向参赛者发布初步测试结果
  • 2026.07.15 论文提交截止（内部评审）
  • 2026.09.x LoViF工作坊和挑战赛、结果发布及颁奖仪式 (ECCV 2026)

 

挑战赛概览

第2届低阶视觉前沿工作坊：生成式AI、偏好优化与智能体系统 (LoViF)将于2026年9月与ECCV 2026联合举办。

低阶视觉正在经历范式转变。传统恢复方法正在被生成式AI、偏好优化和智能体系统所增强和重新定义。LoViF工作坊旨在探索这些前沿领域，重点关注生成式基础模型如何提供优越先验、人类反馈如何改进视觉质量，以及智能体如何自主处理复杂恢复任务。

近期的进展表明，低阶任务不再仅是像素级精度（如PSNR），而是关乎感知丰富度和与人类意图的对齐。这一演进为专业内容创作、自主系统和可穿戴设备等领域打开了新的大门，在这些领域中视觉信号完整性和美学质量至关重要。

作为LoViF工作坊的核心部分，此挑战赛为研究人员和从业者提供了一个基准测试平台，用于评估其最新算法并推进下一代图像和视频处理技术的边界。

 

竞赛动机

虽然经典图像恢复取得了重大进展，但现实场景通常涉及复杂的非线性退化和主观质量要求，这些都是传统指标无法捕捉的。生成式AI的出现提供了"重新想象"丢失细节的强大工具，但保持高保真度并使这些输出与人类偏好对齐仍然是一个关键挑战。

为了弥补这一差距，LoViF挑战赛引入了一个专门的基准测试，专注于真实世界全能图像恢复。我们的目标是鼓励那些不仅能获得高量化分数，而且能展示优越视觉质量和自适应能力的解决方案，可能会利用基于智能体的推理或基于偏好的改进。

此竞赛的目标是为社区建立一个新的、可应用的基准。最终排名将由标准指标（如PSNR、SSIM、LPIPS）和高级感知或对齐评估相结合来确定。

请参考 [1] [2]了解更多关于真实世界全能恢复数据的详情。

[1] Hao Li, Xiang Chen, Jiangxin Dong, Jinhui Tang, Jinshan Pan. "FoundIR: Unleashing Million-scale Training Data to Advance Foundation Models

pages.title: data
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Real-World All-in-One Image Restoration

 

Data overview

The dataset for this challenge is provided by FoundIR and the LoViF organizing committee. It is specifically designed to benchmark the frontiers of low-level vision, incorporating five categories of common real-world degraded images: blur, low-light, haze, rain, and snow. The dataset includes diverse scenes for training, validation, and testing. Specifically, each degradation contains 4,900 paired LQ–GT image (512 X 512) pairs for training, 100 images for validation, and 100 images for testing.

In this competition, the dataset is composed of:

  • Train data: 24,500 pairs of degraded images and their corresponding ground truth references.
  • Validation data: 500 degraded images for performance tuning during the development phase.
  • Test data: 500 degraded images reserved for final performance evaluation.

Please note that in both the validation and test sets, images indexed from 0001-0100 correspond to Blur, 0101-0200 to Low-light, 0201-0300 to Haze, 0301-0400 to Rain, and 0401-0500 to Snow.

 

Data access

By accessing the data, participants implicitly agree with the terms and conditions of the LoViF challenge.

 

    • Development phase / Learning:
      • Train Data: [Google Drive]. Participants are allowed to use publicly available external datasets for training, as long as the sources of the data are explicitly documented in the factsheet. More details about the training data can be found in FoundIR.
      • Validation Data (input images only): [Google Drive].
      • Test Data (input images only): [TBA]

 

Final test phase submission guidelines (Factsheet can be found in [overleaf])

The final submission should be emailed to the organizers (see the "Organizers" tab for contact details).

The title of the mail should be: [LoViF2026] - [TASK_NAME] - [TEAM_NAME]

The body of the mail shall include the following:

      1. Challenge full name (LoViF 2026 - [Task Name])
      2. Team name
      3. Team leader's name and email address
      4. Rest of the team members
      5. Affiliations of the team members
      6. Team name and user names on the LoViF 2026 Codabench server
      7. Executable/source code attached or download links
      8. Factsheet attached (please use the provided LoViF template)
      9. Download link(s) to the FULL results of ALL test frames

The executable/source code should include trained models or necessary parameters to ensure reproducibility. A README file explaining how to run the code is mandatory. The Factsheet must be a compiled PDF file along with its LaTeX source files.

 

Final test phase - preliminary results [TBA]

Preliminary results will be posted here once the evaluation is complete.

Note that top-ranked participants will be invited to co-author the challenge report and contribute a paper to the LoViF 2026 workshop. All challenge papers will undergo a peer-review process. For more information, please check the LoViF 2026 official website.
pages.content_zh:
第二届低层次视觉前沿研讨会 (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

真实世界全能图像复原挑战赛

 

数据概览

本挑战赛的数据集由FoundIR和LoViF组织委员会提供。该数据集专门设计用于基准测试低层次视觉的前沿技术，涵盖五类常见真实世界退化图像：模糊、低光、雾霾、雨水和雪。数据集包含用于训练、验证和测试的多样化场景。具体来说，每种退化包含4,900对配对的LQ–GT图像（512×512）用于训练，100张图像用于验证，100张图像用于测试。

在本竞赛中，数据集由以下部分组成：

  • 训练数据：24,500对退化图像及其对应的真值参考。
  • 验证数据：500张退化图像，用于开发阶段的性能调优。
  • 测试数据：500张退化图像，用于最终性能评估。

请注意，在验证集和测试集中，索引为0001-0100的图像对应模糊、0101-0200对应低光、0201-0300对应雾霾、0301-0400对应雨水、0401-0500对应雪。

 

数据访问

通过访问数据，参与者隐含同意LoViF挑战赛的条款和条件。

 

    • 开发阶段/学习：
      • 训练数据：[谷歌云盘]。参与者允许使用公开可用的外部数据集进行训练，但数据源必须在说明书中明确记录。有关训练数据的更多详情可在FoundIR中找到。
      • 验证数据（仅输入图像）：[谷歌云盘]。
      • 测试数据（仅输入图像）：[待定]

 

最终测试阶段提交指南（说明书可在[Overleaf]中找到）

最终提交应通过电子邮件发送至组织者（详见"组织者"选项卡以获取联系方式）。

邮件标题应为：[LoViF2026] - [任务名称] - [团队名称]

邮件正文应包含以下内容：

      1. 挑战赛全名（LoViF 2026 - [任务名称]）
      2. 团队名称
      3. 团队负责人的名字和电子邮件地址
      4. 其他团队成员
      5. 团队成员的所属机构
      6. LoViF 2026 Codabench服务器上的团队名称和用户名
      7. 可执行文件/源代码附件或下载链接
      8. 说明书附件（请使用提供的LoViF模板）
      9. 所有测试帧的完整结果的下载链接

可执行文件/源代码应包含训练模型或必要参数以确保可重复性。说明如何运行代码的README文件是必需的。说明书必须是编译后的PDF文件以及其LaTeX源文件。

 

最终测试阶段 - 初步结果 [待定]

评估完成后，初步结果将发布在此处。

请注意，排名靠前的参与者将被邀请共同撰写挑战赛报告，并为LoViF 2026研讨会投稿论文。所有挑战赛论文都将经过同行评审过程。更多信息请查看

pages.title: evaluation
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Real-World All-in-One Image Restoration

 

Evaluation

The evaluation consists of the comparison of the restored/generated images with the reference ground truth images. Please ensure all images are processed using a unified model to maintain consistency across different test scenarios.

To comprehensively assess performance in the frontiers of low-level vision, we consider both fidelity-based and perception-based metrics, including PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS.

As the goal of this challenge is to explore a robust and powerful all-in-one image restoration model, participants are required to use one restoration network to handle all types of degradations and obtain the final results. Specifically, participants should ensure that their final submission contains only one trained image restoration model parameter file (e.g., .pt, .pth), which facilitates reproducibility of the experimental results by the organizers. It should be noted that the use of existing pre-trained networks (e.g., VAE, CLIP) for feature extraction is allowed and will not be counted as the aforementioned model parameter file.

The final ranking will be determined by a composite score that balances pixel-wise accuracy and perceptual quality. The weighted formula for the initial results is:
Final_Score = PSNR (Y) + 10 * SSIM (Y) - 5 * LPIPS.

For submitting the results, please follow the instructions below:

  1. To ensure fairness in evaluation, participants are recommended to save the model's output images in JPG format using the code provided below. 
    
from PIL import Image
img = Image.open(img_path)
img.load()
if img.mode != "RGB":
    img = img.convert("RGB")
save_kwargs = dict(format="JPEG", quality=96, subsampling=0, optimize=True)
img.save(out_path, **save_kwargs)
  2. Process the input images and keep the same name for the output image results as produced by your method (example: for an input file with the name "0001.jpg" the output file should be "0001.jpg"). Note that the output images should have the size of the input images. [Submission example] .
  3. Ensure your submission includes a short description of your method (Fact Sheet).
  4. Submit your results to the Codabench server before the deadline.

 

For more details regarding the workshop themes such as Preference Optimization or Agentic Systems in evaluation, please visit our official website: https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/
pages.content_zh:
第2届低级视觉前沿研讨会(LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

真实世界全能图像复原挑战赛

 

评估

评估包括将复原/生成的图像与参考真实图像进行比较。请确保所有图像使用统一的模型进行处理，以保持不同测试场景的一致性。

为了全面评估低级视觉前沿领域的性能，我们采用基于保真度和基于感知的指标，包括PSNR、SSIM和LPIPS。

由于本挑战赛的目标是探索稳健且强大的全能图像复原模型，参赛者需要使用一个复原网络来处理所有类型的图像退化并获得最终结果。 具体来说，参赛者应确保最终提交仅包含一个训练好的图像复原模型参数文件（例如.pt、.pth），这有利于组织方对实验结果的可重复性验证。 需要说明的是，允许使用现有的预训练网络（例如VAE、CLIP）进行特征提取，这些不计入上述模型参数文件。

最终排名将由平衡像素级精度和感知质量的综合得分决定。初始结果的加权公式为：
Final_Score = PSNR (Y) + 10 * SSIM (Y) - 5 * LPIPS。

提交结果时，请遵循以下说明：

  1. 为确保评估的公平性，参赛者建议使用以下提供的代码将模型输出图像保存为JPG格式。 
    
from PIL import Image
img = Image.open(img_path)
img.load()
if img.mode != "RGB":
    img = img.convert("RGB")
save_kwargs = dict(format="JPEG", quality=96, subsampling=0, optimize=True)
img.save(out_path, **save_kwargs)
  2. 处理输入图像，并为你的方法生成的输出图像结果保留相同的名称（示例：对于名为"0001.jpg"的输入文件，输出文件应为"0001.jpg"）。注意输出图像应具有与输入图像相同的尺寸。[提交示例]。
  3. 确保你的提交包含方法的简短描述（事实说明书）。
  4. 在截止日期前将你的结果提交到Codabench服务器。

 

有关研讨会主题的更多详情，如偏好优化或评估中的代理系统，请访问我们的官方网站：https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/

pages.title: organizers
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Organizers

 

The LoViF challenge is organized jointly with The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers with Generative AI, Preference Optimization and Agentic Systems (LoViF) and the LoViF 2026 workshop. The results of the challenge will be published at the LoViF 2026 workshop and in the ECCV 2026 Workshop proceedings.

If you have any questions, please contact persons and direct managers of the LoViF Real-World All-in-One Image Restoration challenge:
