pages.title: overview
pages.content:
🏆 MISP Challenge 2026

Audio-Visual-Text Multimodal Keyword Spotting Challenge

📋 Overview

This competition focuses on audio-visual-text multimodal keyword spotting task in realistic speech processing scenarios.

Task Description

Your model needs to determine whether given audio-video pairs come from the same speaker and the same keyword.

inset vs outset

Term Description
inset Keywords seen during training
outset New keywords not seen during training

Evaluation Metrics

AUC_INSET

AUC on inset keywords
Higher is better

EER_INSET

EER on inset (%)
Lower is better

AUC_OUTSET

AUC on outset keywords
Higher is better

EER_OUTSET

EER on outset (%)
Lower is better

🚀 Quick Start - 5 Steps to Submit

1
Generate Predictions

Run your model on the test set and collect prediction results

2
Prepare CSV

Save predictions in the required CSV format

3
Compress CSV

Directly compress CSV file to zip format

4
Upload to Codabench

Submit your zip file on this platform

5
View Results

Wait for scoring and check the leaderboard!

⚠️ Important Update:

You do NOT need to submit code or models! Only a CSV file with your predictions is required.

📊 Metric Interpretation

AUC (Area Under Curve)

  • Value Range: 0.0 - 1.0
  • Perfect Score: 1.0
  • Random Score: 0.5
  • Higher AUC indicates better model discrimination ability

EER (Equal Error Rate)

  • Value Range: 0% - 100%
  • Perfect Score: 0%
  • Random Score: 50%
  • Lower EER indicates better model balance performance

📞 Technical Support

If you encounter issues during submission, please check the Data & Submission Guide tab or use the competition forum.

🏆 MISP Challenge 2026

音频-视频-文本多模态关键词检测竞赛

📋 竞赛概述

本次竞赛聚焦于真实语音处理场景下的音频-视频-文本多模态关键词检测任务。

任务简介

您的模型需要判断给定的音频-视频对是否来自同一说话人和同一关键词。

inset vs outset

术语 说明
inset 训练时见过的词汇
outset 训练时未见过的新词汇

评价指标

AUC_INSET

inset词汇的AUC
越高越好

EER_INSET

inset词汇的EER (%)
越低越好

AUC_OUTSET

outset词汇的AUC
越高越好

EER_OUTSET

outset词汇的EER (%)
越低越好

🚀 快速入门 - 5步完成提交

1
生成预测

在测试集上运行您的模型，收集预测结果

2
准备CSV

按照要求格式保存预测结果为CSV

3
压缩CSV

直接将CSV文件压缩为zip格式

4
上传到Codabench

在此平台提交您的zip文件

5
查看结果

等待评分，查看排行榜！

⚠️ 重要更新:

您不需要提交代码和模型！只需提交预测结果的CSV文件即可。

📊 指标解读

AUC (Area Under Curve)

  • 取值范围: 0.0 - 1.0
  • 完美分数: 1.0
  • 随机分数: 0.5
  • AUC越高表示模型的区分能力越好

EER (Equal Error Rate)

  • 取值范围: 0% - 100%
  • 完美分数: 0%
  • 随机分数: 50%
  • EER越低表示模型的平衡性能越好

📞 技术支持

如果在提交过程中遇到问题，请查看数据与提交指南标签页，或使用竞赛论坛。
pages.content_zh:
# 🏆 MISP Challenge 2026

音频-视觉-文本多模态关键词识别挑战

## 📋 概述

本竞赛专注于现实语音处理场景中的音频-视觉-文本多模态关键词识别任务。

### 任务描述

您的模型需要判断给定的音频-视频对是否来自同一说话者和同一关键词。

### 内集合 vs 外集合

| 术语 | 描述 |
|------|------|
| **inset（内集合）** | 训练过程中看到的关键词 |
| **outset（外集合）** | 训练过程中未见过的新关键词 |

### 评估指标

- **AUC_INSET**
  内集合关键词上的AUC
  越高越好

- **EER_INSET**
  内集合上的EER (%)
  越低越好

- **AUC_OUTSET**
  外集合关键词上的AUC
  越高越好

- **EER_OUTSET**
  外集合上的EER (%)
  越低越好

## 🚀 快速开始 - 提交的5个步骤

1. **生成预测**
   在测试集上运行您的模型并收集预测结果

2. **准备CSV**
   以所需的CSV格式保存预测结果

3. **压缩CSV**

pages.title: data
pages.content:
📊 Data & Submission Guide

Complete guide for data format and submission requirements

📁 Submission Requirements

⚠️ Important:

You only need to submit a zip file containing ONE CSV file. No code or model files required!

File Required Description
predictions.csv ✅ Required Your prediction results (any filename ending with .csv works)
submission.zip ✅ Required Zip file containing only the CSV file

📝 CSV Format Specification

Required Columns

Column Name Type Description
id Integer Sample index, starting from 0
type String Must be either "inset" or "outset"
prediction Float Prediction score between 0.0 and 1.0

Important Rules

  • id Numbering: Both inset and outset must start from 0 and be numbered separately
  • Prediction Scores: Must be float values between 0.0 and 1.0 representing match probability
    • Closer to 1 means more likely to be a match
    • Closer to 0 means less likely to be a match
  • Sample Order: Prediction order must strictly match the test set order
  • Completeness: Must include predictions for all test samples

CSV Example

id,type,prediction
0,inset,0.85
1,inset,0.12
2,inset,0.95
3,inset,0.34
...
99,inset,0.67
0,outset,0.45
1,outset,0.78
2,outset,0.23
...
99,outset,0.56

💻 How to Generate Prediction CSV

Python Code Example

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

def generate_predictions_csv(inset_predictions, outset_predictions, output_file="predictions.csv"):
    """
    Generate CSV file for competition submission

    Args:
        inset_predictions: list/array, inset prediction scores (0-1 between)
        outset_predictions: list/array, outset prediction scores (0-1 between)
        output_file: output filename
    """
    data = []

    # Add inset predictions
    for i, pred in enumerate(inset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'inset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # Add outset predictions
    for i, pred in enumerate(outset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'outset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # Create DataFrame and save
    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    df.to_csv(output_file, index=False)

    print(f"✅ Prediction CSV generated: {output_file}")
    print(f"   - inset samples: {len(inset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - outset samples: {len(outset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - Total samples: {len(data)}")

    return df

# Usage example
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Replace with your actual predictions
    num_inset = 100   # Replace with actual number of inset samples
    num_outset = 100  # Replace with actual number of outset samples

    my_inset_preds = np.random.rand(num_inset)   # Replace with your predictions
    my_outset_preds = np.random.rand(num_outset) # Replace with your predictions

    # Generate CSV
    generate_predictions_csv(my_inset_preds, my_outset_preds, "predictions.csv")

Prediction Value Notes

✅ Correct Prediction Values:
  • Floats between 0.0 and 1.0
  • Probability values after sigmoid or softmax
❌ Incorrect Prediction Values:
  • Negative numbers: -0.1
  • Numbers greater than 1: 1.5
  • Logits (not through sigmoid): 2.3, -1.2

📤 How to Submit

Submission Steps

  1. Prepare CSV File: Generate predictions.csv following the instructions above
  2. Compress File: Compress the CSV file to zip format
    • Windows: Right-click CSV file → Send to → Compressed (zipped) folder
    • Mac/Linux: zip submission.zip predictions.csv
  3. Upload to Codabench: Click "Submit" button and upload your zip file
  4. Wait for Processing: Platform will automatically process your submission
  5. View Results: After processing is complete, results will appear on the leaderboard
⚠️ Important Notes:
  • Ensure the zip contains only one CSV file
  • Do not include folders in the zip
  • Ensure CSV format is correct
  • Prediction order must be correct

📥 Downloads

Available Downloads

  • Test Dataset - Preprocessed test data
  • Sample Predictions - Example CSV file showing correct format
  • Example Code - Python script for generating submission CSV

📊 数据与提交指南

数据格式和提交要求的完整指南

📁 提交要求

⚠️ 重要:

您只需要提交一个包含CSV文件的zip文件！不需要提交代码或模型文件！

文件 必需 说明
predictions.csv ✅ 必需 您的预测结果（任何以.csv结尾的文件名都可以）
submission.zip ✅ 必需 只包含CSV文件的zip文件

📝 CSV格式说明

必需列

列名 类型 说明
id 整数 样本编号，从0开始
type 字符串 必须是 "inset" 或 "outset"
prediction 浮点数 0.0到1.0之间的预测分数

重要规则

  • id编号: inset和outset必须分别从0开始编号
  • 预测分数: 必须是0.0到1.0之间的浮点数，表示匹配概率
    • 越接近1表示越可能匹配
    • 越接近0表示越不可能匹配
  • 样本顺序: 预测顺序必须严格与测试集顺序一致
  • 完整性: 必须包含所有测试样本的预测

CSV示例

id,type,prediction
0,inset,0.85
1,inset,0.12
2,inset,0.95
3,inset,0.34
...
99,inset,0.67
0,outset,0.45
1,outset,0.78
2,outset,0.23
...
99,outset,0.56

💻 如何生成预测CSV

Python代码示例

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

def generate_predictions_csv(inset_predictions, outset_predictions, output_file="predictions.csv"):
    """
    生成竞赛提交用的CSV文件

    参数:
        inset_predictions: list/array，inset的预测分数（0-1之间）
        outset_predictions: list/array，outset的预测分数（0-1之间）
        output_file: 输出文件名
    """
    data = []

    # 添加inset预测
    for i, pred in enumerate(inset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'inset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # 添加outset预测
    for i, pred in enumerate(outset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'outset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # 创建DataFrame并保存
    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    df.to_csv(output_file, index=False)

    print(f"✅ 预测CSV已生成: {output_file}")
    print(f"   - inset样本数: {len(inset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - outset样本数: {len(outset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - 总样本数: {len(data)}")

    return df

# 使用示例
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # 替换为您的实际预测
    num_inset = 100   # 替换为实际的inset样本数
    num_outset = 100  # 替换为实际的outset样本数

    my_inset_preds = np.random.rand(num_inset)   # 替换为您的预测
    my_outset_preds = np.random.rand(num_outset) # 替换为您的预测

    # 生成CSV
    generate_predictions_csv(my_inset_preds, my_outset_preds, "predictions.csv")

预测值注意事项

✅ 正确的预测值:
  • 0.0到1.0之间的浮点数
  • 经过sigmoid或softmax后的概率值
❌ 错误的预测值:
  • 负数: -0.1
  • 大于1的数: 1.5
  • logits（未经过sigmoid）: 2.3, -1.2

📤 如何提交

提交步骤

  1. 准备CSV文件: 按照上述说明生成predictions.csv
  2. 压缩文件: 将CSV文件压缩为zip格式
    • Windows: 右键CSV文件 → 发送到 → 压缩(zipped)文件夹
    • Mac/Linux: zip submission.zip predictions.csv
  3. 上传到Codabench: 点击"提交"按钮，上传您的zip文件
  4. 等待处理: 平台会自动处理您的提交
  5. 查看结果: 处理完成后，结果将显示在排行榜上
⚠️ 重要注意事项:
  • 确保zip中只包含一个CSV文件
  • zip中不要包含文件夹
  • 确保CSV格式正确
  • 预测顺序必须正确

📥 下载

可用下载

  • 测试数据集 - 预处理后的测试数据
  • 示例预测 - 展示正确格式的示例CSV文件
  • 示例代码 - 用于生成提交CSV的Python脚本

16375.title: EventAid Frame Interpolation Challenge @ EBMV Workshop, ECCV 2026
16375.description: Event-based RGB frame interpolation with 1, 3, 7, and 15 skipped-frame settings, based on EventAid benchmark. EventAid Challenge is a track of the EBMV (Event-Based Multimodal Vision: Imaging, Perception, and Understanding) Workshop @ ECCV 2026!
16375.description_zh: 基于EventAid基准的RGB帧插值，包含1、3、7和15跳帧设置。EventAid挑战赛是EBMV（事件驱动多模态视觉：成像、感知与理解）研讨会@ECCV 2026的一个赛道！

pages.title: Participation
pages.content:
# EventAid Frame Interpolation Challenge

This challenge is a track of the EBMV (Event-Based Multimodal Vision: Imaging, Perception, and Understanding) Workshop @ ECCV 2026! Workshop homepage: https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io/

If you encounter any issues, please contact the organizers: Peiqi Duan (duanqi0001@pku.edu.cn), Boyu Li (liboyu@pku.edu.cn), or Hanyue Lou (hylz@pku.edu.cn).

## Task

This challenge is an event-based frame interpolation task. For each sequence, a method is given:

- a sparse set of RGB input frames,
- event streams between frames,
- timestamps and frame metadata.

The goal is to reconstruct every frame marked as `[TODO]` in `frame_info.txt`.

The data is organized into four interpolation settings:

- `1skip`: reconstruct 1 missing frame between neighboring input frames.
- `3skip`: reconstruct 3 missing frames between neighboring input frames.
- `7skip`: reconstruct 7 missing frames between neighboring input frames.
- `15skip`: reconstruct 15 missing frames between neighboring input frames.

Larger skip settings have wider temporal gaps and are more difficult.

## Data Usage

This challenge uses a subset of the publicly released EventAid dataset. The full public EventAid dataset includes ground-truth data for scenes that overlap with this challenge, and using it can easily cause overfitting or data leakage even if the ground truth is not directly inspected. Therefore, participants must **not** use the publicly released EventAid dataset, or any data derived from it, for training, validation, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, pretraining, or any other part of method development.

Participants may use the data officially provided for this challenge, including the released input data and the validation sequence ground truth, for developing and validating their methods. The validation sequence is provided only to help participants verify the data format and evaluation pipeline; it is not part of the final scored test set.

Participants may use any other real or synthetic datasets, pretrained models, or external resources, provided that they are legally accessible and are not based on the public EventAid dataset. All external datasets and pretrained resources must be clearly disclosed in the final technical report. Example training datasources include: Synthetic EVFI data based on video datasets such as [GOPRO](https://github.com/SeungjunNah/DeepDeblur_release), [REDS](https://seungjunnah.github.io/Datasets/reds) and [Adobe240FPS](https://www.cs.ubc.ca/labs/imager/tr/2017/DeepVideoDeblurring/), and event simulators such as [ESIM](https://github.com/uzh-rpg/rpg_esim), [V2E](https://github.com/SensorsINI/v2e) and [V2V](https://github.com/HYLZ-2019/V2V); Real EVFI datasets such as [HQ-EVFI](https://openimaginglab.github.io/TimeLens-XL/) and [ERF-X170FPS](https://github.com/intelpro/CBMNet).

The hidden test set must only be used for official evaluation. Participants must not attempt to access, reconstruct, manually annotate, or reverse-engineer the hidden ground-truth data.

## Public Data

Download `challenge_data.zip` from the public data tab and extract it:

```bash
unzip challenge_data.zip
```

The extracted directory contains:

```text
challenge_data/
  validation/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      gt/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  test/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  run_baseline_method.py
  evaluate_results.py
  terms.md
```

The validation split includes ground-truth frames under `gt/` so that participants can verify their pipeline locally. The test split does not include ground truth and is used for official ranking.

## Submission Format

Submit only reconstructed `[TODO]` frames. The required layout is:

```text
results/
  {skip}/
    {sequence}/
      {frame_index:06d}.png
```

For example:

```text
results/1skip/ball/000001.png
results/15skip/traffic/000016.png
```

All submitted images must be PNG files with the exact `width height` in the corresponding `shape.txt`.

Create the submission zip from the directory containing `results/`:

```bash
zip -r submission.zip results
```

## Local Baseline and Evaluation

A simple linear interpolation baseline is provided. It ignores events and interpolates between neighboring input frames:

```bash
python challenge_data/run_baseline_method.py \
  --data-dir challenge_data \
  --output-dir results
```

After producing a `results/` directory, run:

```bash
python challenge_data/evaluate_results.py \
  --data-dir challenge_data \
  --results-dir results
```

This script checks the submission format for both validation and test splits, and computes PSNR/SSIM only on the public validation ground truth. The script cannot compute test-set metrics because test ground truth is not public.

## Evaluation Phases

The validation phase reports PSNR and SSIM on the public validation split. After the validation phase closes, the organizers evaluate each team's last valid validation-phase submission on the hidden test split for the final ranking.
pages.content_zh:
# EventAid帧插值挑战赛

本挑战赛是ECCV 2026 EBMV（基于事件的多模态视觉：成像、感知和理解）研讨会的一个分赛道！研讨会主页：https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io/

如遇到任何问题，请联系组织者：段佩琪 (duanqi0001@pku.edu.cn)、李博宇 (liboyu@pku.edu.cn) 或娄寒月 (hylz@pku.edu.cn)。

## 任务

本挑战赛是一项基于事件的帧插值任务。对于每个序列，参赛者将获得：

- 一组稀疏的RGB输入帧，
- 帧之间的事件流，
- 时间戳和帧元数据。

目标是重建`frame_info.txt`中所有标记为`[TODO]`的帧。

数据分为四种插值设置：

- `1skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建1个缺失帧。
- `3skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建3个缺失帧。
- `7skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建7个缺失帧。
- `15skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建15个缺失帧。

较大的跳过设置具有更宽的时间间隙，难度更大。

## 数据使用

本挑战赛使用了公开发布的EventAid数据集的一个子集。完整的公开EventAid数据集包含与本挑战赛重叠的场景的真值数据，使用该数据容易导致过拟合或数据泄漏，即使不直接检查真值也是如此。因此，参赛者**不得**使用公开发布的EventAid数据集或任何源自该数据集的数据进行训练、验证、模型选择、超参数调整、预训练或方法开发的任何其他部分。

参赛者可以使用本挑战赛正式提供的数据，包括发布的输入数据和验证序列真值，用于方法开发和验证。验证序列仅提供用于帮助参赛者验证数据格式和评估流程；它不属于最终计分的测试集。

参赛者可以使用任何其他真实或合成数据集、预训练模型或外部资源，前提是这些资源在法律上可访问且不基于公开EventAid数据集。所有外部数据集和预训练资源必须在最终技术报告中清楚地披露。示例训练数据源包括：基于视频数据集（如[GOPRO](https://github.com/SeungjunNah/DeepDeblur_release)、[REDS](https://seungjunnah.github.io/Datasets/reds)和[Adobe240FPS](https://www.cs.ubc.ca/labs/imager/tr/2017/DeepVideoDeblurring/)）的合成EVFI数据，以及事件模拟器（如[ESIM](https://github.com/uzh-rpg/rpg_esim)、[V2E](https://github.com/SensorsINI/v2e)和[V2V](https://github.com/HYLZ-2019/V2V)）；真实EVFI数据集，如[HQ-EVFI](https://openimaginglab.github.io/TimeLens-XL/)和[ERF-X170FPS](https://github.com/intelpro/CBMNet)。

隐藏测试集仅用于官方评估。参赛者不得尝试访问、重构、手动标注或反向工程隐藏的真值数据。

## 公共数据

从公共数据选项卡下载`challenge_data.zip`并解压：

```bash
unzip challenge_data.zip
```

解压后的目录包含：

```text
challenge_data/
  validation/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      gt/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  test/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  run_baseline_method.py
  evaluate_results.py
  terms.md
```

验证集在`gt/`下包含真值帧，以便参赛者在本地验证其流程。测试集不包含真值，用于官方排名。

## 提交格式

仅提交重建的`[TODO]`帧。所需的布局为：

```text
results/
  {skip}/
    {sequence}/
      {frame_index:06d}.png
```

例如：

```text
results/1skip/ball
```

16261.title: Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Semantic Segmentation Track @ ECCV 2026

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Semantic Segmentation Track @ ECCV 2026

Welcome to the **Semantic Segmentation Track** of the **Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge**, officially hosted at **ECCV 2026**!

## 🎯 Overview & Objective

This challenge aims to advance **semantic segmentation** using **event-based vision and frame data**. Unlike conventional RGB cameras, event cameras asynchronously capture brightness changes with high temporal resolution, low latency, and high dynamic range, making them particularly suitable for challenging scenarios such as high-speed motion, low-light environments, and scenes with strong illumination changes.

The objective of this track is to benchmark and promote robust semantic segmentation methods that can effectively leverage complementary information from **event streams and intensity frames**. Participants are expected to develop models that predict accurate pixel-level semantic labels from multimodal inputs, with a particular focus on generalization, robustness, and scene understanding under challenging visual conditions.

## 📝 Task Description

Participants are required to submit predicted semantic segmentation maps for the test set. Each prediction should follow the official submission format and correspond to the provided event and frame input data.

The task focuses on:

- **Semantic Segmentation**: Predicting pixel-level semantic labels from event and frame data.
- **Multimodal Fusion**: Effectively combining asynchronous event streams with image frames for semantic perception.
- **Pixel-Level Scene Understanding**: Assigning each valid pixel to a predefined semantic category.
- **Robust Perception**: Handling challenging visual conditions such as low illumination, high dynamic range.

## 📅 Important Dates

All deadlines are at **11:59 PM UTC**, unless otherwise specified.

- **May 13, 2026**: Challenge Website Opens.
- **May 15, 2026**: Training Set Release.
- **June 20, 2026**: Test Set & Evaluation Server Online.
- **June 30, 2026**: Challenge Submission Deadline.
- **July 5, 2026**: Challenge Results Announcement.
- **July 10, 2026**: Technical Report Deadline.

## 📊 Dataset

This track uses subsets of the **[CoSEC dataset](https://cosec.org.cn/)** and the **[REAL dataset](https://github.com/jinghan-xu/NEC-Diff)**. The dataset provides multimodal sensory data, including image frames, event streams, and semantic segmentation annotations.

The released data for this track includes:

- **Image Frames**: RGB frames captured from a monocular camera.
- **Event Streams**: Asynchronous event data recorded by an event camera.
- **Semantic Segmentation Labels**: Ground-truth pixel-level semantic labels provided for supervised training and validation.

The training sets are released for model development and performance validation. The test set will be used for official evaluation, and its ground-truth semantic segmentation labels will remain hidden.

The dataset statistics for this track are summarized below:

| Split | Number of Samples | 
|---|---:|
| Training | 2569 |
| Test | To be released | 

## ⚠️ Challenge Rules

To maintain fairness and scientific integrity, all participants must strictly follow the challenge rules:

- **No Test-Set Training**: The test set must not be used for training, validation, model selection, or manual tuning.
- **No Ground-Truth Probing**: Participants must not attempt to infer, reconstruct, or extract hidden ground-truth semantic segmentation labels from the evaluation server.
- **No Manual Annotation**: Submitted predictions must be generated by an automated method. Manually created, manually corrected, or manually tuned predictions are not allowed.
- **No Evaluation Server Exploitation**: Any attempt to exploit the evaluation system, submission feedback, or leaderboard behavior to gain an unfair advantage is strictly prohibited.
- **External Resources Disclosure**: Any external datasets, pretrained models, synthetic data, or additional training resources used by participants must be disclosed in the final technical report if requested.
- **Verification Requirement**: Top-ranked teams may be required to submit a short technical report, code, logs, or additional information for result verification.

## 📩 Contact

For questions about the challenge, please contact the organizers through the official competition page or by email.
pages.content_zh:
# 基于事件的多模态感知挑战赛：语义分割赛道 @ ECCV 2026

欢迎参加**基于事件的多模态感知挑战赛**的**语义分割赛道**，官方举办地为**ECCV 2026**！

## 🎯 概述与目标

本挑战赛旨在使用**事件摄像头和帧数据**推进**语义分割**技术发展。与传统RGB摄像头不同，事件摄像头以高时间分辨率、低延迟和高动态范围异步捕捉亮度变化，特别适合应对高速运动、低光环境和强光照变化等具有挑战性的场景。

本赛道的目标是评估和推动稳健的语义分割方法，使其能够有效利用**事件流和强度帧**的互补信息。参与者需要开发能够从多模态输入预测精确的像素级语义标签的模型，重点关注在具有挑战性视觉条件下的泛化性、鲁棒性和场景理解能力。

## 📝 任务描述

参赛者需要为测试集提交预测的语义分割图。每个预测应遵循官方提交格式，并对应提供的事件和帧输入数据。

该任务重点关注：

- **语义分割**：从事件和帧数据预测像素级语义标签。
- **多模态融合**：有效结合异步事件流和图像帧进行语义感知。
- **像素级场景理解**：将每个有效像素分配到预定义的语义类别。
- **鲁棒感知**：处理低照度、高动态范围等具有挑战性的视觉条件。

## 📅 重要日期

所有截止日期均为**世界协调时间午夜11:59**，除非另有说明。

- **2026年5月13日**：挑战赛网站开放。
- **2026年5月15日**：训练集发布。
- **2026年6月20日**：测试集与评估服务器上线。
- **2026年6月30日**：挑战赛提交截止。
- **2026年7月5日**：挑战赛结果公布。
- **2026年7月10日**：技术报告截止。

## 📊 数据集

本赛道使用**[CoSEC数据集](https://cosec.org.cn/)**和**[REAL数据集](https://github.com/jinghan-xu/NEC-Diff)**的子集。数据集提供多模态传感器数据，包括图像帧、事件流和语义分割标注。

本赛道发布的数据包括：

- **图像帧**：单目摄像头捕捉的RGB帧。
- **事件流**：由事件摄像头记录的异步事件数据。
- **语义分割标签**：为监督学习和验证提供的像素级语义标签真实数据。

训练集用于模型开发和性能验证。测试集用于官方评估，其语义分割标签真实数据保持隐藏。

本赛道的数据集统计信息汇总如下：

| 数据集 | 样本数量 |
|---|---:|
| 训练集 | 2569 |
| 测试集 | 待发布 |

## ⚠️ 挑战赛规则

为保持公平性和科学诚实性，所有参与者必须严格遵守挑战赛规则：

- **禁止使用测试集训练**：测试集不得用于训练、验证、模型选择或手动调优。
- **禁止测试集标签探测**：参与者不得尝试从评估服务器推断、重建或提取隐藏的语义分割标签真实数据。
- **禁止手动标注**：提交的预测必须由自动化方法生成。禁止使用手动创建、手动修正或手动调优的预测。
- **禁止评估服务器滥用**：严格禁止任何尝试利用评估系统、提交反馈或排行榜行为来获得不公平优势的行为。
- **外部资源披露**：任何外部数据集、预训练模型、合成数据或其他

pages.title: Data Preparation
pages.content:
# Data Preparation Guide

To participate in the **Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Semantic Segmentation Track**, participants need to prepare the training data, understand the data format, and generate semantic segmentation predictions for the test set once it is released.

This track uses a curated subset of the **[CoSEC dataset](https://cosec.org.cn/)** for event-based semantic segmentation. Each sequence contains multimodal data, including image frames, event streams, timestamps, and semantic segmentation annotations for training.

## Training Set Download

The training set contains image frames, event streams, timestamps, and ground-truth semantic segmentation labels. Participants may use the training set to develop, train, and validate their semantic segmentation models.

The training split includes:

- Image frames from the left camera.
- Event streams from the left event camera.
- Timestamps for samples.
- Ground-truth semantic segmentation labels.

Please download the official training set from the following links:

👉 **Download CoSEC Segmentation Training Set**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: [train.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1TeXpiWIRDxGcbjubGB1bCA?pwd=HUST)

- **Google Drive**: [train.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1V29ahlLQTm2O__I-VI_qloR9JkzGYmex/view?usp=drive_link)

## Test Set Download

The official test set has **not been released yet**.

The test set will be released according to the challenge schedule. Once released, the download links will be updated on this page.

The test split will contain image frames, event streams, and timestamps. The ground-truth semantic segmentation labels for the test set will remain hidden and will be used only by the Codabench evaluation server.

Please download the official test set from the following links once it is released:

👉 **Download CoSEC Semantic Segmentation Test Set**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: To be released

- **Google Drive**: To be released

## Dataset Structure and Format

The expected dataset structure is:

```text
segmentation_challenge
├── train
│   ├── Day_Campus_000
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   ├── Day_Campus_001
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   └── ...
└── test
    ├── Day_Campus_012
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    ├── Day_Campus_013
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    └── ...
```

Each sample is identified by its sequence name and frame file name. The image frame and semantic segmentation label with the same file name correspond to the same sample.

For a training sample:

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

The corresponding event data is stored in the sequence-level HDF5 file:

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/events_co_left.h5
```

The timestamps are stored as:

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/timestamps.txt
```

For a test sample after the test set is released:

```text
segmentation/test/Day_Campus_012/img_co_left/000000.png
```

the expected submitted prediction should be:

```text
Day_Campus_012/segment_co/000000.
```

### Image Frame Format

Image frames are stored as `.png` files under the `img_co_left` folder, with a resolution of `1200 x 624`. They have been aligned with the event streams and can be used together with the event data for multimodal semantic segmentation.

### Event Stream Format

Each sequence contains one `events_co_left.h5` file. The event stream has already been aligned with the image frames.

A typical event file contains the following fields:

```text
x          # event x coordinates
y          # event y coordinates
p          # event polarities
t          # event timestamps
ms_to_idx  # mapping from millisecond timestamp to event index
```

The event timestamps `t` are stored in microseconds. The `ms_to_idx` array provides a convenient way to quickly locate event indices by millisecond-level timestamps.

For a given image frame, participants may use the corresponding timestamp in `timestamps.txt` to extract a temporal window of events. For example, one can take the events within `50 ms` before the image exposure end timestamp and use them together with the image frame for multimodal semantic segmentation.

Example code:

```python
import os
import cv2
import h5py
import numpy as np

seq_dir = "path/to/sequence"

frame_id = 0
event_window_ms = 50

# Load image timestamp.
timestamps = np.loadtxt(os.path.join(seq_dir, "timestamps.txt"), dtype=np.int64)
img_ts_us = timestamps[frame_id]  # exposure end timestamp of this image, in microseconds

# Load image frame.
img_path = os.path.join(seq_dir, "img_co_left", f"{frame_id:06d}.png")
img = cv2.imread(img_path, cv2.IMREAD_UNCHANGED)

# Load event file.
event_path = os.path.join(seq_dir, "events_co_left.h5")
with h5py.File(event_path, "r") as events:
    ms_to_idx = events["ms_to_idx"][:]

    # Select events within [img_ts_us - 50 ms, img_ts_us].
    start_us = img_ts_us - event_window_ms * 1000
    end_us = img_ts_us

    start_ms = int(round(start_us / 1000.0))
    end_ms = int(round(end_us / 1000.0))

    start_idx = ms_to_idx[start_ms]
    end_idx = ms_to_idx[end_ms]

    xs = events["x"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ys = events["y"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ps = events["p"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ts = events["t"][start_idx:end_idx]

# img and the selected event segment can then be used together.
```

### Timestamp Format

Each sequence provides a `timestamps.txt` file. Each line stores the timestamp of one image frame, and the order is consistent with the frame order in `img_co_left`.

The timestamp can be regarded as the exposure end timestamp of the corresponding image frame.

For example:

```text
img_co_left/000000.png  ->  first line in timestamps.txt
img_co_left/000001.png  ->  second line in timestamps.txt
img_co_left/000002.png  ->  third line in timestamps.txt
```

For the training set, the semantic segmentation labels share the same timestamps as the corresponding image frames.

The timestamps are stored as integer values in microseconds. They can be used to associate each image/label sample with a corresponding segment of the event stream.

### Semantic Segmentation Label Format

For the semantic segmentation task, each label is stored as a **single-channel PNG mask**. Each pixel value in the mask represents the semantic class ID of the corresponding pixel.

The valid class IDs range from `0` to `18`. Pixels with values outside this range are considered invalid during evaluation. The mask should be saved with an integer pixel data type. We recommend saving the mask as an **8-bit unsigned integer (`uint8`) PNG**, where each pixel stores the corresponding class ID directly.

| Class ID | Class Name |
| ---: | --- |
| 0 | road |
| 1 | sidewalk |
| 2 | building |
| 3 | wall |
| 4 | fence |
| 5 | pole |
| 6 | traffic light |
| 7 | traffic sign |
| 8 | vegetation |
| 9 | terrain |
| 10 | sky |
| 11 | person |
| 12 | rider |
| 13 | car |
| 14 | truck |
| 15 | bus |
| 16 | train |
| 17 | motorcycle |
| 18 | bicycle |

Please note that the PNG mask should store the **class IDs as raw pixel values**, not visualization colors. For example, a pixel value of `13` represents the class `car`, and a pixel value of `0` represents the class `road`.
pages.content_zh:
# 数据准备指南

为参与**基于事件的多模态感知挑战赛：语义分割赛道**，参赛者需要准备训练数据、理解数据格式，并在测试集发布后为其生成语义分割预测。

本赛道使用精选的**[CoSEC数据集](https://cosec.org.cn/)**子集进行基于事件的语义分割。每个序列包含多模态数据，包括图像帧、事件流、时间戳和用于训练的语义分割标注。

## 训练集下载

训练集包含图像帧、事件流、时间戳和真值语义分割标签。参赛者可以使用训练集来开发、训练和验证其语义分割模型。

训练分割包括：

- 左摄像头的图像帧。
- 左事件摄像头的事件流。
- 样本的时间戳。
- 真值语义分割标签。

请从以下链接下载官方训练集：

👉 **下载CoSEC分割训练集**

- **百度网盘**：[train.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1TeXpiWIRDxGcbjubGB1bCA?pwd=HUST)

- **谷歌云盘**：[train.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1V29ahlLQTm2O__I-VI_qloR9JkzGYmex/view?usp=drive_link)

## 测试集下载

官方测试集**尚未发布**。

测试集将根据挑战赛日程发布。发布后，下载链接将在本页面更新。

测试分割将包含图像帧、事件流和时间戳。测试集的真值语义分割标签将保持隐藏，仅由Codabench评估服务器使用。

测试集发布后，请从以下链接下载官方测试集：

👉 **下载CoSEC语义分割测试集**

- **百度网盘**：即将发布

- **谷歌云盘**：即将发布

## 数据集结构和格式

预期的数据集结构为：

```text
segmentation_challenge
├── train
│   ├── Day_Campus_000
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   ├── Day_Campus_001
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   └── ...
└── test
    ├── Day_Campus_012
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    ├── Day_Campus_013
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    └── ...
```

每个样本由其序列名称和帧文件名标识。具有相同文件名的图像帧和语义分割标签对应同一样本。

对于训练样本：

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

对应的事件数据存储在序列级HDF5文件中：

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/events_co_left.h5
```

时间戳存储为：

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/timestamps.txt
```

对于测试集发布后的测试样本：

```text
segmentation/test/Day_Campus_012/img_co_left/000000.png
```

期望的提交预测应为：

```text
Day_Campus_012/segment_co/000000.
```

### 图像帧格式

图像帧以`.png`文件形式存储在`img_co_left`文件夹下，分辨率为`1200 x 624`。它们已与事件流对齐，可用于多模态语义分割。

### 事件流格式

每个序列包含一个`events_co_left.h5`文件。事件流已与图像帧对齐。

典型的事件文件包含以下字段：

```text
x          # 事件x坐标
y          # 事件y坐标
p          # 事件极性
t          # 事件时间戳
ms_to_idx  # 从毫秒时间戳到事件索引的映射
```

事件时间戳

pages.title: Evaluation Details
pages.content:
# Evaluation Metrics & Submission Guidelines

## Submission Format

Participants are required to submit semantic segmentation predictions for all samples in the test set. Each prediction should be a pixel-level semantic label map corresponding to one test image frame.

The submission must be a single `.zip` archive. The archive should contain all predicted semantic segmentation masks while preserving the required sequence-level directory structure.

Each prediction file must satisfy the following requirements:

- The file extension must be `.png`.
- Each prediction file must contain a semantic segmentation label map.
- The predicted label map must be a **single-channel PNG mask**.
- The predicted label map must be saved as integer pixel values. We recommend using `uint8`.
- The shape of the predicted label map must match the corresponding ground-truth semantic segmentation label.
- The file name must be identical to the corresponding test sample name.
- The predicted label values must follow the official semantic class definitions.
- Valid label values are integers from `0` to `18`.
- Invalid label values outside the predefined class set will be treated as incorrect predictions.

## Semantic Label Format

The submitted semantic segmentation map should store pixel-level semantic class IDs directly.

Each pixel value represents its predicted semantic category according to the official class definition. For example, a pixel value of `13` represents the class `car`, while a pixel value of `0` represents the class `road`.

The prediction mask should store the **class IDs as raw pixel values**, not visualization colors. Participants should not submit colorized RGB visualization results as prediction masks.

The valid semantic classes are:

| Class ID | Class Name |
| ---: | --- |
| 0 | road |
| 1 | sidewalk |
| 2 | building |
| 3 | wall |
| 4 | fence |
| 5 | pole |
| 6 | traffic light |
| 7 | traffic sign |
| 8 | vegetation |
| 9 | terrain |
| 10 | sky |
| 11 | person |
| 12 | rider |
| 13 | car |
| 14 | truck |
| 15 | bus |
| 16 | train |
| 17 | motorcycle |
| 18 | bicycle |

## Submission Structure

An example submission structure is shown below:

```text
submission.zip
├── Day_Campus_000/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       ├── 000002.png
│       └── ...
├── Day_Campus_001/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       └── ...
└── ...
```

A dummy submission file is provided to help participants verify the required file structure and submission format.

👉 **Download Dummy Submission**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1Q8BdgnOfex4XX3JUFgS-JA?pwd=HUST)
- **Google Drive**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-KTZAXIKoWlOQsBBnyNZHebq_WJ-Juqe/view?usp=drive_link)

Participants may use the dummy submission as a reference when preparing their own prediction files.

For each test image frame, for example:

```text
test/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
```

the submitted prediction should be:

```text
Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

Please make sure that the submitted `.zip` file directly contains the sequence folders. Do not include unnecessary parent directories.

## How to Submit

1. **Prepare your predictions**: Generate semantic segmentation predictions for all test samples.
2. **Convert label format**: Convert model outputs to the required semantic label format according to the official class ID mapping.
3. **Save predictions**: Save each prediction as a single-channel `.png` mask with integer class IDs from `0` to `18`.
4. **Check your file structure**: Make sure the directory structure matches the required format. Each test sample should have exactly one corresponding prediction file.
5. **Compress your results**: Zip the sequence folders into a single `.zip` archive.
6. **Upload**: Navigate to the **My Submissions** tab on the challenge page, select the active phase, and upload your `.zip` file.
7. **Evaluation**: The evaluation server will automatically process your submission and update the leaderboard.

## Metrics

The challenge evaluates semantic segmentation performance on the test set. The evaluation server compares the submitted semantic segmentation predictions with the ground-truth semantic segmentation labels using standard semantic segmentation metrics.

The following metrics are reported on the leaderboard:

- **mIoU**: Mean Intersection over Union.
- **mAcc**: Mean Class Accuracy.
- **aAcc**: Overall Pixel Accuracy.

The main leaderboard ranking metric is **mIoU**, where higher is better.

## Final Scoring

The final leaderboard ranking is determined by **mIoU** on the test set.

Higher **mIoU** indicates better semantic segmentation performance. Other metrics, including **mAcc** and **aAcc**, are also reported for comprehensive comparison, but they are not used as the primary ranking criterion.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估指标与提交指南

## 提交格式

参赛者需要为测试集中的所有样本提交语义分割预测结果。每个预测应该是对应一个测试图像帧的像素级语义标签图。

提交文件必须是单个 `.zip` 压缩包。该压缩包应包含所有预测的语义分割掩码，同时保持所需的序列级目录结构。

每个预测文件必须满足以下要求：

- 文件扩展名必须为 `.png`。
- 每个预测文件必须包含语义分割标签图。
- 预测标签图必须是**单通道PNG掩码**。
- 预测标签图必须保存为整数像素值。建议使用 `uint8`。
- 预测标签图的尺寸必须与对应的真值语义分割标签相匹配。
- 文件名必须与对应的测试样本名称完全相同。
- 预测的标签值必须遵循官方语义类别定义。
- 有效的标签值为 `0` 到 `18` 之间的整数。
- 不在预定义类别集合中的无效标签值将被视为错误预测。

## 语义标签格式

提交的语义分割图应直接存储像素级语义类ID。

每个像素值根据官方类别定义代表其预测的语义类别。例如，像素值 `13` 表示类别 `汽车`，而像素值 `0` 表示类别 `道路`。

预测掩码应存储**原始像素值形式的类ID**，而非可视化颜色。参赛者不应提交彩色RGB可视化结果作为预测掩码。

有效的语义类别如下：

| 类别ID | 类别名称 |
| ---: | --- |
| 0 | 道路 |
| 1 | 人行道 |
| 2 | 建筑 |
| 3 | 墙体 |
| 4 | 栅栏 |
| 5 | 杆 |
| 6 | 交通灯 |
| 7 | 交通标志 |
| 8 | 植被 |
| 9 | 地形 |
| 10 | 天空 |
| 11 | 行人 |
| 12 | 骑行者 |
| 13 | 汽车 |
| 14 | 卡车 |
| 15 | 公交车 |
| 16 | 火车 |
| 17 | 摩托车 |
| 18 | 自行车 |

## 提交结构

下面展示了提交结构的示例：

```text
submission.zip
├── Day_Campus_000/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       ├── 000002.png
│       └── ...
├── Day_Campus_001/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       └── ...
└── ...
```

提供了一份虚拟提交文件，以帮助参赛者验证所需的文件结构和提交格式。

👉 **下载虚拟提交文件**

- **百度网盘**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1Q8BdgnOfex4XX3JUFgS-JA?pwd=HUST)
- **Google Drive**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-KTZAXIKoWlOQsBBnyNZHebq_WJ-Juqe/view?usp=drive_link)

参赛者可在准备自己的预测文件时将虚拟提交作为参考。

例如，对于每个测试图像帧：

```text
test/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
```

提交的预测应为：

```text
Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

请确保提交的 `.zip` 文件直接包含序列文件夹。不要包含不必要的父目录。

## 如何提交

1. **准备预测结果**：为所有测试样本生成语义分割预测。
2. **转换标签格式**：根据官方类ID映射，将模型输出转换为所需的语义标签格式。
3. **保存预测结果**：将每个预测保存为单通道 `.png` 掩码，类ID为 `0` 到 `18` 的整数值。
4. **检查文件结构**：确保目录结构与所需格式匹配。每个测试样本应恰好有一个对应的预测文件。
5. **压缩结果**：将序列文件夹压缩为单个 `.zip` 压缩包。
6. **上传**：导航到竞赛页面的**我的提交**标签页，选择活跃阶段，并上传你的 `.zip` 文件。

16218.description: This is the monocular depth estimation track of the Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge.

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Monocular Depth Estimation Track @ ECCV 2026

Welcome to the **Monocular Depth Estimation Track** of the **Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge**, officially hosted at **ECCV 2026**!

## 🎯 Overview & Objective

This challenge aims to advance **monocular depth estimation** using **event-based vision and frame data**. Unlike conventional RGB cameras, event cameras asynchronously capture brightness changes with high temporal resolution, low latency, and high dynamic range, making them particularly suitable for challenging scenarios such as high-speed motion, low-light environments, and scenes with strong illumination changes.

The objective of this track is to benchmark and promote robust depth estimation methods that can effectively leverage complementary information from **event streams and intensity frames**. Participants are expected to develop models that predict accurate depth maps from monocular multimodal inputs, with a particular focus on generalization, robustness, and metric depth estimation.

## 📝 Task Description

Participants are required to submit predicted depth maps for the test set. Each prediction should follow the official submission format and correspond to the provided event and frame input data.

The task focuses on:

- **Monocular Depth Estimation**: Predicting depth maps from a single viewpoint using event and frame data.
- **Multimodal Fusion**: Effectively combining asynchronous event streams with image frames for depth perception.
- **Absolute Depth Prediction**: Estimating metric depth values rather than only relative depth.
- **Robust Perception**: Handling challenging visual conditions such as low illumination, high dynamic range.

## 📅 Important Dates

All deadlines are at **11:59 PM UTC**, unless otherwise specified.

- **May 13, 2026**: Challenge Website Opens.
- **May 15, 2026**: Training Set Release.
- **June 20, 2026**: Test Set & Evaluation Server Online.
- **June 30, 2026**: Challenge Submission Deadline.
- **July 5, 2026**: Challenge Results Announcement.
- **July 10, 2026**: Technical Report Deadline.

## 📊 Dataset

This track uses a subset of the [**CoSEC dataset**](https://cosec.org.cn/). The dataset provides multimodal sensory data, including image frames, event streams, and depth annotations.

The released data for this track includes:

- **Image Frames**: RGB frames captured from a monocular camera.
- **Event Streams**: Asynchronous event data recorded by an event camera.
- **Depth Labels**: Ground-truth depth maps provided for supervised training and validation.

The training sets are released for model development and performance validation. The test set will be used for official evaluation, and its ground-truth depth labels will remain hidden.

The dataset statistics for this track are summarized below:

| Split | Number of Sequences | Number of Samples |
|---|---:|---:|
| Training | 102 | 11782 |
| Test | 26 | 2951 |

## ⚠️ Challenge Rules

To maintain fairness and scientific integrity, all participants must strictly follow the challenge rules:

- **No Test-Set Training**: The test set must not be used for training, validation, model selection, or manual tuning.
- **No Ground-Truth Probing**: Participants must not attempt to infer, reconstruct, or extract hidden ground-truth depth data from the evaluation server.
- **No Manual Annotation**: Submitted predictions must be generated by an automated method. Manually created, manually corrected, or manually tuned predictions are not allowed.
- **No Evaluation Server Exploitation**: Any attempt to exploit the evaluation system, submission feedback, or leaderboard behavior to gain an unfair advantage is strictly prohibited.
- **External Resources Disclosure**: Any external datasets, pretrained models, synthetic data, or additional training resources used by participants must be disclosed in the final technical report if requested.
- **Verification Requirement**: Top-ranked teams may be required to submit a short technical report, code, logs, or additional information for result verification.

## 📩 Contact

For questions about the challenge, please contact the organizers through the official competition page or by email.
pages.content_zh:
# 基于事件的多模态感知挑战赛：单目深度估计赛道 @ ECCV 2026

欢迎参加**基于事件的多模态感知挑战赛**的**单目深度估计赛道**，该赛事官方举办于**ECCV 2026**！

## 🎯 概述与目标

本挑战赛旨在利用**事件相机视觉和帧数据**推进**单目深度估计**技术。与传统RGB相机不同，事件相机可以异步捕捉亮度变化，具有高时间分辨率、低延迟和高动态范围等特点，特别适合处理高速运动、低光环境和强照明变化等复杂场景。

本赛道的目标是对能够有效利用**事件流和强度帧**互补信息的鲁棒深度估计方法进行基准测试和推广。参赛者需要开发模型，能从单目多模态输入预测精准的深度图，特别关注泛化性、鲁棒性和绝对深度估计。

## 📝 任务描述

参赛者需要提交测试集的深度图预测结果。每个预测应遵循官方提交格式，并对应提供的事件和帧输入数据。

任务重点包括：

- **单目深度估计**：使用事件和帧数据从单个视角预测深度图。
- **多模态融合**：有效结合异步事件流与图像帧用于深度感知。
- **绝对深度预测**：估计度量深度值而非仅相对深度。
- **鲁棒感知**：处理低照度、高动态范围等复杂视觉条件。

## 📅 重要日期

所有截止时间为**世界协调时(UTC)晚上11:59**，另有说明除外。

- **2026年5月13日**：挑战赛网站开放。
- **2026年5月15日**：训练集发布。
- **2026年6月20日**：测试集与评估服务器上线。
- **2026年6月30日**：挑战赛提交截止。
- **2026年7月5日**：挑战赛结果公布。
- **2026年7月10日**：技术报告截止。

## 📊 数据集

本赛道采用[**CoSEC数据集**](https://cosec.org.cn/)的子集。该数据集提供多模态传感数据，包括图像帧、事件流和深度标注。

本赛道发布的数据包括：

- **图像帧**：来自单目相机的RGB帧。
- **事件流**：事件相机记录的异步事件数据。
- **深度标签**：用于监督训练和验证的真实深度图。

发布的训练集用于模型开发和性能验证。测试集用于官方评估，其真实深度标签将保持隐藏。

本赛道的数据集统计信息如下表所示：

| 划分 | 序列数 | 样本数 |
|---|---:|---:|
| 训练集 | 102 | 11782 |
| 测试集 | 26 | 2951 |

## ⚠️ 挑战赛规则

为维护公平性和科学诚信，所有参赛者必须严格遵守挑战赛规则：

- **禁止用测试集训练**：测试集不得用于训练、验证、模型选择或手动调参。
- **禁止探测真实标签**：参赛者不得尝试从评估服务器推断、重构或提取隐藏的真实深度数据。
- **禁止手动标注**：提交的预测必须由自动化方法生成。禁止手动创建、手动修正或手动调优的预测。
- **禁止利用评估服务器**：严禁任何利用评估系统、提交反馈或排行榜行为获得不公平优势的尝试。
- **外部资源披露**：参赛者使用的任何外部数据集、预训练模型、合成数据或其他额外训练资源必须在最终技术报告中披露（如要求）。
- **验证要求**：排名靠前的团队可能需要

pages.title: Data Preparation
pages.content:
# Data Preparation Guide

To participate in the **Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Monocular Depth Estimation Track**, participants need to prepare the training data, understand the data format, and generate depth predictions for the test set once it is released.

This track uses a curated subset of the [**CoSEC dataset**](https://cosec.org.cn/) for event-based monocular depth estimation. Each sequence contains multimodal data, including image frames, event streams, camera intrinsics, timestamps, and depth annotations.

## Training Set Download

The training set contains image frames, event streams, camera intrinsics, timestamps, and ground-truth depth maps. Participants may use the training set to develop, train, and validate their monocular depth estimation models.

The training split includes:

- Image frames from the left camera.
- Event streams from the left event camera.
- Camera intrinsic parameters.
- Timestamps for samples.
- Ground-truth depth maps.

Please download the official training set from the following links:

👉 **Download CoSEC Depth Training Set**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: [train.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1MhphyA-iaNJ7HbdpqqNtJA?pwd=HUST)

- **Google Drive**: [train.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1R8q_h6jVUA27Q7QC2qUHxySMYqFGwH2P/view?usp=drive_link)

## Test Set Download

The official test set has **not been released yet**.

The test set will be released according to the challenge schedule. Once released, the download links will be updated on this page.

The test split will contain image frames, event streams, camera intrinsics, and timestamps. The ground-truth depth maps for the test set will remain hidden and will be used only by the Codabench evaluation server.

Please download the official test set from the following links once it is released:

👉 **Download CoSEC Depth Test Set**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: To be released

- **Google Drive**: To be released

## Dataset Structure and Format

The expected dataset structure is:

```text
depth_challenge
├── train
│   ├── Day_Campus_000
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── depth_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   ├── intrinsics.json
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   ├── Day_Campus_001
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── depth_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   ├── intrinsics.json
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   └── ...
└── test
    ├── Day_Campus_012
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   ├── intrinsics.json
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    ├── Day_Campus_013
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   ├── intrinsics.json
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    └── ...
```

Each sample is identified by its sequence name and frame file name. The image frame and depth map with the same file name correspond to the same sample.

For a training sample:

```text
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/depth_co/000000.png
```

The corresponding event data is stored in the sequence-level HDF5 file:

```text
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/events_co_left.h5
```

The camera intrinsics and timestamps are stored as:

```text
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/intrinsics.json
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/timestamps.txt
```

For a test sample after the test set is released:

```text
depth/test/Day_Campus_012/img_co_left/000000.png
```

the expected submitted prediction should be:

```text
Day_Campus_012/depth_co/000000.npz
```

### Image Frame Format

Image frames are stored as `.png` files under the `img_co_left` folder, with a resolution of `1200 x 624`. They have been aligned with the event streams and can be used together with the event data for multimodal depth estimation.

### Event Stream Format

Each sequence contains one `events_co_left.h5` file. The event stream has already been aligned with the image frames.

A typical event file contains the following fields:

```text
x          # event x coordinates
y          # event y coordinates
p          # event polarities
t          # event timestamps
ms_to_idx  # mapping from millisecond timestamp to event index
```

The event timestamps `t` are stored in microseconds. The `ms_to_idx` array provides a convenient way to quickly locate event indices by millisecond-level timestamps.

For a given image frame, participants may use the corresponding timestamp in `timestamps.txt` to extract a temporal window of events. For example, one can take the events within `50 ms` before the image exposure end timestamp and use them together with the image frame for multimodal depth estimation.

Example code:

```python
import os
import cv2
import h5py
import numpy as np

seq_dir = "path/to/sequence"

frame_id = 0
event_window_ms = 50

# Load image timestamp.
timestamps = np.loadtxt(os.path.join(seq_dir, "timestamps.txt"), dtype=np.int64)
img_ts_us = timestamps[frame_id]  # exposure end timestamp of this image, in microseconds

# Load image frame.
img_path = os.path.join(seq_dir, "img_co_left", f"{frame_id:06d}.png")
img = cv2.imread(img_path, cv2.IMREAD_UNCHANGED)

# Load event file.
event_path = os.path.join(seq_dir, "events_co_left.h5")
with h5py.File(event_path, "r") as events:
    ms_to_idx = events["ms_to_idx"][:]

    # Select events within [img_ts_us - 50 ms, img_ts_us].
    start_us = img_ts_us - event_window_ms * 1000
    end_us = img_ts_us

    start_ms = int(round(start_us / 1000.0))
    end_ms = int(round(end_us / 1000.0))

    start_idx = ms_to_idx[start_ms]
    end_idx = ms_to_idx[end_ms]

    xs = events["x"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ys = events["y"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ps = events["p"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ts = events["t"][start_idx:end_idx]

# img and the selected event segment can then be used together.
```

### Timestamp Format

Each sequence provides a `timestamps.txt` file. Each line stores the timestamp of one image frame, and the order is consistent with the frame order in `img_co_left`.

The timestamp can be regarded as the exposure end timestamp of the corresponding image frame.

For example:

```text
img_co_left/000000.png  ->  first line in timestamps.txt
img_co_left/000001.png  ->  second line in timestamps.txt
img_co_left/000002.png  ->  third line in timestamps.txt
```

For the training set, the depth maps in `depth_co` share the same timestamps as the corresponding image frames.

The timestamps are stored as integer values in microseconds. They can be used to associate each image/depth sample with a corresponding segment of the event stream.

### Camera Intrinsics

Camera intrinsic parameters are provided in the `intrinsics.json` file of each sequence.

Since the image frames and event streams have already been aligned, both the image camera and the event camera use the `Co_Rect_L` intrinsic parameters. The format is shown below:

```json
"Co_Rect_L":
{
    "K":
    [
        [ fx,  0,   cx ],
        [ 0,   fy,  cy ],
        [ 0,   0,   1  ]
    ],

    "resolution": [ height, width ]
}
```

Here, `K` is the camera intrinsic matrix, where `fx` and `fy` are the focal lengths, and `cx` and `cy` are the principal point coordinates. The `resolution` field is given in `[height, width]` order, which is `[624, 1200]` for the provided image frames and event streams.

### Depth Map Format

Ground-truth depth maps in the training set are stored as `.png` files under the `depth_co` folder.

The depth values are stored as **uint16 scaled depth**.

Specifically, if the metric depth is represented in meters, the stored value follows:

```text
stored_depth = metric_depth_in_meters * 256
```

To recover the metric depth in meters:

```text
metric_depth_in_meters = stored_depth / 256.0
```
pages.content_zh:
# 数据准备指南

要参与**基于事件的多模态感知挑战赛：单目深度估计赛道**，参赛者需要准备训练数据、理解数据格式，并在测试集发布后为其生成深度预测。

本赛道使用**CoSEC数据集**的精选子集进行基于事件的单目深度估计。每个序列包含多模态数据，包括图像帧、事件流、相机内参、时间戳和深度标注。

## 训练集下载

训练集包含图像帧、事件流、相机内参、时间戳和真实深度图。参赛者可使用训练集开发、训练和验证单目深度估计模型。

训练集包括：

- 左侧相机的图像帧
- 左侧事件相机的事件流
- 相机内参
- 样本时间戳
- 真实深度图

请从以下链接下载官方训练集：

👉 **下载CoSEC深度估计训练集**

- **百度网盘**：[train.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1MhphyA-iaNJ7HbdpqqNtJA?pwd=HUST)

- **Google Drive**：[train.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1R8q_h6jVUA27Q7QC2qUHxySMYqFGwH2P/view?usp=drive_link)

## 测试集下载

官方测试集**尚未发布**。

测试集将根据挑战赛日程发布。发布后，下载链接将在本页面更新。

测试集将包含图像帧、事件流、相机内参和时间戳。测试集的真实深度图将保密，仅由Codabench评估服务器使用。

测试集发布后，请从以下链接下载官方测试集：

👉 **下载CoSEC深度估计测试集**

- **百度网盘**：待发布

- **Google Drive**：待发布

## 数据集结构与格式

预期的数据集结构如下：

```text
depth_challenge
├── train
│   ├── Day_Campus_000
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── depth_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   ├── intrinsics.json
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   ├── Day_Campus_001
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── depth_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   ├── intrinsics.json
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   └── ...
└── test
    ├── Day_Campus_012
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   ├── intrinsics.json
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    ├── Day_Campus_013
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   ├── intrinsics.json
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    └── ...
```

每个样本由其序列名和帧文件名标识。具有相同文件名的图像帧和深度图对应同一样本。

对于训练样本：

```text
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/depth_co/000000.png
```

相应的事件数据存储在序列级HDF5文件中：

```text
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/events_co_left.h5
```

相机内参和时间戳存储为：

```text
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/intrinsics.json
depth/train/Day_Campus_000/timestamps.txt
```

对于测试集发布后的测试样本：

```text
depth/test/Day_Campus_012/img_co_left/000000.png
```

预期的提交预测应为：

```text
Day_Campus_012/depth_co/000000.npz
```

### 图像帧格式

图像帧以`.png`文件格式存储在`img_co_left`文件夹中，分辨率为`1200 x 624`。它们已与事件流对齐，可与事件数据一起用于多模态深度估计。

### 事件流格式

每个序列包含一个`events_co_left.h5`文件。事件流已与图像帧对齐。

典型的事件文件包含以下字段：

```text
x          # 事件x坐标
y          # 事件y坐标
p          # 事件极性
t          # 事件时间戳
```

pages.title: Evaluation Details
pages.content:
# Evaluation Metrics & Submission Guidelines

## Submission Format

Participants are required to submit depth predictions for all samples in the test set. Each prediction should be saved as a `.npz` file containing a single depth array.

The submission must be a single `.zip` archive. The archive should contain all predicted depth files while preserving the required sequence-level directory structure.

Each `.npz` prediction file must satisfy the following requirements:

- The file extension must be `.npz`.
- Each `.npz` file must contain a depth array with the key `depth`.
- The `depth` array must be a 2D array.
- The shape of the predicted depth array must match the corresponding ground-truth depth map.
- The file name must be identical to the corresponding test sample name.
- The depth values must be saved in the same format as the ground-truth depth labels.
- Invalid values such as `NaN`, `Inf`, or negative depth values are not allowed.

### Depth Value Format

The submitted depth map should be stored as **uint16 scaled depth**.

Specifically, if the predicted metric depth is `depth_m` in meters, the submitted value should be:

```text
depth_uint16 = depth_m * 256
```

The resulting depth map should be saved as a `uint16` array in the `.npz` file.

During evaluation, the server will recover the metric depth by:

```text
depth_m = depth_uint16 / 256.0
```

Therefore, participants should not directly save floating-point depth values in meters. Please first convert the prediction to the required scaled `uint16` format.

Each `.npz` file must contain exactly one depth array with the key:

```text
depth
```

We recommend saving the prediction using `np.savez_compressed`, with the scaled `uint16` depth map stored under the key `depth`.

### Submission Structure

An example submission structure is shown below:

```text
submission.zip
├── Day_Campus_000
│   └── depth_co
│       ├── 000000.npz
│       ├── 000001.npz
│       ├── 000002.npz
│       └── ...
├── Day_Campus_001
│   └── depth_co
│       ├── 000000.npz
│       ├── 000001.npz
│       └── ...
└── ...
```

A dummy submission file is provided to help participants verify the required file structure and submission format.

👉 **Download Dummy Submission**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1lPezWP_CpMIiEEORrsGExA?pwd=HUST)  

- **Google Drive**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1w4BouQGbzcXs4rrG-BQl9xqxWWMuGyCo/view?usp=drive_link)

Participants may use the dummy submission as a reference when preparing their own prediction files. 

For each test image frame, for example:

```text
test/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
```

the submitted prediction should be:

```text
Day_Campus_000/depth_co/000000.npz
```

Please make sure that the submitted `.zip` file directly contains the sequence folders. Do not include unnecessary parent directories.

## How to Submit

1. **Prepare your predictions**: Generate depth predictions for all test samples.
2. **Convert depth format**: Convert metric depth in meters to scaled `uint16` format using `depth_uint16 = depth_m * 256`.
3. **Save as `.npz`**: Save each prediction as a `.npz` file with the key `depth`.
4. **Check your file structure**: Make sure the directory structure matches the required format. Each test sample should have exactly one corresponding `.npz` prediction file.
5. **Compress your results**: Zip the prediction folders into a single `.zip` archive.
6. **Upload**: Navigate to the **My Submissions** tab on the challenge page, select the active phase, and upload your `.zip` file.
7. **Evaluation**: The evaluation server will automatically process your submission and update the leaderboard.

## Metrics

The challenge evaluates monocular depth estimation performance on the test set. The evaluation server compares the submitted depth predictions with the ground-truth depth maps using standard depth estimation metrics.

The following metrics are reported on the leaderboard:

- **RMSE**: Root Mean Squared Error.
- **AbsRel**: Absolute Relative Error.
- **δ < 1.25**: Percentage of valid pixels whose prediction is within a threshold of 1.25.
- **δ < 1.25²**: Percentage of valid pixels whose prediction is within a threshold of 1.25².
- **δ < 1.25³**: Percentage of valid pixels whose prediction is within a threshold of 1.25³.

The main leaderboard ranking metric is **RMSE**, where lower is better.

## Final Scoring

The final leaderboard ranking is determined by **RMSE** on the test set.

Lower **RMSE** indicates better depth estimation performance. Other metrics, including **AbsRel**, **δ < 1.25**, **δ < 1.25²**, and **δ < 1.25³**, are also reported for comprehensive comparison, but they are not used as the primary ranking criterion.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估指标与提交指南

## 提交格式

参赛者需要为测试集中的所有样本提交深度预测。每个预测应保存为包含单个深度数组的 `.npz` 文件。

提交物必须是单个 `.zip` 压缩包。压缩包应包含所有预测的深度文件，同时保留所需的序列级目录结构。

每个 `.npz` 预测文件必须满足以下要求：

- 文件扩展名必须为 `.npz`。
- 每个 `.npz` 文件必须包含一个键为 `depth` 的深度数组。
- `depth` 数组必须是二维数组。
- 预测深度数组的形状必须与对应的地面真值深度图匹配。
- 文件名必须与对应的测试样本名称完全相同。
- 深度值必须以与地面真值深度标签相同的格式保存。
- 不允许包含 `NaN`、`Inf` 或负深度值等无效值。

### 深度值格式

提交的深度图应存储为 **uint16 缩放深度**。

具体而言，如果预测的公制深度为 `depth_m`（单位：米），提交的值应为：

```text
depth_uint16 = depth_m * 256
```

生成的深度图应在 `.npz` 文件中保存为 `uint16` 数组。

评估期间，服务器将通过以下方式恢复公制深度：

```text
depth_m = depth_uint16 / 256.0
```

因此，参赛者不应直接保存浮点格式的米制深度值。请先将预测转换为所需的缩放 `uint16` 格式。

每个 `.npz` 文件必须恰好包含一个键为以下值的深度数组：

```text
depth
```

建议使用 `np.savez_compressed` 保存预测，将缩放后的 `uint16` 深度图存储在键 `depth` 下。

### 提交结构

提交结构示例如下：

```text
submission.zip
├── Day_Campus_000
│   └── depth_co
│       ├── 000000.npz
│       ├── 000001.npz
│       ├── 000002.npz
│       └── ...
├── Day_Campus_001
│   └── depth_co
│       ├── 000000.npz
│       ├── 000001.npz
│       └── ...
└── ...
```

提供了虚拟提交文件，以帮助参赛者验证所需的文件结构和提交格式。

👉 **下载虚拟提交文件**

- **百度网盘**：[dummy_submission.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1lPezWP_CpMIiEEORrsGExA?pwd=HUST)

- **谷歌云盘**：[dummy_submission.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1w4BouQGbzcXs4rrG-BQl9xqxWWMuGyCo/view?usp=drive_link)

参赛者可参考虚拟提交文件准备自己的预测文件。

对于每个测试图像帧，例如：

```text
test/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
```

提交的预测应为：

```text
Day_Campus_000/depth_co/000000.npz
```

请确保提交的 `.zip` 文件直接包含序列文件夹，不包含不必要的父目录。

## 如何提交

1. **准备预测**：为所有测试样本生成深度预测。
2. **转换深度格式**：使用 `depth_uint16 = depth_m * 256` 将米制深度转换为缩放的 `uint16` 格式。
3. **保存为 `.npz`**：将每个预测保存为 `.npz` 文件，键为 `depth`。
4. **检查文件结构**：确保目录结构符合所需格式。每个测试样本应恰好对应一个 `.npz` 预测文件。
5. **压缩结果**：将预测文件夹压缩为单个 `.zip` 压缩包。
6. **上传**：在挑战页面导航至**我的提交**选项卡，选择活跃阶段，并上传 `.zip` 文件。
7. **评估**：评估服务器将自动处理您的提交并更新排行榜。

## 评估指标

本挑战在测试集上评估单目深度估计性能。评估服务器使用标准深度估计指标将提交的深度预测与地面真值深度图进行比较。

15662.title: MVAA 2026: Mitral Valve Anatomy Analysis Using Multimodal Imaging Data
15662.description:
MVAA 2026 evaluates multimodal AI methods for mitral valve anatomy analysis across
three clinically grounded segmentation tasks: (1) CT segmentation,
(2) 3D TEE segmentation, and (3) surgical video segmentation.

Final ranking is based on normalized task-level scores aggregated equally across tasks.
15662.description_zh:
MVAA 2026 评估跨越三个临床基础分割任务的二尖瓣解剖学多模态AI方法：（1）CT分割、（2）3D TEE分割和（3）手术视频分割。

最终排名基于跨任务平均聚合的归一化任务级分数。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Mitral Valve Anatomy Analysis Using Multimodal Imaging Data ## MICCAI 2026 Challenge - International Workshop on Medical World Models (MWM)
Mitral valve assessment plays a central role in the diagnosis, treatment planning, and intraoperative guidance of structural heart disease. In clinical practice, physicians rely on multimodal imaging data with very different characteristics, including cardiac CT for pre-procedural geometry, 3D transesophageal echocardiography for intraoperative anatomical reference, and selected intraoperative frames for direct procedural observation. However, manual analysis across these modalities remains labor-intensive and subject to inter-observer variability. The MVAA challenge aims to advance automated mitral valve analysis by providing a unified benchmark across multiple imaging modalities and surgical stages. Participants are encouraged to develop robust methods for segmentation and anatomy analysis that remain consistent across heterogeneous data sources and support clinically meaningful downstream use. ## Aim of the Challenge To develop automated methods for multimodal mitral valve anatomy analysis across CT, 3D TEE, and intraoperative image frames, with evaluation focused on segmentation quality, structural consistency, and clinical relevance. **Objectives:** - To establish a multimodal benchmark for mitral valve analysis spanning pre-operative, intraoperative, and procedural imaging data. - To evaluate algorithms for accurate segmentation and anatomy understanding under diverse image characteristics and clinical conditions. - To encourage models that generalize across modalities while preserving coherent structural representations of the mitral valve. - To promote collaboration between AI researchers, imaging scientists, and clinical experts in cardiac intervention. ## Resources - **Challenge website:** https://mwm2026.github.io/mvaa - **Dataset (Training & Validation) - Link 1:** Full data for Task 1 and Task 2, plus the labeled data for Task 3: https://drive.google.com/file/d/14WneBUBZ1X4p69tRdRzximNb0IsWuh2B/view?usp=sharing - **Dataset (Training & Validation) - Link 2:** Unlabeled data for Task 3: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1K4vFo-4pdL1_xt-8msO-32bc2aEdJ7wS/view?usp=sharing - **Baseline code:** https://github.com/db0725/MVAA - **Contact:** dbd2002@foxmail.com
pages.content_zh:
# 使用多模态影像数据的二尖瓣解剖分析 ## MICCAI 2026挑战赛 - 医学世界模型国际研讨会(MWM)
二尖瓣评估在结构性心脏病的诊断、治疗规划和术中引导中起着核心作用。在临床实践中，医生依赖具有非常不同特征的多模态影像数据，包括用于术前几何结构的心脏CT、用于术中解剖参考的3D经食管超声心动图，以及用于直接手术观察的选定术中帧。然而，跨这些模态的手动分析仍然劳动密集且受观察者间变异性的影响。 MVAA挑战赛旨在通过提供跨多种影像模态和手术阶段的统一基准来推进自动化二尖瓣分析。鼓励参赛者开发稳健的分割和解剖分析方法，这些方法在异构数据源上保持一致，并支持临床意义的下游应用。 ## 挑战赛目标 开发用于跨CT、3D TEE和术中图像帧的多模态二尖瓣解剖分析的自动化方法，评估重点关注分割质量、结构一致性和临床相关性。 **具体目标：** - 建立涵盖术前、术中和手术影像数据的二尖瓣分析多模态基准。 - 评估算法在多样化影像特征和临床条件下的准确分割和解剖理解能力。 - 鼓励开发在模态间具有良好泛化性能同时保留二尖瓣连贯结构表示的模型。 - 促进AI研究人员、影像科学家和心脏介入临床专家之间的协作。 ## 资源 - **挑战赛网站:** https://mwm2026.github.io/mvaa - **数据集(训练与验证) - 链接1:** 任务1和任务2的完整数据，加上任务3的标注数据: https://drive.google.com/file/d/14WneBUBZ1X4p69tRdRzximNb0IsWuh2B/view?usp=sharing - **数据集(训练与验证) - 链接2:** 任务3的未标注数据: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1K4vFo-4pdL1_xt-8msO-32bc2aEdJ7wS/view?usp=sharing - **基线代码:** https://github.com/db0725/MVAA - **联系方式:** dbd2002@foxmail.com

* **Password (提取码):** `3ymi`
* **Note:** The folder contains a total of **6 compressed files**. If the page displays a violation warning or any compressed files are missing, please use the GoogleDrive links below instead.

### Option 2: GoogleDrive (Must download BOTH links below)
If you choose to use GoogleDrive, you **must download both files** from the links below to get the complete dataset:
* **Link 1:** [https://drive.google.com/file/d/14WneBUBZ1X4p69tRdRzximNb0IsWuh2B/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/14WneBUBZ1X4p69tRdRzximNb0IsWuh2B/view?usp=sharing)

### Task 3 Training Annotation Note
Please note that one Task 3 video training sample may contain an annotation issue:

`t3_vid/train/REC_20250322_101917_746A/REC_20250322_101917_746A_000130.png`

In this sample, the label includes not only the mitral valve region but also part of the hollow cardiac chamber. Participants may choose to exclude this single training sample during model training or data cleaning. This issue is limited to the training set sample listed above; the validation set and test set annotations are not affected.

### Official Baseline Model
Participants can access the official baseline and documentation via the following link (The model saving method of the baseline will not be used for final result evaluation; the final evaluation shall be subject to the official platform.):
