16281.title: EBMV 2026 LOW-LEVEL IMAGING CHALLENGE: EVENT-GUIDED LOW-LIGHT VIDEO DEPTH ESTIMATION ON SDE DATASET
16281.description:
This challenge focuses on event-guided low-light video depth estimation on the SDE dataset. Participants are asked to develop algorithms that estimate accurate depth maps from low-light video sequences with the assistance of event-camera signals.

The goal is to promote robust depth estimation under challenging illumination conditions, where conventional frame-based methods often suffer from motion blur, low signal-to-noise ratio, and poor texture visibility. Submissions will be evaluated using quantitative depth-estimation metrics on a hidden test set.

The challenge is organized as part of EBMV 2026 and is intended for researchers working on low-level vision, event-based vision, video enhancement, and monocular or multi-modal depth estimation.
16281.description_zh:
本挑战赛重点关注SDE数据集上的事件引导低光视频深度估计。参赛者需要开发算法，在事件相机信号的辅助下，从低光视频序列中估计准确的深度图。

该挑战赛的目标是促进在具有挑战性的光照条件下的鲁棒深度估计，在这些条件下，传统的基于帧的方法通常会受到运动模糊、低信噪比和纹理可见性差的影响。提交的方案将使用隐藏测试集上的定量深度估计指标进行评估。

本挑战赛作为EBMV 2026的一部分组织举办，面向从事低级视觉、基于事件的视觉、视频增强以及单目或多模态深度估计研究的科研人员。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
EBMV 2026 Low-Level Imaging Challenge

Event-Guided Low-Light Video Depth Estimation on the SDE Dataset

This challenge focuses on event-guided video depth estimation in low-light scenes. Participants are asked to use low-light RGB frames together with event streams to predict dense depth maps for each scene.

The benchmark is organized at the scene/video level so that temporal consistency is preserved across frames.

Task

Given a low-light video sequence and the corresponding event camera data, participants should estimate an aligned dense depth sequence for each target scene.

Keywords

  • Event camera
  • Low-light video
  • Video depth estimation
  • Depth estimation
  • Low-level imaging
pages.content_zh:
EBMV 2026 低光成像挑战赛

事件引导的低光视频深度估计——基于SDE数据集

本挑战赛聚焦于低光场景下的事件引导视频深度估计。 参赛者需要结合低光RGB帧和事件流来预测每个场景的稠密深度图。

基准在场景/视频级别组织，以保持帧间的时间一致性。

任务

给定低光视频序列及其对应的事件相机数据，参赛者应为每个目标场景估计一个配准的稠密深度序列。

关键词

  • 事件相机
  • 低光视频
  • 视频深度估计
  • 深度估计
  • 低级成像

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
Dataset

The competition dataset is a workshop-aligned version of the DVD event-guided video depth estimation data. It packages each scene into a canonical folder tree that aligns low-light RGB frames, event slices, scene-level event streams, and depth supervision.

Dataset Download

The dataset is hosted on Hugging Face:

https://huggingface.co/datasets/Ethanliang99/ECCV_Event_Video_Depth_Estimation/tree/main

Dataset Structure

The official split is encoded directly in the top-level directory structure: 

train/
  <scene_name>/
    low/
    normal/
val/
  <scene_name>/
    low/
    normal/
test/
  <scene_name>/
    low/
    normal/

Each scene directory contains aligned low-light inputs and depth targets. A manifest.json file is also provided with release and split metadata.

Scene Layout

<scene_name>/
  low/
    <timestamp>.png
    <timestamp>.npz
    lowlight_event.npz
  normal/
    <timestamp>.png
    depth.npz
    manifest.json

Input

  • Low-light RGB frames under low/
  • Per-frame event slices under low/
  • Scene-level event stream stored as lowlight_event.npz

Output / Supervision

  • Aligned depth sequence stored as normal/depth.npz

Important Notes

  • The dataset is split by scene/video count, not by frame count.
  • The folder name itself indicates the split assignment.
  • The event slices in the workshop version are trimmed to avoid double-counting overlapping events.
  • Please keep the scene-level temporal ordering intact when training or evaluating temporal models.
pages.content_zh:
数据集

竞赛数据集是与研讨会对齐的DVD事件引导视频深度估计数据的版本。 它将每个场景打包到规范的文件夹树中，对齐低光RGB帧、事件切片、场景级事件流和深度监督。

数据集下载

数据集托管在Hugging Face上：

https://huggingface.co/datasets/Ethanliang99/ECCV_Event_Video_Depth_Estimation/tree/main

数据集结构

官方划分直接编码在顶级目录结构中： 

train/
  <scene_name>/
    low/
    normal/
val/
  <scene_name>/
    low/
    normal/
test/
  <scene_name>/
    low/
    normal/

每个场景目录包含对齐的低光输入和深度目标。还提供了manifest.json文件，包含发布和划分元数据。

场景布局

<scene_name>/
  low/
    <timestamp>.png
    <timestamp>.npz
    lowlight_event.npz
  normal/
    <timestamp>.png
    depth.npz
    manifest.json

输入

  • low/下的低光RGB帧
  • low/下的逐帧事件切片
  • 存储为lowlight_event.npz的场景级事件流

输出/监督

  • 存储为normal/depth.npz的对齐深度序列

重要说明

  • 数据集按场景/视频数量划分，而不是按帧数划分。
  • 文件夹名称本身指示划分分配。
  • 研讨会版本中的事件切片已修剪，以避免重复计算重叠事件。
  • 在训练或评估时间模型时，请保持场景级时间顺序完整。

pages.title: Submission
pages.content:
Submission Format

Participants should submit one predicted depth sequence for each test scene. The predicted depth should be aligned with the frame order of the corresponding scene.

Recommended Layout

submission/
  <scene_name>/
    normal/
      depth.npz

Depth File Format

Each depth.npz file should contain the predicted depth array for the corresponding scene. A 4D tensor with shape [T, H, W, 1] is preferred for compatibility with the existing DVD-style tooling. A 3D tensor with shape [T, H, W] may also be supported by the evaluation script.

Submission Requirements

  • Submit predictions for all required test scenes.
  • Preserve the original scene names.
  • Preserve the temporal frame order within each scene.
  • Do not include ground-truth files in the submission.
  • Compress the submission/ folder into a zip file before uploading.
pages.content_zh:
提交格式

参赛者应为每个测试场景提交一个预测深度序列。 预测深度应与相应场景的帧顺序保持对齐。

推荐目录结构

submission/
  <scene_name>/
    normal/
      depth.npz

深度文件格式

每个depth.npz文件应包含相应场景的预测深度数组。 建议使用形状为[T, H, W, 1]的4D张量，以兼容现有的DVD风格工具。 评估脚本也可能支持形状为[T, H, W]的3D张量。

提交要求

  • 提交所有必需测试场景的预测结果。
  • 保持原始场景名称。
  • 保持每个场景内的时间帧顺序。
  • 不要在提交物中包含真实标签文件。
  • 在上传前将submission/文件夹压缩为zip文件。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
Evaluation

Submissions will be evaluated by comparing the predicted depth sequences with the hidden ground-truth depth annotations.

Metrics

The following metrics will be reported:

  • AbsRel / abs_relative_difference
  • RMSE / rmse_linear
  • delta1 / delta1_acc
  • delta2 / delta2_acc
  • delta3 / delta3_acc
  • SILog / silog_rmse

Ranking Metric

The final ranking metric will be announced before the official evaluation phase. For launch, we recommend using AbsRel as the primary ranking metric and reporting the remaining metrics as diagnostics.

Evaluation Protocol

  • Predictions are evaluated scene by scene.
  • Each predicted depth sequence should be temporally aligned with the corresponding input sequence.
  • Invalid or missing predictions may lead to evaluation failure or a penalty.
pages.content_zh:
评估

将通过将预测的深度序列与隐藏的地面实况深度标注进行比较来评估提交。

指标

将报告以下指标：

  • AbsRel / abs_relative_difference（绝对相对差异）
  • RMSE / rmse_linear（均方根误差）
  • delta1 / delta1_acc
  • delta2 / delta2_acc
  • delta3 / delta3_acc
  • SILog / silog_rmse（尺度不变对数RMSE）

排名指标

最终排名指标将在官方评估阶段前宣布。 在启动阶段，我们建议使用 AbsRel 作为主要排名指标，并将其余指标作为诊断指标进行报告。

评估协议

  • 按场景逐个评估预测。
  • 每个预测的深度序列应与对应的输入序列在时间上对齐。
  • 无效或缺失的预测可能导致评估失败或受到惩罚。

pages.title: Rules
pages.content:
Rules and Terms

General Rules

  • Participants should use the official training data and any additional resources explicitly allowed by the organizers.
  • Participants must not inspect, annotate, or reverse-engineer hidden test labels.
  • Participants should preserve scene-level temporal ordering when producing predictions.
  • Each team should submit only valid prediction files following the required submission format.

External Data

The policy for external data and pretrained models will be announced before the official challenge launch.

Submission Quota

Submission quota: TBD.

Timeline

  • Development phase: TBD
  • Validation phase: TBD
  • Final test phase: TBD
  • Winner announcement: TBD

License and Terms

  • Dataset license: TBD
  • Code and baseline license: TBD
  • By participating, teams agree to the competition rules and the platform terms.
  • The organizers may update the submission format or evaluation script with prior notice.
pages.content_zh:
规则和条款

总体规则

  • 参赛者应使用官方训练数据和组织者明确允许的任何其他资源。
  • 参赛者不得检查、标注或反向工程隐藏的测试标签。
  • 参赛者在生成预测时应保持场景级时间顺序。
  • 每支队伍应仅提交遵循规定提交格式的有效预测文件。

外部数据

外部数据和预训练模型的相关政策将在正式竞赛启动前公布。

提交配额

提交配额：待定。

时间安排

  • 开发阶段：待定
  • 验证阶段：待定
  • 最终测试阶段：待定
  • 获奖者宣布：待定

许可证和条款

  • 数据集许可证：待定
  • 代码和基线许可证：待定
  • 参赛队伍通过参与即同意遵守竞赛规则和平台条款。
  • 组织者可在事先通知的情况下更新提交格式或评估脚本。

16195.title: EBMV 2026 @ ECCV - Event-Guided Brightness Adjustment on SEE-600K
16195.description:
# EBMV 2026 Low-Level Imaging Challenge: Event-Guided Brightness Adjustment on SEE-600K

## Overview

Real-world images often suffer from different lighting degradations, including low light, over-exposure, mixed illumination, and high-contrast scenes. This challenge aims to study a unified event-guided solution for low-level image enhancement under broad lighting conditions.

Participants are asked to use RGB frames and event data to recover clear, well-exposed, and structurally faithful images. The challenge is associated with the ECCV 2026 Workshop on Event-Based Multimodal Vision: Imaging, Perception, and Understanding.

## Dataset

This challenge is based on SEE-600K, introduced in:

“SEE: See Everything Every Time - Adaptive Brightness Adjustment for Broad Light Range Images via Events”

SEE-600K is a large-scale RGB-event dataset collected under diverse lighting conditions and real-world scenarios. It provides paired RGB images and event data for event-guided brightness adjustment and low-level image restoration.

Dataset:
https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K

Baseline code:
https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE

Workshop website:
https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io

## Task

Participants need to develop an event-guided low-level imaging model.

Input:
1. An RGB image captured under challenging illumination.
2. The corresponding event stream or event representation.
3. Optional metadata or exposure prompt, if provided.

Output:
1. A brightness-adjusted RGB image.
2. The output should preserve scene structure, recover useful details, and avoid unrealistic hallucination.

The task focuses on event-guided brightness adjustment and image enhancement, rather than general image generation.

## Challenge Track

### Broad-Light Adaptive Brightness Adjustment

Participants are encouraged to train models on SEE-600K. Extra training data is allowed, but all external datasets, collection settings, and pretrained models should be clearly reported in the final technical report.

## Submission Format

Each submission should be uploaded as a compressed ZIP file.

Recommended structure:

submission.zip
├── scene_000001.png
├── scene_000002.png
├── scene_000003.png
└── ...

Requirements:
1. The output filename must match the input sample ID.
2. Images should be saved as PNG files.
3. Output images should be RGB images.
4. The output resolution must be the same as the input/reference resolution.
5. Do not change the file names or folder structure.
6. Do not include extra files unless explicitly required.

## Evaluation

The official evaluation will be conducted on the test set.

Metrics:
1. PSNR: pixel-level reconstruction accuracy.
2. SSIM: structural similarity.
3. LPIPS: perceptual similarity.

Ranking:
1. PSNR is the primary ranking metric.
2. SSIM and LPIPS will be used as secondary metrics.
3. The final leaderboard will be based on the official test set.

The validation server is mainly for development and submission-format checking.

## Important Dates

May 15, 2026: Challenge website opens.

May 25, 2026: Validation server online.

June 25, 2026: Test data released and test server online.

July 3, 2026: Test submission deadline and test server closes.

July 10, 2026: Results announcement. Tentative.

## Awards

Top teams will be recognized at the ECCV 2026 Workshop on Event-Based Multimodal Vision.

## Citation

If you use SEE-600K or the SEE baseline, please cite:

“SEE: See Everything Every Time - Adaptive Brightness Adjustment for Broad Light Range Images via Events”

More information is available at:
https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE

## Contact

Yunfan Lu  
ylu066@connect.hkust-gz.edu.cn
16195.description_zh:
# EBMV 2026 低级图像处理挑战赛：基于事件指导的亮度调整 (SEE-600K)

## 概述

真实世界图像经常受到各种光照退化的影响，包括低光、过曝光、混合光照和高对比度场景。本挑战赛旨在研究在广泛光照条件下基于事件指导的统一低级图像增强方案。

参赛者需要利用RGB帧和事件数据来恢复清晰、曝光正确且结构保真的图像。本挑战赛与ECCV 2026事件多模态视觉研讨会相关联：成像、感知与理解。

## 数据集

本挑战赛基于SEE-600K数据集，详见：

"SEE: See Everything Every Time - Adaptive Brightness Adjustment for Broad Light Range Images via Events"

SEE-600K是在多样化光照条件和真实场景下收集的大规模RGB-事件数据集。它为事件指导的亮度调整和低级图像恢复提供配对的RGB图像和事件数据。

数据集：
https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K

基线代码：
https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE

研讨会网址：
https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io

## 任务

参赛者需要开发一个基于事件指导的低级图像处理模型。

输入：
1. 在具有挑战性光照条件下捕获的RGB图像。
2. 对应的事件流或事件表示。
3. 可选的元数据或曝光提示（如提供）。

输出：
1. 亮度调整后的RGB图像。
2. 输出应保持场景结构、恢复有用细节，避免不真实的幻觉。

该任务重点关注事件指导的亮度调整和图像增强，而非通用图像生成。

## 挑战赛赛道

### 广泛光照自适应亮度调整

参赛者可在SEE-600K上训练模型。允许使用额外训练数据，但所有外部数据集、采集设置和预训练模型应在最终技术报告中明确说明。

## 提交格式

每份提交应上传为压缩ZIP文件。

推荐结构：

submission.zip
├── scene_000001.png
├── scene_000002.png
├── scene_000003.png
└── ...

要求：
1. 输出文件名必须与输入样本ID匹配。
2. 图像应保存为PNG文件。
3. 输出图像应为RGB图像。
4. 输出分辨率必须与输入/参考分辨率相同。
5. 不得更改文件名或文件夹结构。
6. 除非明确要求，不包含额外文件。

## 评估

官方评估将在测试集上进行。

指标：
1. PSNR：像素级重建准确度。
2. SSIM：结构相似性。
3. LPIPS：感知相似性。

排名：
1. PSNR是主要排名指标。
2. SSIM和LPIPS用作次要指标。
3. 最终排行榜基于官方测试集。

验证服务器主要用于开发和提交格式检查。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# SEE Challenge 2026: Event-Guided Brightness Adjustment on SEE-600K

Welcome to the **SEE Challenge 2026**, held with **EBMV @ ECCV 2026**. This competition studies **event-guided brightness adjustment under broad lighting conditions**.

The goal is to recover a well-exposed RGB image from a challenging RGB input and its corresponding event-camera signal. Participants may use the official baseline code, the SEE-600K dataset, external data, and pretrained models, as long as external resources are clearly reported in the final technical report.

## Start Here

| Need | Link |
| --- | --- |
| Code, baseline, dataset links, pretrained models | [GitHub: yunfanLu/SEE](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE) |
| Full setup, training, inference, and evaluation tutorial | [docs/TUTORIAL.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md) |
| CodaBench packaging and upload instructions | [docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md) |
| Chinese competition guide | [docs/COMPETITION_GUIDE_ZH.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/COMPETITION_GUIDE_ZH.md) |
| Baseline results | [docs/TEST_RESULTS.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TEST_RESULTS.md) |
| Dataset | [SEE-600K on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K) |

Recommended workflow:

1. Clone the repository and install dependencies.
2. Download SEE-600K.
3. Train or adapt a model using the official training split.
4. Run inference at the original image size, `346 x 260`.
5. Collect prediction images with the provided CodaBench script.
6. Zip the collected folder and upload it to CodaBench.

## Task

Given:

- an RGB image captured under difficult illumination;
- the corresponding event stream or event representation;
- optional brightness or exposure metadata when available;

participants should output a **brightness-adjusted RGB image**.

The output should:

- preserve scene structure and image details;
- produce natural color and exposure;
- avoid unrealistic hallucinated content;
- match the original SEE image resolution for submission.

## Dataset: SEE-600K

The challenge uses **SEE-600K**, a large-scale RGB-event dataset for broad-light-range image enhancement.

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Images | 610,126 |
| Real-world scenarios | 202 |
| Modalities | RGB frames + event data |
| Lighting conditions | low light, normal light, high light, mixed illumination |
| Image resolution | `346 x 260` |

Download:

- Hugging Face: [yunfanlu/SEE-600K](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K)
- Additional mirrors and notes: [GitHub README](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE#dataset-download)

Expected dataset structure and setup instructions are documented in [docs/TUTORIAL.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md#2-prepare-the-dataset).

## Train/Test Split

The following scenario groups are reserved for official testing and **must not be used for training**:

| Type | Reserved groups |
| --- | --- |
| Indoor | 000, 001, 002, 006, 012, 018, 030, 042, 048, 054, 060, 065, 070, 074, 075 |
| Outdoor | 100, 106, 112, 118, 124, 130, 136, 142, 148, 154, 160, 166, 173, 184, 189, 194, 200, 206, 212, 217, 222, 225 |

All other groups are available for training. See the split details in [docs/TUTORIAL.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md#23-traintest-split).

## Baseline: SEE-Net

The official repository provides **SEE-Net**, a compact baseline with approximately **1.9M parameters**. It uses:

- an RGB frame;
- event data;
- a brightness/exposure prompt.

The baseline supports controllable brightness adjustment through the prompt value `B`:

- `B ~= 0.5`: normal exposure;
- `B > 0.5`: brighter output;
- `B < 0.5`: darker output.

Baseline setup, training, inference, and evaluation are documented here:

- Setup: [docs/TUTORIAL.md#3-set-up-the-python-environment](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md#3-set-up-the-python-environment)
- Training: [docs/TUTORIAL.md#5-train-see-net](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md#5-train-see-net)
- Inference and evaluation: [docs/TUTORIAL.md#6-evaluate-or-run-inference](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md#6-evaluate-or-run-inference)
- Pretrained checkpoints: [GitHub README: Pretrained Models](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE#pretrained-models)

## Quick Commands

Clone and install:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE.git
cd SEE

conda create -n see python=3.10 -y
conda activate see
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Download SEE-600K with Hugging Face CLI:

```bash
pip install huggingface_hub
hf download yunfanlu/SEE-600K --local-dir ./SEE-600K
```

Before inference for submission, make sure the YAML crop size is the original image size:

```yaml
crop_w: 346
crop_h: 260
```

Run inference with a trained checkpoint:

```bash
python see/main.py \
  --yaml_file="options/SEE/SEENet_SEE.yaml" \
  --log_dir="./logs/SEE/SEENet_SEE/vis/" \
  --alsologtostderr=True \
  --RESUME_PATH="./logs/SEE/SEENet_SEE/checkpoint.pth.tar" \
  --TEST_ONLY=True \
  --VISUALIZE=True \
  --VAL_BATCH_SIZE=4
```

## Submission Format

After inference, the repository will produce a `vis` folder. Use the provided script to collect only the prediction images required by CodaBench:

```bash
python codabench/collect_codabench_pred.py ${prediction_vis_directory} ${output_directory}
```

Example:

```bash
python codabench/collect_codabench_pred.py /path/to/vis /path/to/output_dir
```

Then create the zip file:

```bash
cd /path/to/output_dir
zip -r submission.zip ./*
```

Upload `submission.zip` on the CodaBench submission page.

Before uploading, check that:

- prediction images are `.png` files;
- prediction images are saved at `346 x 260`;
- the zip contains the collected prediction folder structure, not an extra parent directory;
- filenames and folders are preserved by the collection script;
- the submitted images correspond to the main prediction output used for evaluation. If you use the official `vis` naming convention, the evaluator prioritizes prediction files containing `_p0_`.

Full submission instructions: [docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md).

## Evaluation

Ranking is based on reconstruction quality against the official reference images.

| Metric | Role | Direction |
| --- | --- | --- |
| PSNR | Primary ranking metric | Higher is better |
| SSIM | Secondary metric | Higher is better |

The scorer reports overall PSNR/SSIM and category-level scores for `high-normal`, `low-normal`, and `normal-normal` samples. Missing or invalid predictions receive zero-score penalties for the affected samples.

The validation server is intended for development and format checking. Final ranking is determined by the official test phase.

## Important Dates

| Date | Event |
| --- | --- |
| May 10, 2026 | Competition website opens |
| May 25, 2026 | Validation server online |
| June 25, 2026 | Test data released and test server online |
| July 3, 2026 | Test submission deadline |
| July 10, 2026 | Results announcement, tentative |

## Rules

- Official SEE-600K training and validation data may be used.
- External training data, synthetic data, and pretrained models are allowed, but must be clearly described in the technical report.
- Reserved test groups and hidden ground-truth images must not be used for training or manual tuning.
- Do not exploit the evaluation server or submission system.
- Top-ranked teams may be asked to submit a short technical report and provide reproducibility details.

## FAQ

**Which repository should I use?**  
Use the official repository: [https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE).

**Where are the training and inference commands?**  
They are in [docs/TUTORIAL.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md).

**How do I prepare the CodaBench zip?**  
Follow [docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md). The key steps are: generate `vis`, collect predictions with `codabench/collect_codabench_pred.py`, then zip the collected output folder.

**Why must inference use `346 x 260`?**  
This is the original SEE image size. Submissions with a different output size may fail evaluation or produce invalid scores.

**Can I use external data or pretrained models?**  
Yes, but all external resources must be reported in the final technical report.

## Links

- Competition page: [https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16195/](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16195/)
- Competition forum: [https://www.codabench.org/forums/15944/](https://www.codabench.org/forums/15944/)
- GitHub repository: [https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE)
- Dataset: [https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K)
- Workshop website: [https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io](https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io)
- Competition report: [https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.21120](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.21120)

## Citation

If you use SEE-600K, SEE-Net, or the official baseline code, please cite:

```bibtex
@article{lu2025SEE,
  title={SEE: See Everything Every Time - Adaptive Brightness Adjustment for Broad Light Range Images via Events},
  author={Yunfan Lu, Xiaogang Xu, Hao Lu, Yanlin Qian, Pengteng Li, Huizai Yao, Bin Yang, Junyi Li, Qianyi Cai, Weiyu Guo, Hui Xiong},
  year={2025},
}
```

## Contact

- Yunfan Lu: <ylu066@connect.hkust-gz.edu.cn>
- Mingchao Xu：<mingchao.xu.casia@gmail.com>
pages.content_zh:
# SEE挑战赛2026：SEE-600K上的事件引导亮度调整

欢迎参加**SEE挑战赛2026**，由**EBMV @ ECCV 2026**主办。本竞赛研究**在广泛光照条件下基于事件相机引导的亮度调整**。

目标是从具有挑战性的RGB输入图像及其对应的事件相机信号中恢复一张曝光良好的RGB图像。参赛者可使用官方基准代码、SEE-600K数据集、外部数据和预训练模型，但需在最终技术报告中明确说明所用外部资源。

## 快速开始

| 需求 | 链接 |
| --- | --- |
| 代码、基准、数据集链接、预训练模型 | [GitHub: yunfanLu/SEE](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE) |
| 完整设置、训练、推理及评估教程 | [docs/TUTORIAL.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md) |
| CodaBench打包和上传说明 | [docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/CODABENCH_SUBMISSION_GUIDE.md) |
| 中文竞赛指南 | [docs/COMPETITION_GUIDE_ZH.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/COMPETITION_GUIDE_ZH.md) |
| 基准结果 | [docs/TEST_RESULTS.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TEST_RESULTS.md) |
| 数据集 | [SEE-600K on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K) |

推荐工作流程：

1. 克隆仓库并安装依赖。
2. 下载SEE-600K。
3. 使用官方训练集训练或改进模型。
4. 在原始图像尺寸`346 x 260`下运行推理。
5. 使用提供的CodaBench脚本收集预测图像。
6. 压缩收集的文件夹并上传至CodaBench。

## 任务

给定：

- 在困难光照条件下拍摄的RGB图像；
- 对应的事件流或事件表示；
- 可选的亮度或曝光元数据（如可用）；

参赛者应输出**亮度调整后的RGB图像**。

输出应满足：

- 保留场景结构和图像细节；
- 产生自然的色彩和曝光效果；
- 避免不切实际的幻觉内容；
- 匹配原始SEE图像分辨率以供提交。

## 数据集：SEE-600K

本挑战赛采用**SEE-600K**，这是一个用于宽光度范围图像增强的大规模RGB-事件数据集。

| 属性 | 数值 |
| --- | --- |
| 图像数量 | 610,126 |
| 真实场景 | 202 |
| 数据模态 | RGB帧 + 事件数据 |
| 光照条件 | 低光、正常光、高光、混合光照 |
| 图像分辨率 | `346 x 260` |

下载：

- Hugging Face: [yunfanlu/SEE-600K](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yunfanlu/SEE-600K)
- 其他镜像和说明：[GitHub README](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE#dataset-download)

预期数据集结构和设置说明见[docs/TUTORIAL.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md#2-prepare-the-dataset)。

## 训练/测试划分

以下场景组保留用于官方测试，**不能用于训练**：

| 类型 | 保留组 |
| --- | --- |
| 室内 | 000, 001, 002, 006, 012, 018, 030, 042, 048, 054, 060, 065, 070, 074, 075 |
| 室外 | 100, 106, 112, 118, 124, 130, 136, 142, 148, 154, 160, 166, 173, 184, 189, 194, 200, 206, 212, 217, 222, 225 |

所有其他组可用于训练。详细的划分信息见[docs/TUTORIAL.md](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md#23-traintest-split)。

## 基准：SEE-Net

官方仓库提供**SEE-Net**，一个参数量约为**190万**的紧凑基准模型。它使用：

- RGB帧；
- 事件数据；
- 亮度/曝光提示。

基准支持通过提示值`B`进行可控亮度调整：

- `B ~= 0.5`：正常曝光；
- `B > 0.5`：更亮的输出；
- `B < 0.5`：更暗的输出。

基准的设置、训练、推理和评估文档详见：

- 设置：[docs/TUTORIAL.md#3-set-up-the-python-environment](https://github.com/yunfanLu/SEE/blob/main/docs/TUTORIAL.md)

pages.title: Rules & Timeline
pages.content:
# Rules

Participants may use SEE-600K for model training.

External training data, pretrained models, and synthetic data are allowed, but they must be clearly reported in the final technical report.

Participants must not use hidden ground-truth test data, manually tune results on the test set, or exploit the evaluation server.

Top teams may be asked to submit a short technical report for result verification.

# Important Dates

May 15, 2026: Challenge website opens.

May 25, 2026: Validation server online.

June 25, 2026: Test data released and test server online.

July 3, 2026: Test submission deadline and test server closes.

July 10, 2026: Results announcement. Tentative.
pages.content_zh:
# 规则

参赛者可以使用 SEE-600K 进行模型训练。

允许使用外部训练数据、预训练模型和合成数据，但必须在最终技术报告中清楚地报告。

参赛者不得使用隐藏的测试集真实标签、手动调整测试集结果或滥用评估服务器。

排名靠前的团队可能被要求提交简短技术报告以进行结果验证。

# 重要日期

2026年5月15日：竞赛网站开放。

2026年5月25日：验证服务器上线。

2026年6月25日：发布测试数据并启动测试服务器。

2026年7月3日：测试提交截止日期和测试服务器关闭。

2026年7月10日：结果公布。暂定。

14963.title: DeepPhenoTree - CVPPA Data Challenge
14963.description:
The DeepPhenoTree - Apple Edition dataset (1 438 RGB images of apple trees collected in Switzerland, Belgium, Spain and Italy in 2025) contains 22 500 manually annotated bounding boxes for the three phenological stages flowering, fruitlet and fruit.

Develop and benchmark object‑detection models that automatically locate the three phenological stages in the images. Submissions are evaluated with mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5), the standard metric for detection challenges.

The results of the challenge will be presented during the CVPPA workshop (8‑9 September) of the ECCV conference (8‑13 September 2026) in Malmö, Sweden.
14963.description_zh:
DeepPhenoTree - Apple Edition 数据集（包含 1,438 张苹果树 RGB 图像，采集自瑞士、比利时、西班牙和意大利，2025 年）包含 22,500 个手动标注的边界框，涵盖三个物候阶段：开花期、果实小球期和果实期。

开发并基准测试目标检测模型，该模型能够自动定位图像中的三个物候阶段。提交的结果将使用平均精度均值（mAP@0.5）进行评估，这是检测挑战的标准指标。

挑战赛的结果将在 ECCV 会议（2026 年 9 月 8-13 日）期间的 CVPPA 研讨会（9 月 8-9 日）中呈现，会议地点为瑞典马尔默。

pages.title: DeepPhenoTree – ECCV 2026 CVPPA Data Challenge
pages.content: # DeepPhenoTree – ECCV 2026 CVPPA Data Challenge The objective of this challenge is to develop models capable of detecting objects representative of apple tree phenological stages. ## Why a Phenology Detection Challenge? Phenology is a key indicator of plant development, from dormancy to harvest, and plays a central role in orchard management. However, it is still largely assessed manually, making it time-consuming and difficult to scale. This challenge introduces a standardized benchmark for phenological object detection under real-world conditions. Collected across four apple European experimental orchard sites [1], the dataset captures strong variability across multiple sites, acquisition conditions, and phenological stages, while benefiting from controlled illumination. The task is particularly challenging due to: - Variability across sites and acquisition conditions, - Different object sizes and occlusions within tree canopies, - High intra-class similarity between adjacent phenological stages All images were acquired using a standardized imaging platform under controlled illumination conditions, ensuring consistent visual quality across sites while preserving real open field variability conditions. A key objective of this challenge is to publicly promote models that generalize across sites and conditions. ## Challenge Overview Given an input image of apple trees, participants are asked to design a model that predict bounding boxes for objects associated with Biologische Bundesanstalt,Bundessortenamt et CHemische Industrie (or BBCH) [2] including in three main phenological stages: from Dormant to Flowering (BBCH 00-69), Fruitlet (BBCH 71-77) and Fruit (BBCH 81-89). Ground truth annotations consist of manually labeled bounding boxes with corresponding main phenological stages. Ground-truth boxes are provided in YOLO format (class 0 = flowering, 1 = fruitlet, 2 = fruit). Models are evaluated automatically upon each submission. During the Development Phase, participants receive real-time evaluation scores on a public test set and can iteratively improve their models. During the Blind Evaluation Phase, predictions are generated on a separate private test set, and final scores are only revealed at the end of the competition to ensure fair ranking. ## Contribution ### Paper (under review) Metuarea Herearii, Ousseini Hamza Abdoul-Djalil, Guerra Walter, Zuffa Francesca, Panzeri Francesco, Patocchi Andrea, Lozano Lidia, Van Hoye Shauny, Laurens François, Labrosse Jeremy, Rasti Pejman & Rousseau David. (2026, February 27). DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition: A multi-site apple phenology RGB annotated dataset with deep learning baseline models (Version 1) [Preprint]. Research Square. https://doi.org/10.21203/rs.3.rs-8977752/v1 ### Resources The [DeepPhenoTree](https://napari-hub.org/plugins/deepphenotree) plugin is an user interface of our paper DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition (paper above), and is designed to support experimentation and reproducibility. The plugin allows users to: - Run baseline inference of our RT-DETR models (Flowering, Fruitlet, Fruit), - Visualize predictions interactively, - Export results in YOLO format. This tool is optional and provided for convenience; participants are free to use their own pipelines. ## Acknowledgments The DeepPhenoTree Data Challenge is led by **IRHS**, **Université d'Angers**, **INRAe**, **Institut Agro** and four research institutes of the apple REFPOP consortium : **IRTA** (Spain), **Research Centre Laimburg** (Italy), **Agroscope** (Switzerland) and **Better3fruit N.V.** (Belgium). It is supported by : - The **European Union’s Horizon Europe research innovation programme** under grant agreement No 101094587 (PHENET) - The French National Research Agency (ANR) as part of the **Programme d'Investissements d'Avenir** under grant agreement ANR-11-INBS-0012 (PHENOME-EMPHASIS) - The French National Research Agency (ANR) as part of the **France 2030** investment plan under grant agreement ANR-22-PEAE-0012 (AGROECOPHEN) ## Reference [1] Jung, M., Roth, M., Aranzana, M. J., Auwerkerken, A., Bink, M., Denancé, C., Dujak, C., Durel, C.-E., Font i Forcada, C., Cantin, C. M., Guerra, W., Howard, N. P., Keller, B., Lewandowski, M., Ordidge, M., Rymenants, M., Sanin, N., Studer, B., Zurawicz, E., Laurens, F., Patocchi, A., & Muranty, H. (2020). The apple REFPOP—a reference population for genomics-assisted breeding in apple. Horticulture Research, 7, Article 66. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41438-020-0298-7 [2] Meier, U., Graf, H., Hack, H., Hess, M., Kennel, W., Klose, R., Mappes, D., Seipp, D., Stauss, R., Streif, J., & Boom, T. V. D. (1994). Phenological growth stages of pome fruits (Malus domestica Borkh. and Pyrus communis L.), stone fruits (Prunus species), currants (Ribes species) and strawberry (Fragaria × ananassa Duch.). --- ## Contact For questions, clarifications, or to request additional resources, please contact the organizers at: **challenge2026-deepphenotree@inrae.fr** We look forward to seeing your innovative approaches to phenological detection!
pages.content_zh:
# DeepPhenoTree – ECCV 2026 CVPPA数据挑战赛

本挑战赛的目标是开发能够检测代表苹果树物候阶段的对象的模型。

## 为什么要进行物候检测挑战赛？

物候是植物发育的关键指标，从休眠期到收获期，在果园管理中起着核心作用。然而，物候评估仍主要依靠人工进行，这使得该过程耗时且难以规模化。本挑战赛在真实条件下引入了一个标准化的物候对象检测基准。数据集从四个欧洲苹果试验果园基地收集[1]，在多个基地、采集条件和物候阶段中捕捉了强烈的变异性，同时受益于受控照明条件。

该任务特别具有挑战性，原因包括：
- 不同基地和采集条件之间的变异性，
- 树冠内不同的对象大小和遮挡现象，
- 相邻物候阶段之间的高类内相似性

所有图像均使用标准化成像平台在受控照明条件下采集，确保各基地之间视觉质量的一致性，同时保留真实的开放田间变异条件。本挑战赛的一个关键目标是公开推广能够跨基地和条件泛化的模型。

## 挑战赛概述

给定苹果树的输入图像，参赛者需要设计一个模型来预测与德国农业协会、联邦植物品种办公室和化学工业协会(BBCH)[2]相关的对象的边界框，包括三个主要物候阶段：从休眠期到花期(BBCH 00-69)、小果期(BBCH 71-77)和果实期(BBCH 81-89)。真实标注包括手动标记的边界框及相应的主要物候阶段。真实标注框以YOLO格式提供(类别0 = 花期，1 = 小果期，2 = 果实期)。

模型在每次提交时自动进行评估。

在开发阶段，参赛者在公开测试集上获得实时评估分数，并可迭代改进其模型。

在盲评阶段，对单独的私有测试集进行预测，最终分数仅在竞赛结束时公布，以确保排名公平。

## 贡献

### 论文(审查中)

Metuarea Herearii, Ousseini Hamza Abdoul-Djalil, Guerra Walter, Zuffa Francesca, Panzeri Francesco, Patocchi Andrea, Lozano Lidia, Van Hoye Shauny, Laurens François, Labrosse Jeremy, Rasti Pejman & Rousseau David. (2026年2月27日). DeepPhenoTree – 苹果版本：具有深度学习基线模型的多基地苹果物候RGB标注数据集(版本1)[预印本]。Research Square. https://doi.org/10.21203/rs.3.rs-8977752/v1

### 资源

[DeepPhenoTree](https://napari-hub.org/plugins/deepphenotree)插件是我们论文DeepPhenoTree – 苹果版本(上述论文)的用户界面，旨在支持实验和可重现性。

该插件允许用户：
- 运行我们RT-DETR模型的基线推理(花期、小果期、果实期)，
- 交互式地可视化预测，
- 以YOLO格式导出结果。

此工具是可选的，仅为方便起见；参赛者可自由使用自己的工作流程。

## 致谢

DeepPhenoTree数据挑战赛由**IRHS**、**昂热大学**、**INRAe**、**Institut Agro**和苹果REFPOP联盟的四个研究机构领导：**IRTA**(西班牙)、**Laimburg研究中心**(意大利)、**Agroscope**(瑞士)和**Better3fruit N.V.**(比利时)。得到以下机构的支持：
- **欧盟地平线欧洲研究创新计划**的资助

pages.title: Challenge and Timeline
pages.content:
# Challenge and Timeline
## Task

Given an input image of apple trees, a participating system must output, for each instance in the image, a bounding box and the corresponding phenological stage label.

* **Input:** An RGB image of apple trees (provided in the `images/` folder).
* **Output:**  A `.txt` file with the same base name as the image, containing one detection per line:
```txt
<class_id> <x_center> <y_center> <width> <height> <confidence>
```
where :
- **class_id** (0 = flowering, 1 = fruitlet, 2 = fruit)
- **x_center, y_center, width, height** (bounding boxes coordinates normalized to image width/height in YOLO-style format, one line per detection)
- **confidence** is the confidence the model outputs for the given bounding box (if not provided, it default to 1)

The dataset contains images with multiple instances per image, corresponding to different parts of the tree at various phenological stages. Systems must produce predictions for all detectable instances in each image.

Each object should be detected only once (no duplicate detections with high overlap). See **Competition metric** section for more details.

## Submission Format

Follow the submission guidelines described below.

### 1. Model development

Download the data for the Development stage. Feel free to use your split train/val structure, external data or pretrained models.
A baseline implementation and additional resources may be provided to facilitate reproducibility and benchmarking.

### 2. Prediction Generation

For each image in test set, participants must generate a prediction file in YOLO format using python file. The python file must define a class named Model. This class must be loadable by the evaluation system and expose a prediction method with the following structure :

```
class Model:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        ...

    def predict(self, image_path: str):
        ...
```

Predict :
- Input : Path to a test image
- Output : A list of strings in YOLO format as follow:

```
class_id x_center y_center width height confidence
```
where `class_id` is class index and  `x_center y_center width height` are coordinates of bounding box (in range [0,1]) normalized by image width height, `confidence` is the confidence level (in range [0,1]).

### 3. Package your submission

Create a zip archive named `deepphenotree_challenge2026.zip` with the following structure:

```
deepphenotree_challenge2026/
├─ model.py
├─ weights.pth (or other format)
├─ params.yml (if needed by your model)
└─ README.md   (optional short description)
```

Participants may rely on standard Python deep learning libraries. Any non-standard dependency required for inference must be clearly specified in the README file.

Upload the zip on the **Codabench** competition page.

* Provide predictions for **all images** in the test set.
* All coordinates must be floating-point numbers between 0 and 1.
* Files must be UTF-8 encoded with Unix line endings.

## Competition Metric

Submissions are scored automatically with **Mean Average Precision (mAP)** at IoU 0.5, the standard metric for object-detection challenges.

* **mAP@0.5:0.95** -  Primary leaderboard score (average over IoU thresholds 0.5-0.95).
* **mAP@0.5** - Secondary score.

The evaluation pipeline:

1. For each image, predictions are matched to ground-truth boxes using the Hungarian algorithm.
2. True positives are counted when IoU ≥ 0.5 and class matches.
3. Precision-recall curves are computed per class, then averaged to obtain mAP.

## Timeline

| Date (2026) | Milestone |
|-------------|-----------|
| **15 April** | Codabench competition page goes live; leaderboard starts updating;|
| **15 April** | Training set released|
| **26 June** | Development Phase closes; no more leaderboard updates. |
| **26 June - 5 July** | Deadline for uploading final prediction code / Docker image. |
| **5-6  July** | Final leaderboard published; winners announced. |
| **10  July** | Paper submission deadline (EasyChair). |
| **14 August** | Acceptance notifications. |
| **September 2026** | Presentation at **CVPPA Workshop @ ECCV 2026 (Malmö, Sweden)**. |

All deadlines are **23:59 AoE** (Anywhere on Earth) unless otherwise noted
pages.content_zh:
# 挑战与时间表
## 任务

给定苹果树的输入图像，参赛系统必须为图像中的每个实例输出一个边界框和相应的物候阶段标签。

* **输入：** 苹果树的RGB图像（在`images/`文件夹中提供）。
* **输出：** 与图像同名的`.txt`文件，每行包含一个检测结果：
```txt
<class_id> <x_center> <y_center> <width> <height> <confidence>
```
其中：
- **class_id**（0 = 开花，1 = 小果实，2 = 果实）
- **x_center, y_center, width, height**（边界框坐标，归一化到图像宽度/高度，YOLO格式，每个检测一行）
- **confidence**是模型对给定边界框输出的置信度（如果未提供，默认为1）

数据集包含多实例图像，对应于树的不同部分处于不同的物候阶段。系统必须对每个图像中所有可检测的实例进行预测。

每个对象应该仅被检测一次（无高重叠的重复检测）。详见**竞赛指标**部分。

## 提交格式

遵循下述提交指南。

### 1. 模型开发

下载开发阶段的数据。可自由使用训练/验证集划分、外部数据或预训练模型。
可能会提供基准实现和其他资源以便于重现性和基准测试。

### 2. 预测生成

对于测试集中的每个图像，参赛者必须使用Python文件以YOLO格式生成预测文件。Python文件必须定义一个名为Model的类。该类必须能被评估系统加载，并暴露一个具有以下结构的预测方法：

```
class Model:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        ...

    def predict(self, image_path: str):
        ...
```

预测：
- 输入：测试图像的路径
- 输出：YOLO格式的字符串列表，如下所示：

```
class_id x_center y_center width height confidence
```
其中`class_id`是类索引，`x_center y_center width height`是边界框坐标（范围[0,1]），按图像宽高归一化，`confidence`是置信度（范围[0,1]）。

### 3. 打包提交

创建名为`deepphenotree_challenge2026.zip`的zip存档，结构如下：

```
deepphenotree_challenge2026/
├─ model.py
├─ weights.pth（或其他格式）
├─ params.yml（如模型需要）
└─ README.md   （可选的简短描述）
```

参赛者可使用标准Python深度学习库。推理所需的任何非标准依赖必须在README文件中明确说明。

在**Codabench**竞赛页面上传zip文件。

* 为测试集中的**所有图像**提供预测。
* 所有坐标必须是0到1之间的浮点数。
* 文件必须使用UTF-8编码和Unix行尾。

## 竞赛指标

提交内容使用**平均精度均值(mAP)** IoU 0.5自动评分，这是目标检测竞赛的标准指标。

* **mAP@0.5:0.95** - 主排行榜得分（IoU阈值0.5-0.95的平均值）。
* **mAP@0.5** - 次要得分。

评估流程：

1. 对于每个图像，使用匈牙利算法将预测与真实框匹配。
2. 当IoU ≥ 0.5且类别匹配时，计数为真正例。
3. 计算每个类别的精确率-召回率曲线，然后取平均值得到mAP。

## 时间表

| 日期（2026年） | 里程碑 |
|-------------|-----------|
| **4月15日** | Codabench竞赛页面上线；排行榜开始更新 |
| **4月15日** | 发布训练集 |
| **6月26日** | 开发阶段关闭；排行榜不再更新 |
| **6月26日-7月5日** | 上传最终预测代码/Docker镜像的截止日期 |
| **7月5-6日** | 发布最终排行榜；宣布获胜者 |
| **7月10日** | 论文提交截止日期（EasyChair） |
| **7月14日** | ... |

pages.title: Dataset and Baseline
pages.content:
# Dataset and Baseline

## Dataset

The challenge is based on the **DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition**

| Component | Description | Link |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| **DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition** | 808 annotated RGB images (Switzerland, Belgium, Spain, Italy) collected in 2025 covering flowering, fruitlet, and fruit. | https://entrepot.recherche.data.gouv.fr/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.57745/NORPF1 [2] |
| **License** | etalab 2.0 (open reuse with citation). | – |
| **Metadata** | Includes site and BBCH main stage distribution. | – |

The dataset is split by the organizers into a public dataset that you can split for train/val and a private development test set (accessed via Codabench during the development phase), and a blind test set used for the final evaluation of the model.

| Subset                               | Description                                                                   | Size     |
| ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- |
| **Train / Validation (public)**      | Labeled dataset provided to participants for model development                | 472 / 86 |
| **Development Test (private)** | Hidden test set during the development phase via Codabench | 86       |
| **Blind Test (private)**             | Hidden test set used for final evaluation                                     | 164      |

Participants are free to split the public dataset into training and validation subsets as needed. The development test set allows participants to benchmark their models. Final rankings will be based solely on performance on the blind test set.


## Baseline Results

For the purpose of this challenge, we expect models for three stages.

In our paper, baseline performance is reported using a cross-validation protocol. We trained three separate baseline models, one for each task (Flowering, Fruitlet, Fruit). These models are specialized and optimized independently. Models are trained and evaluated across five folds, and the best-performing model is selected based on test performance. For the purpose of this challenge, we report results obtained from the best fold, corresponding to the model achieving the highest test score (corresponding to blind phase for this data challenge).

| Model | mAP@0.5 | mAP@0.5:0.95 |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| **Fruit-only fine-tuned** | 0.86 | 0.58 |
| **Fruitlet-only fine-tuned** | 0.84 | 0.54 |
| **Flowering-only fine-tuned** | 0.68 | 0.39 |

**For this challenge, participants are encouraged to go beyond this setting and propose a unified or multi-task model capable of jointly addressing all three phenological stages.**

Below, we show results for all stages based on our trained model on Fruit-only fine-tuned to indicate a baseline for participant. Note that the baseline does not model cross-stage relationships, which opens opportunities for improved performance through multi-task or unified approaches.

| Model | mAP@0.5 | mAP@0.5:0.95 |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| **Baseline** | 0.29 | 0.16 |
pages.content_zh:
# 数据集和基线

## 数据集

本挑战赛基于 **DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition**

| 组件 | 描述 | 链接 |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| **DeepPhenoTree – Apple Edition** | 808张标注RGB图像（瑞士、比利时、西班牙、意大利），采集于2025年，涵盖开花期、果实幼期和果实期。 | https://entrepot.recherche.data.gouv.fr/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.57745/NORPF1 [2] |
| **许可证** | etalab 2.0（开放重用需注明出处）。 | – |
| **元数据** | 包括采集地点和BBCH主阶段分布。 | – |

组织方将数据集分为公开数据集（参赛者可自行分割为训练集/验证集）、私密发展阶段测试集（在Codabench上的开发阶段可访问）和盲测集（用于最终模型评估）。

| 子集                               | 描述                                                                   | 规模     |
| ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- |
| **训练/验证（公开）**      | 为参赛者提供的标注数据集，用于模型开发                | 472 / 86 |
| **发展测试（私密）** | 开发阶段通过Codabench的隐藏测试集 | 86       |
| **盲测（私密）**             | 用于最终评估的隐藏测试集                                     | 164      |

参赛者可根据需要自由分割公开数据集为训练集和验证集。发展测试集允许参赛者对模型进行基准测试。最终排名仅基于盲测集上的性能。


## 基线结果

针对本挑战赛，我们期望建立三个阶段的模型。

在我们的论文中，基线性能采用交叉验证协议报告。我们为每个任务（开花期、果实幼期、果实期）训练了三个独立的基线模型。这些模型是专用化的，独立优化。模型在五折交叉验证中训练和评估，根据测试性能选择表现最好的模型。为本挑战赛之目的，我们报告来自最佳折的结果，对应于获得最高测试分数的模型（与该数据挑战赛的盲测阶段相对应）。

| 模型 | mAP@0.5 | mAP@0.5:0.95 |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| **仅果实期微调** | 0.86 | 0.58 |
| **仅果实幼期微调** | 0.84 | 0.54 |
| **仅开花期微调** | 0.68 | 0.39 |

**对于本挑战赛，我们鼓励参赛者超越此设置，提出能够联合处理所有三个物候阶段的统一模型或多任务模型。**

下表展示了基于我们训练的仅果实期微调模型在所有阶段的结果，作为参赛者的基线。注意基线未建立跨阶段关系模型，这为通过多任务或统一方法改进性能提供了机会。

| 模型 | mAP@0.5 | mAP@0.5:0.95 |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| **基线** | 0.29 | 0.16 |

pages.title: FAQ
pages.content:
# FAQ

❓ Can I use external data or pretrained models?

Yes. Participants are allowed to use external datasets or pretrained models (e.g., ImageNet, COCO, or self-supervised models), unless explicitly stated otherwise. All training procedures must remain reproducible, and participants are expected to clearly document any external resources used in their README.md.

❓ Do I need to use the provided train/validation split?

No. The provided split is recommended but not mandatory. Participants are free to define their own train/validation split.

❓ What environment is used for the ingestion/scoring of my model?

The evaluation is performed on a dedicated runner equipped with an NVIDIA V100 (32GB) GPU.

Time Limit: The entire inference process (loading the model and processing all test images) must be completed within 30 minutes. Submissions exceeding this limit will be automatically timed out.

Environment: All scripts are executed within a controlled Docker container to ensure reproducibility. You can access the official base image here: `registry.forge.inrae.fr/imhorphen/deepphenotree/cvppa-2026-base:v1`.

Software Stack: To inspect the pre-installed libraries and their exact versions, you can run the following command:
```bash
podman run --rm \
    registry.forge.inrae.fr/imhorphen/deepphenotree/cvppa-2026-base:v1 \
    pip freeze
```

❓ What happens if my submission does not follow the required format?

Submissions are evaluated automatically. Any submission that does not follow the required structure or interface may fail during ingestion and will not be evaluated. Participants are strongly encouraged to validate their submission before uploading.

❓ Can I use the development or test data for training?

No. Although the test labels are not accessible, the development and blind test sets must not be used for training or model tuning.

More questions : contact **challenge2026-deepphenotree@inrae.fr**
pages.content_zh:
# 常见问题

❓ 我可以使用外部数据或预训练模型吗？

可以。参赛者被允许使用外部数据集或预训练模型（例如ImageNet、COCO或自监督模型），除非另有明确说明。所有训练流程必须保持可重现性，参赛者需要在README.md中清楚地记录所使用的任何外部资源。

❓ 我需要使用提供的训练/验证数据划分吗？

不需要。提供的数据划分是推荐的，但不是强制的。参赛者可以自由定义自己的训练/验证数据划分。

❓ 我的模型在什么环境中进行数据导入/评分？

评估在配备NVIDIA V100（32GB）GPU的专用运行器上执行。

时间限制：整个推理过程（加载模型和处理所有测试图像）必须在30分钟内完成。超出此限制的提交将自动超时。

环境：所有脚本在受控的Docker容器内执行以确保可重现性。您可以在此处访问官方基础镜像：`registry.forge.inrae.fr/imhorphen/deepphenotree/cvppa-2026-base:v1`。

软件堆栈：要检查预装库及其确切版本，可以运行以下命令：
```bash
podman run --rm \
    registry.forge.inrae.fr/imhorphen/deepphenotree/cvppa-2026-base:v1 \
    pip freeze
```

❓ 如果我的提交不遵循所需格式会怎样？

提交内容自动进行评估。任何不遵循所需结构或界面的提交可能在数据导入期间失败，将不被评估。强烈建议参赛者在上传前验证其提交。

❓ 我可以使用开发或测试数据进行训练吗？

不可以。虽然测试标签无法访问，但开发和盲测集不得用于训练或模型调优。

更多问题：请联系 **challenge2026-deepphenotree@inrae.fr**

pages.title: Prizes
pages.content:
# Prizes
One full registration at ICCV 2027 conference, sponsored by **Université d'Angers**:

* **Overall Winner** - Highest mAP@0.5:0.95 on the blind test set.
* **Best Academic Submission** - Highest ranking among submissions from universities or research institutes.

To be eligible, participants must submit **reproducible prediction code and trained models** (Docker image or GitHub repository) by the code-submission deadline (see Timeline).

Submitting a short system **paper (≤ 4 pages) is optional**. 

Participation in the ECCV CVPPA workshop requires full submission of a paper through submission system before 10th July.
pages.content_zh:
# 奖项
由**昂热大学**赞助的一个ICCV 2027会议完整注册：

* **总体获胜者** - 在盲测试集上获得最高mAP@0.5:0.95。
* **最佳学术投稿** - 来自大学或研究机构的投稿中排名最高。

为符合资格条件，参赛者必须在代码提交截止日期前提交**可复现的预测代码和训练模型**（Docker镜像或GitHub仓库）（见时间表）。

提交简短的系统**论文（≤4页）是可选的**。

参加ECCV CVPPA研讨会需在7月10日前通过提交系统完整提交论文。

16656.title: LoViF 2026 The Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression @ ECCV 2026
16656.description: Our goal is to introduce a new benchmark for ultra-low bitrate image compression under extreme bitrate constraints.
16656.description_zh: 我们的目标是在极端比特率限制下引入一个超低比特率图像压缩的新基准。

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression

 

Important Dates

  • 2026.05.27 — Release of the train data
  • 2026.05.27 — Release of the validation data
  • 2026.05.27 — Validation server online
  • 2026.07.11 — Submission Deadline for Decoder Executable Files (Maximum File Size: 4 GB)
  • 2026.07.12 — Release of the final test data
  • 2026.07.15 — Deadline for test result submission
  • 2026.07.15 — Deadline for factsheet submission
  • 2026.07.20 — Preliminary test results released to participants
  • 2026.07.20 — Deadline for workshop paper submission
  • 2026.09.xx — LoViF workshop and challenges, results and award ceremony (ECCV 2026)

Challenge Overview

The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers with Generative AI, Preference Optimization and Agentic Systems (LoViF) will be held in September 2026 in conjunction with ECCV 2026.

Image compression is undergoing rapid evolution toward more perceptually oriented visual coding paradigms. Traditional codecs mainly focus on pixel-wise fidelity, which often leads to severe blurring, blocking artifacts, and loss of fine textures at ultra-low bitrates. Recently, advanced learning-based approaches, especially those leveraging powerful generative priors, have demonstrated strong potential in synthesizing perceptually realistic details and improving visual quality under extremely limited bitrate budgets.

This challenge focuses on ultra-low bitrate image compression, aiming to push the boundaries of perceptual reconstruction, visual quality, and compact visual representations under extreme bitrate constraints. Participants are encouraged to explore innovative compression frameworks, including approaches that incorporate advanced generative priors (e.g., diffusion models, GANs, autoregressive models, etc.), as well as other advanced learning-based techniques. We welcome researchers and practitioners from both academia and industry to benchmark their latest algorithms and contribute to the development of next-generation image compression systems.

Motivation for This Competition

While classic image compression has achieved high coding efficiency, traditional methods often suffer from severe blurring, blocking, and texture degradation at ultra-low bitrates. This is largely because standard optimization metrics (such as PSNR and MS-SSIM) prioritize pixel-wise fidelity over perceptual realism, which may not fully align with human visual preferences.

Recent advances in learning-based image generation have opened new opportunities for ultra-low bitrate compression. In particular, generative priors and perceptually driven reconstruction strategies have shown strong capability in restoring realistic textures and improving visual quality from highly compact representations. Nevertheless, balancing perceptual quality, semantic consistency, and coding efficiency under extremely low bitrate constraints remains a challenging problem.

To promote research in this area, the LoViF Challenge introduces a specialized benchmark focused on ultra-low bitrate image compression. We encourage participants to explore advanced perceptual reconstruction techniques and innovative coding strategies that improve human visual experience while maintaining competitive compression performance. Ultimately, this competition aims to establish a practical and forward-looking benchmark for next-generation visual coding systems.

Top-ranked participants will be awarded and invited to submit workshop papers describing their methods and solutions to the associated LoViF workshop at ECCV 2026.

More details are available in the data, submission and evaluation pages.

Awards

The following awards will be provided to the top-performing teams:

  • One First-Class Award (Champion): USD $1,000
  • One Second-Class Award: USD $600
  • One Third-Class Award: USD $400

Provided Resources

  • Scripts: The organizers will provide standard evaluation scripts to facilitate the reproducibility of the images and performance evaluation results.
  • Contact: Please use the forum on the challenge page for technical discussions, or directly contact the challenge organizers by email.
pages.content_zh:
第2届低级视觉前沿工作坊(LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛

 

重要时间节点

  • 2026.05.27 — 发布训练数据
  • 2026.05.27 — 发布验证数据
  • 2026.05.27 — 验证服务器上线
  • 2026.07.11 — 解码器可执行文件提交截止(最大文件大小：4 GB)
  • 2026.07.12 — 发布最终测试数据
  • 2026.07.15 — 测试结果提交截止
  • 2026.07.15 — 信息表单提交截止
  • 2026.07.20 — 向参赛者发布初步测试结果
  • 2026.07.20 — 工作坊论文提交截止
  • 2026.09.xx — LoViF工作坊和挑战赛、结果发布及颁奖仪式(ECCV 2026)

挑战赛概述

第2届低级视觉前沿工作坊(具有生成式人工智能、偏好优化和代理系统)将于2026年9月与ECCV 2026同期召开。

图像压缩正在快速发展，朝着更加感知导向的视觉编码范式演进。传统编码器主要关注逐像素保真度，这在超低比特率下往往导致严重的模糊、分块伪影和细节纹理丧失。最近，先进的基于学习的方法，特别是那些利用强大生成先验的方法，在合成感知逼真细节和在极限比特率预算下改进视觉质量方面展示了强大潜力。

本挑战赛聚焦于超低比特率图像压缩，旨在推进感知重建、视觉质量和极限比特率约束下紧凑视觉表示的边界。参赛者被鼓励探索创新的压缩框架，包括融入先进生成先验(例如扩散模型、GANs、自回归模型等)的方法，以及其他先进的基于学习的技术。我们欢迎来自学术界和产业界的研究人员和从业者对标他们的最新算法，并为下一代图像压缩系统的发展做出贡献。

本次竞赛的动机

虽然经典图像压缩已经实现了高编码效率，但传统方法在超低比特率下往往存在严重的模糊、分块和纹理退化问题。这主要是因为标准优化指标(如PSNR和MS-SSIM)优先考虑逐像素保真度而非感知真实性，这可能不能完全符合人类视觉偏好。

基于学习的图像生成的最近进展为超低比特率压缩开辟了新机遇。特别是，生成先验和感知驱动的重建策略在从高度紧凑的表示中恢复逼真纹理和改进视觉质量方面展现了强大能力。然而，在极限比特率约束下平衡感知质量、语义一致性和编码效率仍然是一个具有挑战性的问题。

为了促进该领域的研究，LoViF挑战赛引入了一个专门的基准测试，聚焦于超低比特率图像压缩。我们鼓励参赛者探索先进的感知重建技术和创新编码策略，在改进人类视觉体验的同时保持竞争力的压缩性能。最终，本竞赛旨在建立一个实用且前瞻性的基准测试。

pages.title: data
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression

 

Data Overview

The dataset for this challenge is constructed from selected samples of open-source high-resolution image datasets and high-quality images collected from the Internet. It is specifically curated to benchmark the frontiers of ultra-low bitrate image compression, featuring diverse real-world scenes with high-quality visual content. In this competition, the dataset is composed of:

  • train data: A total of 14,136 high-quality images are provided for training image compression models. Participants are encouraged to use this dataset. Additional public datasets are permitted, but all external data sources must be clearly disclosed in the factsheet.
  • validation data: A total of 100 high-quality images are provided for performance tuning during the Development Phase.
  • test data: A total of 100 high-quality images are additionally provided for the final evaluation during the Testing Phase.

Data Access

By accessing the data, the participants implicitly agree with the terms and conditions of the challenge.

  • Training Data:
    • Training Dataset (14,136 high-quality images from Flickr2K training set [1], DIV2K training set [2], CLIC2020 training and validation set [3], CLIC2021 test set [4], LSDIR [5])
  • Development Phase:
    • Validation Data (100 high-quality images from DIV2K test set [2], CLIC2020 test set [3], Kodak [6])
  • Testing Phase:
    • Test Data: To be released when the Testing Phase begins.
References
  1. Agustsson, E., & Timofte, R. NTIRE 2017 Challenge on Single Image Super-Resolution: Dataset and Study. In CVPRW 2017.
  2. Timofte, R., Agustsson, E., ... & Zhang, L. NTIRE 2017 Challenge on Single Image Super-Resolution: Methods and Results. In CVPRW 2017.
  3. Toderici, G., Theis, L., ... & Timofte, R. CLIC 2020: Challenge on Learned Image Compression. 2020.
  4. Toderici, G., Theis, L., ... & Timofte, R. CLIC 2021: Challenge on Learned Image Compression. 2021.
  5. Li, Y., Zhang, K., ... & Van Gool, L. LSDIR: A Large Scale Dataset for Image Restoration. In CVPR 2023.
  6. Eastman Kodak Company. Kodak Image Database. 1993.
pages.content_zh:
第2届低阶视觉前沿研讨会(LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛

 

数据概览

本挑战赛的数据集由开源高分辨率图像数据集的精选样本和从互联网收集的高质量图像组成。该数据集专门为超低比特率图像压缩的前沿基准而精选，具有多样化的真实场景和高质量视觉内容。在本次竞赛中，数据集包括：

  • 训练数据： 提供共计14,136张高质量图像用于训练图像压缩模型。鼓励参赛者使用此数据集。允许使用其他公开数据集，但所有外部数据源必须在说明书中清楚地标注。
  • 验证数据： 提供共计100张高质量图像，用于开发阶段的性能调优。
  • 测试数据： 另外提供共计100张高质量图像，用于测试阶段的最终评估。

数据获取

参赛者通过访问数据，即表示隐式同意挑战赛的条款和条件。

  • 训练数据：
    • 训练数据集 (14,136张高质量图像，来自Flickr2K训练集[1]、DIV2K训练集[2]、CLIC2020训练和验证集[3]、CLIC2021测试集[4]、LSDIR[5])
  • 开发阶段：
    • 验证数据 (100张高质量图像，来自DIV2K测试集[2]、CLIC2020测试集[3]、Kodak[6])
  • 测试阶段：
    • 测试数据： 将在测试阶段开始时发布。
参考文献
  1. Agustsson, E., & Timofte, R. NTIRE 2017单图像超分辨率挑战赛：数据集和研究。 CVPRW 2017。
  2. Timofte, R., Agustsson, E., ... & Zhang, L. NTIRE 2017单图像超分辨率挑战赛：方法和结果。 CVPRW 2017。
  3. Toderici, G., Theis, L., ... & Timofte, R. CLIC 2020：学习图像压缩挑战赛。 2020。
  4. Toderici, G., Theis, L., ... & Timofte, R. CLIC 2021：学习图像压缩挑战赛。 2021。
  5. Li, Y., Zhang, K., ... & Van Gool, L. LSDIR：大规模图像复原数据集。 CVPR 2023。
  6. Eastman Kodak Company. 柯达图像数据库。 1993。

pages.title: evaluation
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression

 

Development Phase

During the development phase, submissions will be evaluated using both fidelity-oriented and perception-oriented metrics under ultra-low bitrate constraints. The evaluation metrics include PSNR, MS-SSIM, LPIPS, DISTS, and bitrate (BPP). Both PSNR and MS-SSIM are computed in the RGB domain.

This challenge requires participants to submit results at one single bitrate operating point. The average bitrate constraint over the entire dataset is:

average_bpp ≤ 0.008

Submissions violating the bitrate constraint will be considered invalid and will not appear on the leaderboard.

The leaderboard ranking during the development phase will be determined by the following weighted score:

Final_Score = PSNR + 10 × MS_SSIM + 20 × (1 - LPIPS) + 25 × (1 - DISTS)

Test Phase

Please note that participants are required to use only the corresponding model checkpoints submitted during the code submission phase for the final evaluation.

The final ranking will be determined primarily through subjective evaluation. Submitted methods will be assessed from both reconstruction fidelity and perceptual quality perspectives to better reflect human visual preferences under ultra-low bitrate constraints.

Please refer to the Submission page for detailed submission instructions and required file formats.

Final Test Phase - Preliminary Results [TBA]

Preliminary results will be posted here once the evaluation is complete.
pages.content_zh:
第2届低层次视觉前沿研讨会（LoViF）@ ECCV 2026

 

超低比特率图像压缩竞赛

 

开发阶段

在开发阶段，提交的方案将在超低比特率约束下使用保真度导向和感知导向的指标进行评估。评估指标包括PSNR、MS-SSIM、LPIPS、DISTS和比特率（BPP）。PSNR和MS-SSIM均在RGB域中计算。

本竞赛要求参赛者在单一比特率工作点提交结果。整个数据集的平均比特率约束为：

average_bpp ≤ 0.008

违反比特率约束的提交将被视为无效，不会出现在排行榜上。

开发阶段的排行榜排名将由以下加权评分决定：

Final_Score = PSNR + 10 × MS_SSIM + 20 × (1 - LPIPS) + 25 × (1 - DISTS)

测试阶段

请注意，参赛者必须在最终评估中使用代码提交阶段提交的对应模型检查点。

最终排名将主要通过主观评估确定。提交的方法将从重建保真度和感知质量两个角度进行评估，以更好地反映在超低比特率约束下的人类视觉偏好。

详细的提交说明和所需文件格式请参阅提交页面。

最终测试阶段 - 初步结果 [待公布]

评估完成后，初步结果将在此发布。

pages.title: submission
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression

 

Submission Workflow

The challenge submission process consists of three stages:

  1. Development Phase (due: 2026.07.08): Participants submit a ZIP archive following the required Results Submission Format through the Codabench “My Submissions” page. The evaluation server automatically computes the metrics and updates the public leaderboard.
  2. Code Submission Phase (2026.07.08-2026.07.11): Teams intending to participate in the final test phase must submit their executable/source decoder to the organizers via email. The submitted decoder must be compatible with the bitstreams submitted during the test phase. To limit model size and computational complexity, the size of the submitted executable decoder must not exceed 4 GB.
  3. Final Test Phase (2026.07.12-2026.07.15): Participants must run inference on the released test set using only the corresponding submitted codec, and submit the final ZIP archive via email for evaluation, following the required Results Submission Format.

Results Submission Format

The following submission format must be followed for both the Development Phase and the Final Test Phase submissions.

  1. Please ensure that the reconstructed output images have exactly the same filenames as the corresponding input images (e.g., for an input image named 0001.png, the reconstructed output image must also be named 0001.png). The reconstructed images must preserve the original spatial resolution.
  2. Create a ZIP archive following the structure below: 

    submission.zip
│
├── reconstructed/
│    ├── 0001.png
│    ├── 0002.png
│
├── bitstream/
│    ├── 0001.bin
│    ├── 0002.bin
│
└── readme.txt
    The readme.txt file should contain the runtime per image (in seconds), whether CPU or GPU is used during inference, whether extra training data is used, and a brief description of the submitted method. 
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43
CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
Other description: Briefly describe the submitted solution, including dependencies, code links, scripts, training data, or any other relevant information.

Development Phase Submission Guidelines

During the development phase, participants are only required to submit a ZIP archive following the Results Submission Format through the Codabench “My Submissions” page. The evaluation server will automatically compute the metrics and update the public leaderboard. Executable/source decoder are NOT required during the development phase.


Code Submission Phase Guidelines

After the development phase ends, teams intending to participate in the final test phase must submit their executable/source decoder via email to the challenge organizers at: xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn or zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn.

No additional encoder/decoder updates, model retraining, code modifications, or online/test-time fine-tuning are allowed after the Code Submission Phase.

The email subject should follow the format:

[LoViF2026] - [Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression] - [YOUR_TEAM_NAME]

The email body must include the following information:

  1. Full challenge name: LoViF 2026 - Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression
  2. Team name
  3. Team leader’s name and email address
  4. Team name and usernames registered on the LoViF 2026 Codabench server
  5. Attached executable/source decoder, or corresponding download links (Maximum File Size: 4 GB)

The executable/source decoder package must include all trained decoder models and necessary parameters to ensure reproducibility. A README file describing how to run the code is mandatory.


Final Test Phase Submission Guidelines ( The Factsheet template can be found here )

During the final test phase, participants are required to generate and submit a final ZIP archive following the Results Submission Format using only the corresponding submitted codec and pretrained model checkpoints. Final test phase submissions should be emailed to the challenge organizers at: xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn or zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn.

No additional model updates, retraining, or code modifications are allowed after the Code Submission Phase.

The email subject should follow the format:

[LoViF2026] - [Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression] - [YOUR_TEAM_NAME]

The email body must include the following information:

  1. Full challenge name: LoViF 2026 - Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression
  2. Team name
  3. Team leader’s name and email address
  4. Names, email addresses, and affiliations/institutions of all team members
  5. Team name and usernames registered on the LoViF 2026 Codabench server
  6. Attached Factsheet (Please use the provided LoViF template. In addition, the Factsheet submission must include both the compiled PDF version and the corresponding LaTeX source files.)
  7. Download links to the FULL final submission ZIP archive following the required Results Submission Format

Top-ranked participants will be invited to co-author the challenge report and are encouraged to submit papers describing their methods to the LoViF 2026 workshop. All workshop papers will undergo the standard peer-review process. For more information, please visit the LoViF 2026 Official Website .
pages.content_zh:
第二届低级视觉前沿研讨会（LoViF）@ ECCV 2026

 

超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛

 

提交工作流程

挑战赛提交过程包括三个阶段：

  1. 发展阶段（截止时间：2026.07.08）： 参赛者需通过Codabench的"我的提交"页面提交按照规定结果提交格式组织的ZIP文件。评估服务器将自动计算指标并更新公开排行榜。
  2. 代码提交阶段（2026.07.08-2026.07.11）： 打算参与最终测试阶段的团队必须通过电子邮件将可执行文件/源解码器提交给组织者。提交的解码器必须与测试阶段提交的比特流兼容。为了限制模型大小和计算复杂度，提交的可执行解码器大小不得超过4 GB。
  3. 最终测试阶段（2026.07.12-2026.07.15）： 参赛者必须仅使用相应提交的编解码器对发布的测试集进行推理，并通过电子邮件提交最终ZIP文件进行评估，格式遵循规定的结果提交格式。

结果提交格式

发展阶段和最终测试阶段的提交都必须遵循以下格式。

  1. 确保重建输出图像的文件名与对应输入图像的文件名完全相同（例如，输入图像名为0001.png，重建输出图像也必须命名为0001.png）。重建图像必须保持原始空间分辨率。
  2. 创建遵循以下结构的ZIP文件： 

    submission.zip
│
├── reconstructed/
│    ├── 0001.png
│    ├── 0002.png
│
├── bitstream/
│    ├── 0001.bin
│    ├── 0002.bin
│
└── readme.txt
    readme.txt文件应包含每张图像的运行时间（秒），推理过程中使用的是CPU还是GPU，是否使用了额外训练数据，以及所提交方法的简要说明。 
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43
CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
Other description: 简要描述所提交的解决方案，包括依赖项、代码链接、脚本、训练数据或任何其他相关信息。

发展阶段提交指南

在发展阶段，参赛者只需通过Codabench的"我的提交"页面提交遵循结果提交格式的ZIP文件。评估服务器将自动计算指标并更新公开排行榜。发展阶段不需要提交可执行文件/源解码器。


代码提交阶段指南

发展阶段结束后，打算参与最终测试阶段的团队必须通过电子邮件将其可执行文件/源解码器提交给挑战赛组织者： xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn 或 zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn。

代码提交阶段结束后，不允许进行任何编码器/解码器更新、模型重新训练、代码修改或在线/测试时微调。

电子邮件主题应遵循以下格式：

[LoViF2026] - [Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression] - [YOUR_TEAM_NAME]

电子邮件正文必须包含以下信息：

  1. 完整挑战赛名称： LoViF 2026 - 超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛
  2. 团队名称

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

LoViF 2026 The Third Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images @ ECCV 2026

Important dates

  • 2026.05.22 Release of train data (input and output)
  • 2026.07.01 Final test data release (inputs only)
  • 2026.07.08 Test output results submission deadline
  • 2026.07.08 Fact sheets and code/executable submission deadline
  • 2026.07.15 Preliminary test results release to the participants
  • 2026.07.15 Paper submission deadline for entries (Internal Review)
  • 2026.09.x LoViF workshop and challenges, results and award ceremony (ECCV 2026)

 

Challenge overview

The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) will be held in conjunction with ECCV 2026.

Image manipulation is a key computer vision task, aiming at the restoration of degraded image content, the filling in of missing information, or the needed transformation and/or manipulation to achieve a desired target (with respect to perceptual quality, contents, or performance of apps working on such images). Recent years have witnessed an increased interest from the vision and graphics communities in these fundamental topics of research. Not only has there been a constantly growing flow of related papers, but also substantial progress has been achieved.

Each step forward eases the use of images by people or computers for the fulfillment of further tasks, as image manipulation serves as an important front end. Not surprisingly then, there is an ever-growing range of applications in fields such as surveillance, the automotive industry, electronics, remote sensing, or medical image analysis etc. The emergence and ubiquitous use of mobile and wearable devices offer another fertile ground for additional applications and faster methods.

This workshop aims to provide an overview of the new trends and advances in those areas. Moreover, it will offer an opportunity for academic and industrial attendees to interact and explore collaborations.

 

Motivation for this competition

Raindrops on camera lenses or windshields significantly reduce image visibility in human life, posing challenges for applications like surveillance and autonomous driving. Effective raindrop removal is crucial to ensuring reliable performance in these systems. However, most deraining datasets overlooked the complex environment in real life. In particular, there are few raindrop-focused datasets, and most existing datasets focus on capturing background scenes while the camera is focused on the background. Meanwhile, these datasets primarily target daytime scenarios, with limited attention to nighttime conditions.

To promote the development of deraining, we introduce the first large-scale real-world raindrop-clarity dataset, which includes raindrop-focused images and nighttime scenes. Based on this dataset, we organize LoViF 2026 The Third Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images @ ECCV 2026, jointly with the LoViF workshop.

The goal of this competition is to establish a new and applicable benchmark for the Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images. We are looking forward to the collaborative efforts of our participants, aiming to elevate the quality of restored images. The final ranking will be made based on PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS.

 

The top-ranked participants will be awarded and invited to follow the ECCV submission guide for workshops to describe their solution and to submit to the associated LoViF workshop at ECCV 2026.

More details are found in the competition data section.

Competition

The training data will be made available to the registered participants.

Provided Resources

  • Scripts: With the dataset the organizers will provide scripts to facilitate the reproducibility of the images and performance evaluation results after the validation server is online. More information is provided on the data page.
  • Contact: You can use the forum on the data description page (highly recommended!) or directly contact the challenge organizers by email (yshustc@mail.ustc.edu.cn, jinyeying@u.nus.edu, xin.li@ustc.edu.cn) if you have doubts or any questions.
pages.content_zh:
第2届低阶视觉前沿研讨会(LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

LoViF 2026 第三届日夜双焦点图像雨滴去除挑战赛 @ ECCV 2026

重要日期

  • 2026.05.22 发布训练数据(输入和输出)
  • 2026.07.01 发布最终测试数据(仅限输入)
  • 2026.07.08 测试输出结果提交截止日期
  • 2026.07.08 事实说明书及代码/可执行文件提交截止日期
  • 2026.07.15 向参赛者发布初步测试结果
  • 2026.07.15 参赛作品论文提交截止日期(内部审查)
  • 2026.09.x LoViF研讨会、挑战赛、结果发布及颁奖典礼(ECCV 2026)

 

挑战赛概述

第2届低阶视觉前沿研讨会(LoViF)将与ECCV 2026同期举办。

图像处理是计算机视觉的关键任务,旨在恢复退化的图像内容、填补缺失信息,或进行必要的变换和/或处理以实现所需的目标(关于感知质量、内容或基于此类图像的应用性能)。近年来,视觉和图形社区对这些基础研究课题的兴趣不断增加。不仅相关论文数量持续增长,而且在该领域也取得了实质性进展。

每一项进展都便于人类或计算机利用图像完成进一步任务,因为图像处理是一个重要的前端。因此,在监控、汽车工业、电子产品、遥感或医学图像分析等领域的应用范围在不断扩大。移动和可穿戴设备的出现和普遍使用为额外应用和更快方法提供了又一个富有成效的领域。

本研讨会旨在概述这些领域的新趋势和进展。此外,它将为学术界和业界参与者提供互动和探索合作的机会。

 

竞赛动机

相机镜头或挡风玻璃上的雨滴严重降低了人类生活中的图像可见性,对监控和自动驾驶等应用构成了挑战。有效的雨滴去除对确保这些系统的可靠性能至关重要。然而,大多数去雨数据集忽略了现实生活中的复杂环境。特别是,以雨滴为重点的数据集很少,大多数现有数据集在相机聚焦于背景时主要用于捕捉背景场景。同时,这些数据集主要针对白天场景,对夜间条件的关注有限。

为了推动去雨技术的发展,我们推出了首个大规模真实世界雨滴清晰度数据集,其中包括以雨滴为重点的图像和夜间场景。基于该数据集,我们组织了LoViF 2026第三届日夜双焦点图像雨滴去除挑战赛,与LoViF研讨会联合举办。

本竞赛的目标是为日夜双焦点图像雨滴去除建立新的实用基准。我们期待参赛者的共同努力,旨在提升图像恢复质量。最终排名将基于PSNR、SSIM和LPIPS进行评定。

pages.title: data
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

LoViF 2026 The Third Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images @ ECCV 2026

 

Data overview

The dataset named RainDrop Clarity is contributed by Yeying Jin et al (NUS). The dataset includes both daytime and nighttime scenes for training and testing. (i) The daytime raindrop dataset contains a total of 5,442 paired or triplet images, with 4,713 pairs used for training and the remaining 729 pairs for testing. (ii) The nighttime raindrop dataset consists of 9,744 paired or triplet images, where 8,655 pairs are allocated to the training set, and the remaining 1,089 pairs are reserved for the validation and test set.

In this competition, the used dataset is composed of:

  • train data: A total of 14,139 day and night dual-focused raindrop images with blurry backgrounds and their corresponding clear backgrounds are provided.
  • validation data: A total of 240 day and night rainy images are provided. 
  • test data: A total of 731 day and night rainy images are provided.

 

Data access

By accessing the data the participants implicitly agree with the terms and conditions of the LoViF challenge.

 

    • Development phase / Learning:
      • Train Data, please download day rainy data and night rainy data with Google drive. Participants are allowed to use publicly available external datasets for training, as long as the sources of the data are explicitly documented in the factsheet. More details about the training data can be found in FoundIR.
      • Validation Data (only input images), has been released
      • Test Data (only input images): [TBA]

 

Final test phase submission guidelines (Factsheet can be found in [TBA])

The final submission should be emailed to the organizers (see the "Organizers" tab for contact details).

The title of the mail should be:  [LoViF2026] - [TASK_NAME] - [TEAM_NAME]

The body of the mail shall include the following:

      1. the challenge full name
      2. team name
      3. team leader and all members' names, email addresses and institutes.
      4. team members with LoViF 2026 sponsors
      5. team name and user names on LoViF 2026 Codabench competitions
      6. executable/source code attached or download links
      7. factsheet attached (you should use the updated factsheet of the competition!)
      8. download link(s) to the FULL results of ALL of the test frames (corresponding to the last entry in the online Codabench test server and provided codes/exe)

The executable/source code should include trained models or necessary parameters to ensure reproducibility. A README file explaining how to run the code is mandatory. The Factsheet must be a compiled PDF file along with its LaTeX source files.

Note that you can still submit to the validation server in those competitions for which the validation ground truth images were not released. 

 

Final test phase - preliminary results [TBA]

Preliminary results will be posted here once the evaluation is complete.

Note that top-ranked participants will be invited to co-author the challenge report and contribute a paper to the LoViF 2026 workshop. All challenge papers will undergo a peer-review process. For more information, please check the LoViF 2026 official website.

# 第二届低阶视觉前沿研讨会（LoViF）@ ECCV 2026

# LoViF 2026 第三届双焦点图像日夜雨滴去除挑战赛 @ ECCV 2026

## 数据概览

数据集名称为RainDrop Clarity，由Yeying Jin等人（NUS）贡献。该数据集包含白天和夜间场景的训练和测试数据。(i) 白天雨滴数据集包含共5,442个配对或三元组图像，其中4,713对用于训练，其余729对用于测试。(ii) 夜间雨滴数据集包含9,744个配对或三元组图像，其中8,655对分配给训练集，其余1,089对用于验证和测试集。

本竞赛使用的数据集构成如下：

- 训练数据：提供共14,139张日夜双焦点雨滴图像及其对应的清晰背景图像。
- 验证数据：提供共240张日夜雨滴图像。
- 测试数据：提供共731张日夜雨滴图像。

## 数据访问

**参赛者访问数据即表示隐式同意LoViF挑战赛的条款和条件。**

- **发展阶段/学习：**
  - 训练数据，请通过Google Drive下载[白天雨滴数据](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lHOumI4wDsJfgGnDPOCXM6h_C1F5ZQEA/view)和[夜间雨滴数据](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1_ruwsBCzbOEkpqcqHIeKoyCg6P2Sxclr/view)。参赛者可以使用公开可用的外部数据集进行训练，但必须在信息表中明确说明数据来源。更多关于训练数据的详细信息可在FoundIR中找到。
  - [验证数据](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1t2W0OC8qXZ461EIxNNFHwzVtyCgasQs2/view?usp=drive_link)（仅输入图像）已发布
  - 测试数据（仅输入图像）：待定

## 最终测试阶段提交指南（信息表可在[待定]找到）

最终提交应通过电子邮件发送给组织者（详见"组织者"选项卡中的联系方式）。

邮件主题应为：[LoViF2026] - [任务名称] - [团队名称]

邮件正文应包括以下内容：

1. 挑战赛全名
2. 团队名称
3. 团队负责人及全部成员的名字、邮箱地址和所属机构
4. 与LoViF 2026赞助商关联的团队成员
5. LoViF 2026 Codabench竞赛平台上的团队名称和用户名
6. 可执行文件/源代码附件或下载链接
7. 信息表附件（应使用竞赛的最新信息表！）
8. 全部测试帧的完整结果下载链接（对应在线Codabench测试服务器上的最后一次提交及提供的代码/可执行文件）

可执行文件/源代码应包含训练好的模型或必要的参数以确保可重现性。README文件说明如何运行代码是必须的。信息表必须是编译的PDF文件及其LaTeX源文件。

请注意，对于未发布验证真实标签图像的竞赛，您仍可向验证服务器提交。

# 最终测试阶段——初步结果[待定]

评估完成后，初步结果将在此处发布。

请注意，排名靠前的参赛者将被邀请作为联合作者参与挑战赛报告的撰写，并向LoViF 2026研讨会投稿论文。所有挑战赛论文将接受

pages.title: evaluation
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

LoViF 2026 The Third Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images @ ECCV 2026

 

Evaluation

The evaluation consists of the comparison of the restored images with the reference ground truth images. Please inference all images with the same model (do not distinguish day and night).

The evaluation consists of the comparison of PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS.

For the final results, we will compute it by weighting the above metric as the initial results: final_score = PSNR (Y) + 10*SSIM (Y) - 5*LPIPS.

For submitting the results, you need to follow these steps:

  1. process the input images and keep the same name for the output image results as produced by your method (example: for an input file with the name "00063.png" the output file should be "00063.png") Note that the output images should have the size of the input images.  The submission example can be found in "https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LTGEuGxU0bXOv--pLcj0VJj-S2JoM2PK/view?usp=sharing". 
  2. create a ZIP archive containing the files and a readme.txt  Note that the archive should not include folders, all the files should be in the root of the archive.
  3. the readme.txt file should contain the following lines filled in with the runtime per image (in seconds) of the solution, 1 or 0 accordingly if employs CPU or GPU at runtime, and 1 or 0 if employs extra data for training the models or not.
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43 
    CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
    Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
    Other description: Solution based on A+ of Timofte et al. ACCV 2014. We have a Matlab/C++ implementation, and report single core CPU runtime. The method was trained on Train 91 of Yang et al. and BSDS 200 of the Berkeley segmentation dataset. 
      The last part of the file can have any description you want about the code producing the provided results (dependencies, link, scripts, etc.)
    The provided information is very important both during the validation period when different teams can compare their results / solutions but also for establishing the final ranking of the teams and their methods.
pages.content_zh:
第2届低级视觉前沿研讨会（LoViF）@ ECCV 2026

 

LoViF 2026 第三届日夜雨滴去除双焦点图像挑战赛 @ ECCV 2026

 

评估

评估包括将恢复的图像与参考真值图像进行比较。请使用同一模型推理所有图像（不区分日间和夜间）。

评估包括PSNR、SSIM和LPIPS的比较。

对于最终结果，我们将通过对上述指标进行加权计算得到初始结果：final_score = PSNR (Y) + 10*SSIM (Y) - 5*LPIPS。

提交结果时，需要按照以下步骤进行：

  1. 处理输入图像，输出图像结果保持与输入文件相同的名称（例如：输入文件名为"00063.png"，输出文件应为"00063.png"）。注意输出图像的尺寸应与输入图像相同。提交示例可在"https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LTGEuGxU0bXOv--pLcj0VJj-S2JoM2PK/view?usp=sharing"中找到。
  2. 创建包含文件和readme.txt的ZIP压缩包。注意压缩包中不应包含文件夹，所有文件应位于压缩包的根目录。
  3. readme.txt文件应包含以下信息行，并按要求填写解决方案的每张图像运行时间（秒）、运行时是否使用CPU或GPU（1或0）以及是否使用额外数据训练模型（1或0）。
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43 
    CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
    Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
    Other description: Solution based on A+ of Timofte et al. ACCV 2014. We have a Matlab/C++ implementation, and report single core CPU runtime. The method was trained on Train 91 of Yang et al. and BSDS 200 of the Berkeley segmentation dataset. 
      文件的最后部分可以包含任何关于代码的描述，用于生成所提供的结果（依赖项、链接、脚本等）。
    所提供的信息对于验证期间不同团队比较其结果/解决方案以及建立团队和方法的最终排名都非常重要。
